Great product however they will explain away any concerns that you have.

title: Disappointed
review_text: The house is beautifull from the display but once you settle in things begin to appear out of place. I give an overall 3-star experince because the sellers witheld critical information or bully me after I signed the deal and found issues that needed correction and overall poor concept. For example, the guy that handed over the keys did not explain the working of anything in the smart home, shifting the responsibility to white glove that also did not deliver as promised. Same guy became hostile when I pointed out bulbs hanging from the ceiling in bathroom and closet saying the house was soldas is!!!! I have since realized the sprinkler system was broken, the dish washer storing water from the sink, several (about 10) bulbs sparking and blinking when turned on, 6 dead bulbs, security sensors on windows prevent fully opening curtains making it a shady job. The house is drowning in water from broken sprinkers (and maybe pipes as well) because moisture level about one foot high has appeared on the backwall of the house and fence, and the ground is extremely wet that water can be squeezed out of it. It looks like a known problem that was concealed and sprinklers turned on only during the short buying process. This particular problem should have been disclosed before signing as the soil has gradually become saturted with water. Empire's white glove technician stole all equipment and did not install Ring citing "no equipment on site" and has not returned it after my realtor ask them to bring it back. The technician also could not explain the working of the sound system and sprinkles. The design of the house is flashy but poorly conceived. Example placement of the dinning area is a complete failure, facing the toilet! The house does not receive enough natural light to not use lighting in the living area, kitchen and dinning area during the day due to shading of windows by a poorly spaced 2 storey neighboring building. Make sure to take a tour with lights on and out in order to assess the lighting situation of certain areas of the house. As a new home owner I feel taken advantage of and a bridge of trust and transparency as some of the problems only became apparent after signing a house that they say "was sold as is".

title: Overall we are very happy with our home!
review_text: We have an incredible home. When building a home you're always going to run into small issues. The sales team committed to a few things that didn't end up happening. And the initial site plan was WAY off and without pouring $50k into our backyard it is completely useless. Our house should have been a garden level or walk out. Instead we got a huge retaining wall and two very dangerous "to code" drop offs from our back patio. Also, the survey listed this community as Parker... Our address is Lone Tree and the developer messed our address up on multiple occasions which creates a laundry list ofissues. Overall we are very happy with the product, just disappointed in a couple of small items with the Shea office team. The field team was amazing.

title: We love friendliness of the people that live here and the social gatherings.
review_text: It is taking a long time to do the final paving of the roads. The flooring scratches very easily and the paint used inside is very cheap but for the most part the home is nice.

title: Our home
review_text: This is more for the false advertising of our home. We bought our home because I loved the outdoor paint and all the detail. Well our house looks nothing like the picture of what it was sold to us as . Very disappointing. When asked they said oh ya those pictures are wrong . Well, that should have been told to us when we bought the house !!!!

title: some accessories used is old fashioned, new gadgets should come by default
review_text: some accessories used is old fashioned, Dimmers, Dimmable bulbs, Softclose toilet seats.

title: Jeff&Jill
review_text: Outstanding on site realtor Team led by Tammy. Numerous repairs and fixable workmanship issues but Dan from Shea quick to resolve with supply chain issues out if his control. GE Appliance package picked by design team has been the worst experienced in over 50 years, they have a local repairman that has taken our package and spent tireless hours repairing and ordering our replacement parts with no problems, GE warrinty employees are a joke and cut them out of our issues so we could have a normal time line to repair there inferior products. The Shea folks are also outstanding with one weaklink, if there on site inspector would have been engaged we would have certainly cut the repairs and screw ups by 50% or more. We love the house and the area so we are spending our first 3 months babysitting vendors to repair all the things the inspector should of had done before closing.

title: dunes in marina
review_text: very responsive and professional pre purchase experience. a little disappointing with issues once in the home.

title: Red curb
review_text: No one told us that our guest would not be able to park in front of our house. A month and a half after we moved in, they painted the curb red and made it a No Parking Zone. No one told us one side of each street is a No Parking Zone. The curb in front of the models should be painted red now, because eventually when the models are sold it will be a No Parking Zone. We had cabinet doors installed in the kitchen that were warped. It took 2 months after we closed for the warped doors to be replaced and adjusted properly.

title: Overall great experience!
review_text: Everyone we encountered has been professional. The sales reps were responsive, the designer was excellent, and the field managers were informative. There probably should be more than one person handling warranty issues and customer service after close of escrow. There seems to be disorganization and miscommunication there. The workmanship was generally as expected - middle-grade quality. Despite all the delays prior to closing, the home buying process with Shea Homes was an overall great experience!

title: Great news community
review_text: We love our new home, it looks beautiful, we moved into the house sight unseen, we didn't pick out the finishes, and we're pleasantly surprised at how nice everything is. However, there are things that should have been fixed prior to us moving in that were overlooked, like scratched cabinet door fronts, missing cabinet door pulls, cracked tile, and paint splatters, just to name a few. I'm sure these things will be corrected but it's disappointing to buy a brand new home and have issues that should have been noticed by the builder.

title: Smooth experience from start to end
review_text: Shea team has been very professional from start to end. All parties involved kept us up to date with construction progress and preparing us for next steps until closing. Post closing, we have noticed a delay in responsiveness and have to follow up. They do eventually come and fix the issues. All in all highly recommend, can do better in post sale service.

title: good communication
review_text: All involved from sales to construction to post construction did a good job.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - There needs to be more attention to details: ie.
review_text: There needs to be more attention to details: ie. nailing the molding to the bathroom cabinet and floor, putting molding on the floor next to the kitchen cabinet and refrigerator, installing scribe on the cabinets at both ends of peninsula. Replacing kitchen cabinet panel and trim next to refrigerator, securing kitchen cabinet trim atop of cabinets.

title: Loved the sales process, but was very disappointed with the design center
review_text: Be prepared to do a fair amount of tile, color, flooring shopping and design ideas on your own before meeting in the design center as the time is limited and the choices even more limited.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - This is our second Shea home.
review_text: This is our second Shea home. We love our new house and everything about it, except for the solar system which has been a nightmare since day one. We have been in our home for 10 weeks and are still battling with the solar company to get our system running properly. I have even reached out to Shea for their hope. Truly frustrating.

title: Shea Homes - Good company for first time home.
review_text: Good company for first time home.

title: The professional Friendly staff!
review_text: I was thrilled with the purchase of my home, Terry our sales rep was exceptional as was Manny our building Forman and Victoria our CS rep. Like every new home there were things that needed to be attended to and some were taken care of quickly and others we are still waiting on. Overall we love our home and the community and would highly recommend Shea and Trilogy at the Polo club .

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - I love our home and am delighted that we found this area and
review_text: I love our home and am delighted that we found this area and home builder. It’s not a custom home and so we are adding what we like. I am however very very disappointed with all the outside area soil. I have never moved into a home that the builder did not improve the soil so that it was possible to plant. The other day I saw my neighbor shovels out water in his yard with a BUCKET! I thought a water line broke . I asked him what happened and he said: this is crappy soil - water can’t be absorbed . A very expensive and time consuming fix is required. That is deceitful and not necessary. Youcould definitely do better for your clients.

title: Shea Homes - Repairs are still pending, I had to send emails again
review_text: Repairs are still pending, I had to send emails again

title: Shea home in Encantera
review_text: It would’ve been helpful for us to be able to speak with some recent home owners For options. The sales and design people are helpful but depending upon who you work with you will get different answers or explanations of options. I would never use Shea’s landscaping people again as the work is very poor and expensive. I would also get basic flooring and pay to have an outside company redo the floors for about 1/3 the cost Otherwise the home is beautiful seems well-built And the neighborhood is outstanding

title: I bought a former model. No responsiveness from Froy.
review_text: We are the leftovers. Not a priority. On our own.

title: Attention to detail
review_text: Overall I believe Shea builds a very good home. But my opinion There were too many issues incomplete from our walk thru. the exterior painting of the house, the service garage door, our kitchen counter top stained, too many wrong color paint on patch ups, cabinets with scratches marks that are very visible, crack in bathroom sink. I expected more of the walk thru items completed.

title: Haven’t been happy with getting issues resolved
review_text: We have lived in the home since November and still have issues not addressed. Shea isn’t responsive to issues. Home buying experience was good and our sales person Patty was fantastic.

title: Returning Hone Buyer
review_text: I am a returning homebuyer, I bought a Shea Hime property in 2004 and had a wonderful experience. This time I did have a great experience with the sales team however the process of inspection and addressing construction issues was not compared to my first experience and it definitely needs improvement.

title: K
review_text: Quality of tiles and LVP not good, I have to wait more time to get things fixed by Shea warranty

title: Bad Construction Quality
review_text: Construction quality is so bad, issues not being addressed after home delivery. Everything so visible to the eyes but Sharath homes say they are within threshold.

title: The sales people are awesome!
review_text: The sales people Anne Marie and Jim were the best! Helpful, caring, and communicative throughout the process!

title: Appearance
review_text: The bill took a really long time. Some areas of workmanship are real poor. Response to issues is poor.

title: Shea Homes - Our sales person has been great, even throughout the pandemic/hurricanes/snow
review_text: Our sales person has been great, even throughout the pandemic/hurricanes/snow storms, she has stayed in touch with us. Throughout the build on the other hand there were several issues that were addressed but had they been done when first notified issues would have gone smoother. There is still items that have not been resolved since the walk through. Electrical items and lighting that was selected from the beginning, were forgotten and had to be installed later with more cut outs done to drywall. Other issues like; •Sprinklers system had a leak. •Double garage door is having issues. •Doors tobackyard, handles are coming off. •Have found several areas where grout is missing in tiles. •The seal around main bathroom tub has cracks all around.

title: Overall we are happy with our new home but...
review_text: The subcontractor PSG Landscaping is ruining your reputation as a stellar builder. Between the combination of our warranty manager and PSG, our experience is: • Both our front and back lawns are completely riddled with lumps under the grass where you cannot walk 5 feet without turning an ankle. • We have a drainage problem in both yards but worse in the back to the point there have been washouts I could crawl through. And every time it rains it gets worse. It rains a lot in the Houston area. • The work is entirely substandard, and PSG is unwilling to do anything about it and the warrantyfolks don’t seem to have the governance over them to fix things right or in a timely way. Below are some of our experiences in dealing with this situation: • PSG has threatened to cancel our warranty. • Here we are in month two and nothing has been remedied. • PSG regularly misses promised dates and is unresponsive. • PSG staff are rude to your face and see no need to apologize for any of it. • PSG always does the minimum and hightails it out of here and it takes weeks to get them back. • We have to go outside with our dogs so they don’t get out through these washouts. • When PSG fixes something, invariably they break or disable something else. • PSG leaves our gates open after doing work here so our dogs end up getting out even though there are signs posted on both gates. • We have been watching all their work around the development and it’s all the same low standard everywhere. • Our warranty manager Brandon boasts of not responding to customer needs after 5 and before 8. Sometimes he completely ignores our voicemails or texts. He is completely ineffective with PSG. • The entire chain of command in warranty appears to be afflicted this way. • We end up going weeks before anything is done let alone getting a response from warranty. • When something is done, it’s more of the same substandard work. We have a pie shaped lot and a lot of yard for our dogs. I think PSG's experience is limited to very small yards because they have completely failed here.

title: Shea Homes - Too many poor workmanships to mention but one that stands out
review_text: Too many poor workmanships to mention but one that stands out the most is horrible fit and finish on painting. Just the worst.

title: Workmanship in my opinion was abysmal.
review_text: SOD was laid unevenly. Cabinets had obvious damage and was incorrectly installed. Electrical mounts are crooked. Cracks already showing up in the driveway and basement foundation. Porch concrete discolored. Baseboards were poorly attached with no pride in workmanship.

title: Great expirience
review_text: We had such a great time building our dream home with shea. We had a very responsive builder who took our wants and needs into account and made them happen. They went above and beyond with everything! Thank you to all the builders and crews that made our dream home so special

title: Amos, our rep is terrific. Very responsive. Great follow up.
review_text: We have been in the house (with solar) for two months. As of today, the solar is not turned on. Lots of discussion of why. However, no results. Just got the first PG&E bill. Please resolve.

title: Great Communication
review_text: The communication throughout the process was amazing..Everyone I dealt with was very helpful and you could tell they really cared about making sure our questions needs were meet.

title: Good experience
review_text: Shea has been great overall. Communication, value, and quality. The only problem that we’ve had was length of construction, but I would trade length for quality any day.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Great customer service!
review_text: Great customer service!! the house took a little longer then expected. Aimee, Kyle and Connie have been great to work with!!!

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - I cannot say enough good things about Trilogy team members Kristine,
review_text: I cannot say enough good things about Trilogy team members Kristine, Diana, and Micheal. These individuals are first rate professionals who consistently confirm our perception of Trilogy as a top tier 55+ community.

title: Shea Homes-Family - We had a good experience
review_text: We had a good experience

title: Shea Solstice Experience
review_text: The majority of the experience has been awesome. Many of the personnel with whom we have interacted have been outstanding. Mac, Dave, Erin, Mitch, and Suzanne. - Top notch- - Many small items create big headache. Overall great experience, but Shea drops the ball on several aspects which reflect a lack of vision and consideration for residents - 2 HOA organizations to pay every month - system is subpar and cannot handle bandwidth. Must login to the same system using two different account numbers to pay HOA dues. No auto-pay options. Never heard of this. Why 2 logins? I live in 1 neighborhood.If Shea has subcontracted out services, it is Shea's responsibility to then divide up the money and pay what is due to whoever. NOT the resident. - the fence is put on the homeowners, This creates an extremely awkward situation with new relationships with neighbors. residents with dogs have to suffer through movein and the period before a fence can be installed. We must reach out to get the email for neighbors to try and coordinate the plan and payment. My neighbor has elected to not pay me for the fence I coordinated, scheduled for install, and handled in full, This is Shea's responsibility and they have fallen WAY short by throwing this on new homeowners. Do you sell a house with a driveway. I paid >$600,000 for a new home... you can't install a fence??? ignorance! - The majority of homeowners have a truck. Driveways are not long enough for a fullsize truck to park, A mid size truck barely fits, forcing parking on the street. I paid for a decent sized yard and Shea leaves everyone's setback the same. If I purchased a larger yard, my front AND back yard should be larger. I didn't purchase a large yard so the front of my house sits directly on the sidewalk. Balance out the front/back yards for those who paid for more space. Maybe we wanted a longer driveway so we could park in our own property. This is an EXTREMELY ignorant design for an experienced community builder like Shea, who restricts street parking. I'm sure complaints will start before long about vehicles parked on the street, but Shea has caused the issue and there is no solution. Mini driveways do not facilitate vehicle ownership in the USA in 2022. This might make sense in Europe where they restrict vehicle size, but here it provides benefit for Shea to sell more properties and make more money while creating headaches for their unsuspecting new residents. - concrete is problematic. basement, garage, and driveway are already cracked in the field and in the joints after less than 12 months in service. Shea warranty on concrete is insufficient. Concrete will continue to crack and grow larger until after Shea warranty expires, becoming the problem of the homeowner after Shea walks away. Poor quality and workmanship in concrete - many of the screens were bent when forced into the frame, and others were left partially installed because they could not be bent enough and the installer walked away

title: Quality
review_text: This was our first home purchase that was a new build and everything about our new home is just fantastic. Great quality by SHEA.

title: The follow through and responsiveness of the representative team.
review_text: This is a beautiful, quality-built home. Nicely designed. The design center was a fun experience. The team of community representatives at Trilogy helped us get settled in comfortably, and will be a great resource going forward.

title: Quality and workmanship
review_text: Great experience...nice people to work with...good team work. A few explanations could have been explained a little better, not living in this environment before, but all in all, very satisfied and pleased.

title: Everyone’s helpfulness.
review_text: We have been very pleased with the Trilogy team.

title: Responsiveness
review_text: Shea was great throughout the entire building process. When there was an issue they were very responsive to resolve our concerns.

title: We love customer service but quality has gone down
review_text: It’s so hard to say this but the walls are much thinner and the quality of the products used is less than average. It is not the Grove quality. We really miss having a convenient light switch in the kitchen and that is just a basic item on a builders to do list. We can hear neighbor talking and can feel cold air coming through the walls.

title: Responsive to issues and questions
review_text: Good experience so far, I’ve only been at the home 10 days in the last 21/2 months. Minor improvements would be great I.e. soft close hinges on all cabinet doors, soft closing toilet seats, options for ceiling fans in the living and dining rooms. Little details make a big difference.

title: Great People!
review_text: Donna in sales and Robert in the construction office were equally amazing people to work with.

title: Excellent Response From The Builder For Questions Or Issues.
review_text: Terrible service from the realtors and quite unprofessional.

title: Good experience
review_text: Great experience but escrow company is not good

title: Bought a spec. home
review_text: Great quality and response from Shea

title: Evia model home
review_text: The Evia model home is well made and has a great floor plan with 2 beds and 2 baths. The one big complaint is the position of the heat pump right out the primary bedroom window and the noise for me is too loud.

title: Octavio has been outstanding! Attentive and responsive
review_text: The sales team was good. Pricing above market with initial homes seems like initial home buyers should be made whole with some upgrades. Or rebates . Lack of upgrades relative to model homes is unusual. We’ve bought two prior new homes and the builders were happy to offer us upgrades.. thesewe are profane of happy with the technology and remote app abilities of the home.

title: communication with staff has been exceptional
review_text: Pre-occupancy walk-throughs are very nice.

title: Pretty Good!
review_text: House was built pretty well. Only one issue. It takes a minute and a half to get hot water at the kitchen sink because they did not install a circ pump. Seems crazy to waste 1.5 gallons of water in Las Vegas every time we need hot water. Its on us to fix it they did nothing. For $820,000 you would expect a little better.

title: Right house, right time, right place
review_text: We had been looking for a smaller home after retiring from work, but had trouble finding the right combination of home and neighborhood in the town where we lived. This Shea home came on the market shortly after we renewed our search, and we are very pleased with it. It had been already finished, so I cannot speak to the experience of working with Shea from the ground up. However, Shea has been responsive to minor warrantee claims, in which they proved to be responsive and effective. We love the floor plan and general quality of the build.

title: Good not great
review_text: Good all around. Would recommend.

title: Overall, Shea was great to work with and they build a quality home
review_text: This was our second build in Encanterra with Shea. Everyone we dealt with was friendly and professional. Susan at the design center was very helpful and got us through the process of selecting our finishes quickly. We did spend quite a bit of time before our meeting with her, selecting a lot ahead so our time with her was more efficient. Antonio and Mike, our site managers, were always eager to meet and address any concerns early on.

title: You have good people working for you
review_text: Built long distance - having a good project manager was key.

title: Alex, Josh and Karen were all knowledgeable, helpful and responsive to us throughout the home buying and move in process
review_text: The quality and craftsmanship of the home are outstanding. The followi-up warranty support have been excellent.

title: Fox Point Farms in Encinitas, CA
review_text: We had a great experience working with Shea Homes on our purchase of a home in Fox Point Farms! Spencer was always helpful and professional on the sales side, Candace was so helpful in selecting design options, and Victor has been great as our warranty representative. Our thanks to them and the rest of the team at Shea ...

title: Shea Home Purchase
review_text: We had a great experience with Matt in sales. He is friendly and does a great job with keeping you informed. Overall we had a good home buying experience.

title: Sunnova Solar
review_text: Sunnova is undergoing bankruptcy procedures and the homeowners are not being kept up to date on what is happening. We are new and have no idea when our solar system will be connected. More transparency is needed.

title: Good experience
review_text: Overall experience was good and we are happy either way our home and especially the lot .

title: No Secrets
review_text: Everything was transparent. Models were so helpful to visualize the right floor plan choice for us. There were upgrades in the models that were not available however at the design center. Just be aware. Upon moving in there were some issues (as expected) with a new home but they were addressed quicky by Sanel our warranty specialist.

title: Many quality finishes are not offered.
review_text: Many finishes like LVT AND FIREPLACES ARE NOT OFFERED. No gas fireplaces no gas ranges. You are forced to buy what they offer and then tear it out and put in what you want. The design center is very weak’

title: Trilogy Sunstone
review_text: Sunstone is a very nice community with lots of amenities offered. It’s a great way to meet folks and stay active, as much as or as little that you care to. The homes have very nice floor plans to choose from and the contractors move right along to keep your build on schedule. Their project website is very informative.

title: Relatively low stressful purchase with very responsive team
review_text: This was our first home purchase and we were pleasantly surprised to experience very little stress as it relates to ensuring the place was free from issues and we closed relatively quickly as well. Since moving in Shea and the warranty lead has been very quick to resolve any issue that arises and genuinely wanting us to be very happy with the purchase. I would recommend buying a shea home to anyone.

title: Great staff
review_text: All the people we’ve interacted with: Debbie, Aiden. Rick and others have been most helpful and great to work with. The design and infrastructure of the development is nicely done along with the attention to details and follow-up.

title: They stayed on schedule but communication was poor
review_text: But communication was very poor. They communicate mostly by email. Many times they are sending you documents to sign without anyone explaining to you what this new document is and if/or why you need to sign it. The local team was mostly accessible to answer questions or meet with me to go over things at my request. Particular thanks to Patti at the main office who was a gem, and helped me and my realtor tremendously.

title: Kept us informed through the whole process.
review_text: We appreciated the responsiveness when there was a problem or we had a question. I appreciated the ability to change one of the extras that the first couple had chosen.

title: A great new home
review_text: Good quality and great customer support

title: Good overall
review_text: Bit more custom choices would be nice

title: Excellent overall representation & quality
review_text: Shea representatives from Sales to Building Sup’s and Support Staff, have been fantastic. Many are lifelong employees, which says A Lot, and they all seem to care about providing a great experience and buttoned-up final product. Of course, there are ALWAYS issues at the end of a build and after closing, and you can always expect minor fixes & finishes to be needed. Most frustrations we have had are scheduling trades to fix details post-closing and a few things that could have been avoided with more care during the build (again back to trades - not Shea or Shea employees). Overall - we arevery happy, satisfied and recommend Shea!

title: Shea Homes
review_text: Initial contacts with BreAnn and Marlena in making our decision to buy, Mike at Shea Mortgage and Karen at Chicago Title were excellent and efficient. The first few months of home ownership have had some heating and electrical issues, but Shea has been responsive in addressing and resolving all issues to date. Shea offers a piece-of-mind 12 month warranty to include a 1, 5, and 11 month walkthrough to ensure everything is in order. We love our new home and appreciate the Shea Team.

title: Property owner
review_text: Sales representative was fantastic through complete process and continues to be even after closing. Construction team and warranty team have been very responsive and continue to satisfy our needs. We unfortunately missed out on quite a few options as the design representative was unfamiliar with Shea homes and the time afforded us was insufficient.

title: New Homeowner Experience
review_text: Great people to work with. Kirstie, Mike, Laura, Cooper, Sharon and all security guards very helpful and very friendly.

title: Some Great.....Some Not So Good
review_text: Almost every Shea person we talked with was a pleasure. The home buying process seemed rushed, and the Design Joy was hard to navigate. I felt like we were updated throughout the building process. After moving in, most items that needed repair were taken care of right away. The microwave does't fit correctly in the cabinet and we're still waiting for that to be fixed. My biggest complaint is the person you sent to the house to walk us through the "smart" features. The person who came out was not a pleasure to deal with at all. He was pushy and insistent that we had to sign up anduse all the features of the home. Yes, even the ones we didn't want. So today my Sonos told me that there was someone at my door. The Sonos is in Murphys CA, the door is in Hollister CA. Now, my Sonos won't work in Murphys, because Alexa in Hollister has done just what we were told it wouldn't do, Alexa has taken over. It's too bad that the last Shea person we dealt with was so unpleasant. As stated, Some Great..... Some Not So Good.

title: Brad and Gwen were very professional, knowledgeable and attentive. They explained all the design and warranty features.
review_text: Quite frankly the home buying experience was somewhat overwhelming. The fact that we were working from Cincinnati ,500 miles away, added a degree of difficulty also. There were many different people to work with for their functional needed. Then, there were the many documents that required electronic signatures. All the details with Shea Homes were in addition to the effort required to sell our house in Cincinnati. The onboarding process at Trilogy Lake Norman was also stressful. At times it felt like "trying to take a drink from a fire hose." There are some may topics that requireattention. However, the quality of the house and the support of the people/staff are very good.

title: Lauerence
review_text: Claire was extremely helpful,and a pleasure to work with.I highly recommend Shea Homes

title: Billy and Helena
review_text: They were very responsive with not on words but their actions for results were amazing.

title: Wendell
review_text: Overall experience was good.After occupancy response to issues needs improvement.

title: Measure twice cut once
review_text: We love our home, but simple mistakes should have been caught while building the home. Shea seems to be a mass cookie cutter builder. Very limited changes can be accommodated Mistakes are being corrected, but it takes time. Having Shea provide backyard landscaping was a mistake. There were no plans and we will be redoing the yard.

title: Pretty good, not perfect
review_text: Overall a good experience but definitely room for improvement. Little things like a tiny chip in our countertop that we reported right away and were told it wasn’t covered under warranty. It’s a small thing that would take a few minutes to repair and go a long way to show the homeowner they matter after closing.

title: Mostly a very good experience with Shea.
review_text: Jaime was very healthy in sales office and Brooke was outstanding seeing things that needed corrected at the one month repair period.

title: Quiet and well organized community
review_text: Great place to spend the quieter stage of life

title: Dalton
review_text: Sales was extraordinary making it possible to relocate from VA. Would have been impossible without Margie’s help. Mortgage lending was extremely supportive and innovative. Found a very creative mortgage that was ideal for us! Field mgr was awesome. Jason was a 12 on a 1-10 scale. I’ve built 6 new homes, Jason was by far the best. TLB landscaping is outstanding! Both price and quality exceeded our expectations. Highly recommend to our neighbors!! Very happy with workmanship. Attention to details immaculate. Design outstanding. My only negative feedback is timing? Butthe house and community were worth the wait. No regrets.

title: Love my Home!
review_text: I give the Homebuying experience 4 stars. The only drawback I experienced with the Shea company was that they do not go "out of the box". When you make a request, it was 100% denied. Boundaries were set, and they do not budged, and stayed within the lines. Sometimes, you need to give a little leeway...which would have made the experience a bit more personal, understanding, and not just another transaction with the seller. Other than that, I enjoyed working with your outstanding employees, particularly April, Trisha, Alicia, Frankie, Ryan, and Todd. Thank you.

title: Shea Trilogy Experience
review_text: We really liked that Shea had the different stage reviews, in the building process. Especially showing us the walls before closing with sheet rock. This helped in making decision's of where to place things on the walls. It would have been nice, if during the building process, we could have added solar and or if that had been an option. Especially here in Arizona. Otherwise, everything was great. And the superintendent Alexis was GREAT to work with.

title: Post sales process has been great
review_text: The closing process was difficult due to items not in place that Shea should have known about and senior executives did not respond to emails. The options selections process was also complicated as the team really didn't know about some options and that knowledge would have changed some of the items I selected (the Design Joy catalog was not accurate). That said, now that I'm in, I love the house. Quality is quite good and the construction foreman (Andrew) was great. The delivery team (headed by Vicki) is awesome and she is the most responsive person I have met.

title: Nellie and Carolyn Ann - best ever!
review_text: Nellie made sure that we knew all of our options and helped us choose the home model of our dreams and that which we could afford. Since we moved from the northeast, we were limited in our ability to fly down and track the progress. Nellie made herself available for us, by phone, text and in-person. Carolyn Ann gave us regular updates on the build and inspection processes. She, too, was responsive to our calls and texts, and she made herself available when we were able to fly down to see the house in different stages. We felt supported for sure, also by Larry, with regard to the mortgageprocess. He, too, was responsive to our calls and requests, even on weekends.

title: Overall satisfaction is great with some areas for improvement
review_text: The homebuying process was smooth with good customer service. The house is well-built and made from quality materials, but some design flaws leave a bit of an unpleasant aftertaste. For example, a few awkward door swings, a poorly placed light switch, and a strange kitchen layout make you wonder if the home design was tested for functionality. Some of these issues were addressed and modifications were accepted, but some other issues were simply ignored.

title: Well Managed
review_text: Throughout the buying and building experience I was kept informed of progress and given realistic deadlines. Most of my issues have been with the appliances. In general the fit and finish are very good.

title: Overall Great House
review_text: We had a great experience working with Josh on the home purchasing side of things. He was very helpful and worked with us on our relocation. The experience with him was great. When the house was turned over, Josh was very helpful with going thrugh the property and was helpful with the small things right when we moved it. Where things feel short was the details. With a house at the pricepoint we purchased at, there were just too many small things missed that we had and are having to get addressed. I feel its the details. I also felt we go the same quality if not lower in some of theitems compaired to lower price point new contruction homes we have had in the past. I expected to have some upgrades and they just were not there. Issues being addressed right when we moved in were done promptly. Our one month issues have not been addressed promptly and the workers did not show up when they were supposed to, to address the issue and it took longer than expected

title: Model/Curb Appeal
review_text: My house was selected prior to a model being built..I picked out brick for the outside with the sales office..after the house was built I was told my model couldn’t have brick on the outside..if I had been informed I would have chosen a different house…thus, my house has no curb appeal even after I paid an extra 20,000 for the lot that now has a very large apartment complex behind it.. I was told there would be trees and landscaping…as of now just weeds

title: Very happy with our new home
review_text: We are very happy with our new home. The quality of material and products used is very good. The few, very minor, issues we've come across have been quickly addressed and rectified. Our construction field manager, Brad, has been nothing short of fantastic. Right from the start, he has been accessible and responsive to all of our concerns, which was greatly appreciated since we were coming from out of state. Overall, I'd say it's been really good home-buying experience.

title: Sales and construction employees were exceptional
review_text: Not pleased with the quality of the finishes and appliances at this price point

title: Good quality and aftermarket service but poor experience with sales.
review_text: We are very pleased with the house we bought and are overall satisfied with the quality of the construction and the responsiveness of the aftermarket team. Our inspector did not find any major issues, and Peyton was quick to fix them before closing. After we moved in, some minor things have come up and Carlos has helped us fix them promptly. Our buying process was not what we had expected, mainly due to the treatment we received from the sales agent Theary. All through, we felt as if we were asking her for a favor and received a push back on every single request. Luckily the people takingcare of things are different and have a customer centric approach.

title: Owners response
review_text: I’m satisfied with the home. Didn’t care for the building experience. It was extremely stressful. I’m still dealing with issues from the build, having to turn in warranty request to get items corrected!

title: Shea homes
review_text: We`ve been very pleased with Shea

title: Return to VA
review_text: I searched the Winchester VA area online for 55+ communities, including with Google Earth at street level. The latter was very little help because Trilogy east is still under construction. The Shea website for Lake Frederick was most helpful for previews and prices of models. Dan Wall was very helpful through the contract process. Sight and Sound failed me, my agent actually forgot to order my television equipment. Their Techs showed up and had no television to install.

title: Great purchase experience: simple and quickness
review_text: The house has a good arrangement which is helpful to rent. I like it. Shushan

title: We bought a spec house and had a good experience.
review_text: We bought a spec house and had a good experience.

title: Construction supervisor Jeremi
review_text: The construction supervisor was very responsive to all issues during the building process.

title: Always a good experience so far.
review_text: the team was very helpful and informative.

title: working with crew in Del Sol
review_text: everyone has been great so far - Will responds immediately and does his best to solve problems as soon as possible - Jeanie and shannon are always available to help. great support and customer service so far!

title: Del Sol, New Ventura Community
review_text: Pros: quality of work is good. Shea builds good solid homes. I think this is developing into a really good community. They have been responsive to issues, and they are fixing. The warranty specialist is super friendly, does a good job making his rounds and getting things fixed. Value for price is good for this area and only getting more expensive. Has a community pool and developing pocket parks. The folks in the model home offices are very nice friendly and helpful, even after you move in, they are there to assist if you have any questions. Has an HOA Cons: Water heater iselectric which is fine, but it is really noisy as the built in fan has to run for an hour or more every time you take a shower or use hot water. And the water heater is situated near the bedrooms or next to an office which will run at night or morning and makes it difficult to work or you have to have your bedroom door closed in order to relax in your room or sleep. Note that you cannot have a full salt soft water system installed which is tricky to keep your appliances and faucets clear from hard water deposits. You have to maintain and or eventually replace faucets which is really unfortunate. Would be nice if they could add sport courts such as pickle ball/tennis courts etc. as there are not many things to do in the area that are walking distance. Has an HOA I would give it 5 stars if it wasn't for the water Heater and not being able to set up a full salt soft water system.

title: Fairly pleased
review_text: So the worst part of our experience was the design center. Shea only offered packages for designs. We have built numerous homes, and this was the worst experience. There were things we didn't want that came with the package. Home could have been cleaned a bit better. Painters were terrible with not using drop clothes, so LOTS and LOTS of white paint specs all over the wood floors.

title: Experience with Shea Home
review_text: Overall experience from home purchasing to move was great.

title: Just a perfect second home....
review_text: We had our house built during Covid and while were living in Illinois. We felt the communication with Mike, who was general superintendent for the project, was great. He kept us informed, answered the phone and our questions and always had a terrific attitude. When we got into finishes and punch list items, Conner was fantastic to work with. He was there almost immediately with the normal day to day issues that we asked to deal with and like Mike, always had a great attitude and a smile. This home is amazing and we couldn't be happier.

title: Lorri, Victor and Tom
review_text: We had a great experience buying our first home with Shea! From the design team working with Lorri, to working with Tom and Victor, they are always responsive and here to help! Victor stops by often if we have a question and to check on us and we really appreciate that. We feel cared for and everyone we've worked with has felt like a friend!

title: Beautiful Home. Good Construction. Be careful with Design Phase of Project
review_text: Shea builds a beautiful home with thoughtful layouts. Let me start with Kara. She was incredible to work with throughout the process. She always went above and beyond for us and helped us during the difficult emotional times of building a home. She is the gold standard for representing Shea Homes!!! Scott was out Field Manager. Like Kara, Scott was a G-d send. He was extremely communicative throughout the build. He always took our calls and responded to our texts. No ask was unimportant to Scott. If it was important to us, he looked into it and responded quickly. Scott had zeroblack holes. I never had to remind him a second time about anything throughout the 12 month build. His attention to detail was incredible. He would notice various things (ie tile not level, master bath accent tile appearance, and so many other details),that quite honestly, we may have missed, and he would proactively get them corrected until it met his high standards of workmanship. He always explained every detail of the house build so that we understood why certain aspects of the home are built the way they are (ie the attic insulation methodology). As I mentioned, no ask was too much for Scott. We had heard horror stories about other Site Directors which made us feel so incredibly fortunate to have Scott on our home. Like Kara, Scott is the Gold Standard and represents the Shea product to its highest level!!! We are also very thankful to have Steve as our Post-Close Warranty Specialist. Like Kara and Scott, Steve responds very quickly to anything we have and no ask is too big for Steve to handle. We look forward to working with Steve throughout our first year. Design was a total mess!!! The selection offerings were not up to par with the cost of the home and the designer we were assigned to had no idea of what options were truly available and made minimal suggestions. She also struggled immensely with using the design system and selection tools. She made numerous mistakes, that we had to fix after the close, that cost us over $10,000! She even got downright rude at times. The entire design process is extremely rushed and high pressure. They need to completely revamp their Design portion of the build. As a side note, I rated responsiveness to issues a 3 because of the design process not the warranty service. I would actually rate the Design responsiveness a 1 and the Sales, Construction and Warranty responsiveness a 5. Shea Homes did not seem to care, or be willing to assume any responsibility for what happened during design. Our designer was fired, but that does not help us in any way.

title: Beautiful area with expensive, nicely designed homes
review_text: Beautiful area of the state with expensive, nicely designed homes. Sizes and color schemes are great. Some things in the home could have been done better. The HOA is strict and not quick to respond. Looking forward to the clubhouse being built. Glad we have a Lifestyle Manager already in place.

title: My Shea New Home Review
review_text: This was my first new construction purchase. It was a mix of experiences. My initial sales person disappeared. I learned she was gone via email and never felt that I had a single person that I could direct questions to after that. The most responsive person I dealt with was my Field Manager.

title: Melba
review_text: Very friendly and helpful people to work with

title: Overall Good Experience
review_text: Turnover of staff / reduction of staff and lack of communications is reason for 4 stars. Build process takes too long.

title: Good overall experience
review_text: Overall was good experience. Sales team was responsive and helpful. Wish they had more options on some things like insulation between floors to reduce noise, option for high end appliances like Wolf, laminate on stairs to garage, frosted windows, and more. Had few minor issues when moving in, but most were addressed quickly. One thing was a pain and that was an issue with the front door lock. Shea said I had to work with vendor to get a replacement lock which was time consuming. I would have expected them to resolve it with the vendor.

title: Community Rep
review_text: She was extremely helpful, always very friendly and got things done quickly.

title: SATISFIED WITH OUR HOME SO FAR!
review_text: Everyone involved has been great to work with.

title: Quality home
review_text: Enjoyed working with the Shea team. It wasn't perfect but they all worked together to provide a great new home for us .

title: Trilogy in Summerlin Buying Experience
review_text: Overall, positive buying experience. Very responsive to issues.

title: The people at Shea were professional and courteous.
review_text: The Shea reps at the Enclave sales center were professional and helpful (Tamara and TJ). The experience at the Livermore design center was outstanding. Kristin was knowledgeable, professional and patient. The construction manager for my home, Dave, was courteous, professional and very accommodating. The Shea rep who did the walk through, Octavio, was outstanding and very responsive to my questions and concerns. I appreciate his help in everything he did, including the 30 day walk through. My primary complaint is that Shea homes is not very flexible in the options for their homes (for example,the use of hardwood on the stairs is not allowed), or making simple custom modifications (for example, widening a bedroom doorway for wheelchair access).

title: Trilogy Rio Verde AZ
review_text: Special call out to the sales team, Liz and Laura- and our project manager Brian and warranty coordinator Lala off to a good start. Overall build is good, its definitely not custom, but we new that going into the build. The day we took possession, the painter a few hours before we arrived, dripped white paint all over the floor- and left the doors open in 119 degree heat, creating a massive amount of insects inside the home that took a month to get rid of them- that was definitely frustrating . Problem with original design center team, but Shea Homes stepped up and did the right thingeventually, but at the time, that was very disappointing that we had to go through that experience, what should have been an awesome time at the design center, we left feeling insulted and really frustrated. The community is beautiful, thoughtfully designed and well maintained by the HOA

title: Helpful when issues arose in fixing things.
review_text: Should have done back yards for all homes not just the inventory homes that are not selling.

title: Overall good experience buying new home from Shea
review_text: Overall was a positive experience from initial interactions all the way to closing. Shea team did job of keeping us updated along the way and were responsive to queries and clarifications, and we had pleasant interactions with entire team we were engaged with. Our closing walk through found mostly minor issues so that came as a good surprise. The workmanship while not perfect (we don't expect any home builder to be) certainly met our expectations for the most parts. Would recommend based on our experience so far.

title: Great construction manage - very well managed Jeremi T
review_text: Great construction manage - very well managed

title: Homeowner
review_text: We have had the pleasure of working with some very fine people that have listened to our issues and helped to resolve them.

title: Good experience
review_text: We closed and moved into our new home in April and have been pleased with the warranty follow up, general quality of the workmanship and the responsiveness of the entire sales and service team at Shea homes in Wickenburg.

title: Karen was great! Derek not so good
review_text: We loved working with Karen. We were hoping to finish the process with her. Derek filled in for her whenever Karen wasn’t available. This isn’t my first purchase, abd I have dealt with other builders and staff. Unfortunately didn’t have a great experience with Derek. Especially after closing, he was very cold, and wasn’t willing to help.

title: Good house. Would like more flexibility on options
review_text: I think overall the house is pretty well-made. The walls are a little thin, however, and I would’ve liked more flexibility within my option package.

title: Experience
review_text: I have not yet moved in so I can’t give a thorough review. I found Katie and Kirstie in Sales to be attentive and helpful. I found Mike and Rome equally attentive to resolving issues on punch list. After project completion, I feel value for price paid was insufficient. Processes to deal with upgrade installation have been problematic.

title: Pretty good
review_text: Closed on our home 2 months ago, was about 1 full year since groundbreaking started. Process was generally smooth, issues with sub-contractors, design team, timing. We had an issue with missing carpet from the design center, was known since pre-drywall and was not resolved till 3-weeks before closing. Communication between sales/construction/design seems non-existent. Any issues we've had, have been addressed. We had an independent inspection done, sloppy work with drywall and stucco. Asked to repair and they will say that's the way it's done. I would say Shea is above average. Have heardmany worse stories from other builders.

title: Lots of technology
review_text: Great care and responsiveness to questions

title: Trilogy @ Rio Verde
review_text: Our overall experience with Shea was very positive! Matt our sales person is a tremendous asset to Shea! We had a wonderful experience with our field supervisor, Jason. AND would build an home with Jason again, should the opportunity present itself!! Our warranty specialist, Lala has been tremendous to work with also. The biggest issues we had was during our design center consultation and issues that arose from that process. Outlets were added when they were not needed for low voltage, tile flooring was not ordered to finish the covered patio space to match the home, bathroom faucets choicewas not made originally and our back patio was lowered prior to build to allow for thicker stone when we already had decided to use porcelain tile with the landscaper. I will say that the process at the time was pretty smooth…just did not understand what it meant when our designer was new to Verde River, but not to Shea. Turnover happens and most of our issues have a positive side to them as well. Overall, A+ and we want to thank everyone to make this long distance build possible without many headaches at all!!

title: Review of Trilogy sunstone home
review_text: Too early to tell on a lot of this.

title: Home buying experience
review_text: Mike was great to work with and very responsive thru out the entire Sales process. .Debbie in the Design Center was also the same and our only complaint was that the price of upgrades seemed very high and their lack of flexibility in making simple changes ie deleting a medicine cabinet before the house was even framed. The superintendent, Ryan was fantastic and extremely proud of the work that he and the crew did! Lastly Richard in customer service could not be nicer and extremely responsive and professional. A very good team!!

title: We are pleased with our Shea home
review_text: Overall good communications and progress reports. Overall good quality of materials and construction.

title: Very friendly staff
review_text: Compared to other new builds. The quality for price is outstanding. We haven't encountered many issues with the house, and the few we have were corrected quickly by Shea's warranty department.

title: My wife and I bought this home from Shea and we are very happy with our choice
review_text: Shea built a quality home with added great features. Their sales staff is very responsive and their workmanship is high quality.

title: Great process just a little slow.
review_text: Lots of help from Donnie, Andrew, and Adam. Great to work with. It took a lot longer than we thought, but now that we have moved in we are very happy with the house and the community.

title: Overall good experience and follow up
review_text: All Shea employees are great to work with

title: Positive experience
review_text: We were out of state when we bought our house so we had some apprehension about the whole process. But Jane was there to help guide us through it. She worked closely with our realtor throughout the process. As the build phase wound down, we started working more with Mike the build manager. He was very responsive and understanding throughout the process. We don't think the house would've come out as nice without his leadership. We did have several known problems going into closing that were a pain to get resolved since we had to let people into the house we now owned. We had to deal withthe trades. I know there's a lot to juggle but these things should've been handled earlier. All in all the process went great and we'd do it again.

title: Great Home
review_text: The wait was worth it. Shea Home actually uses good building materials for their construction. We've had 2 neighbors from other builders compare their building structures to what we have and they both confirmed the quality.

title: Wonderful Community
review_text: All service personnel have been very responsive. It is a pleasant experience dealing with Shea.

title: Good, but Opportunities Missed to be Great
review_text: Shea has a reputation for quality and, for most items, the quality is obvious. For other times, Shea falls short. For example, one towel bar in each bath and no hand towel ring. This is a half million+ dollar home. Framed mirrors versus a single sheet - another example. It's the end finishes that homeowners "see" that should be better thought through. Suggestion: Spend about $1000 more to include finishes that will make the home sparkle. Communication is good from all departments - realtor, crew, and warranty. It's a solid home. Struggled with an ADA issue that should have beenhandled better. It was not honored. I do recommend Shea Homes, but with the understanding that some finishes and ADA needs be taken seriously.

title: Good product, needs some fine-tuning to be great.
review_text: Purchase experience was generally smooth. Lots of positive interaction with Sonja throughout the process. Larry, on the finance end was good, too. Brad, the GC has been fantastic, Caroline as our liaison, too. Some head-scratchers regarding appliances not working properly from day-one. The "cleaning" and "punchlist" crews seemed to make more messes than they corrected between walk through & closing. Lots of painting splatter needing work

title: Good experience
review_text: Nice quality home. Closing was rushed, before they could finish there things. Disappointed in the quality of carpet. Overall good service and beautiful new home.

title: Awesome experience except...
review_text: It has been a great experience purchasing my Shea home. Everyone we've met from the sales office to the service team is very professional and helpful, except for Michele in the Design office (I believe she is a VP of some sort), who told me to rethink if this is the right home for me when I requested some minor design changes during the design phase.

title: Shea Homes experience
review_text: The effort made by Shea Homes has been exceptional! The Field Manager (Michael ) kept us abreast of progress on a weekly basis once ground was broken and the Warranty Manager ( Laura )was very affective at addressing any issues that surfaced post delivery. Once your in your home you realize your neighbors are all very friendly & happy! You can't go wrong with Shea Homes at Trilogy!

title: This field is listed as optional.
review_text: Overall my experience was very good. The teams were responsive during the build process. After move-in, I sometimes feel that my warranty rep and I are not communicating the best, but I still have the 5 and 11 month visits to make sure everything gets worked out.

title: Overall great experience
review_text: No major issue with the home buying process,

title: floor plan is so good
review_text: floor plan is so good

title: Rushed completion and no changes allowed
review_text: I appreciated the professionalism of the staff throughout our home buying process. I felt comfortable discussing issues that arise and confident that they would be resolved. I did not like that we had to had to do our walkthrough and close on our purchase before everything was completed. There are many issues we found after we’ve moved in that we now have to address through the warranty department. It’s frustrating having to book appointments and take time off work to handle these issues. Additionally, there were minor changes we wished Shea had offered like adding or moving outlets orputting in a regular bathroom door that are difficult and expensive to make after construction. Considering how much this house cost, this makes the value for the price paid significantly lower.

title: asdfasdfasd
review_text: Shea was very responsive at every step of the way. We are very happy with our home. In full transparency, there were a few workmanship issues with the house (expected for houses being built at this scale), but Shea's customer service and warranty quickly addressed any issues and continue to follow up proactively.

title: Me, myself and I
review_text: Two of the big benefits of this home is that it is a smart home with every appliance connected and controlled via the internet and gas solar electricity with an EV plug for charging. It is easy to open the front door remotely, turn on lights, view security cameras, cycle the water heater for instant hot water, turn on the heat upstairs and downstairs and gave packages delivered from Amazon right into the garage via automated garage door access.

title: I thought this was optional
review_text: Everything was fine, no issues

title: New Home in the Desert
review_text: We love our new home, but defenitley were surprised at the items we needed to do when moving in. When designing your home, be aware of upgrades that don't include basic items like towel bars in the bathroom, tile & granite not sealed by builder. We expect some items needing attention, but did not expect to have to do projects the week we arrived.

title: Our experience with Shea Lake Frederick
review_text: Our experience with Frank in sales was very good. We had a bump in the road and Frank quickly found a solution. We had a change in project managers and both were very nice. Seth ended up being our PM and we were very happy with him. He kept us informed, sent pictures of progress and the house was always clean when we came to see it. The only thing I can see room for improvement is using teams. I had never used it and could have used some guidance. Also, it seems like there is a disconnect with the app and online version. Things weren’t always answered in a timely fashion because of this.

title: Good quality building
review_text: Home buying experience is smooth , quality is good .houses are expensive for the space and size

title: hitech homes
review_text: Great value for money homes

title: Nothing
review_text: We are very satisfied with Shea Home through wonderful customer services

title: Building supervisor Jason
review_text: Jason when out of his way to answer questions, guide us on issues we were having and making us feel comfortable about decisions. The area that needs work is the design center. Our designer did not offer alternatives or give suggestions or give the pros and cons of various materials. When it came to designing the lighting and placement of outlets etc., she was no help at all. There are many things I would have done differently if I had known there was a choice.

title: Worth it in the end
review_text: While I can’t compare to another home I’ve built because this was our first new build we are happy with the home Shea built us. The process itself was a little more difficult because we bought during Covid. We had to make design center selections in one day so it was rushed. The other thing that sucked was b/c Covid slowed things down our models weren’t open when we purchased so we didn’t get to see some design center options they ended up installing in the models which probably would have changed our choices on some things. We’re happy with our purchase but I don’t know if I’d build a newhome again as it’s a daunting process end to end. Parts were fun and obviously new is nice but it’s not for the inpatient.

title: Painless downsizing
review_text: I was out of my element, having never bought a home by myself. It was an orderly transition, with help available when needed.

title: New home woes and support
review_text: First, I have bought 5 new homes, I will say that we love this home in general, but our beginning has been rough. Deanna was a great person to work with on the front end. House wasn't delivered clean, some things out of Shea control... type of insulation, garage door opener downgrade, dented fridge, no washer/dryer (have loaners). Busted kitchen cabinet, broken tiles all through kidtcen, also in master shower. Flooring issues, window blind too small, many little touch things that should have been done prior to delivery. Then to top it off, broken cable line, so we had zero internet/tvfor first two weeks. The good news is that our long punch list is being worked on now, Adam has been an amazing help and overall the staff is very supportive. Biggest disappointment would have to be the tile issues, but other than that, it will eventually be a wonderful home!

title: Good People, Rough Start
review_text: Being at the beginning of a new community build has alot of kinks to work out. Fortunately, BreAnn and my Project Manager Will, along with my Warrantee person, Gus have been easy to work with and responsive. Along with many supply shortages, there have been significant shortages in Skilled labor which has caused delay and less than desirable end results with certain trades. Will and Breann and GUS (he's a GEM) have worked hard to remedy various issues.

title: Staff are very helpful
review_text: 90% of everything in our new home went well. The tile and closets subs should have done a better job.

title: Warranty Team
review_text: The whole team has been very responsive to any issues we have had with our new home. Even small issues that arise they treat them as a big deal and are quick to respond.

title: Very nice home and great community.
review_text: Very beautiful home and community. Construction in general is high quality, and design of the home is well laid out, Energy efficient systems and automatic /wireless options are working well and convenient. Shea did well at keeping me apprised of construction progress and held to the promised dates for the construction steps. i rated responsiveness as a three as the warranty service processes seems to have some gaps that allow for concerns to fall through. One gap is that a service request disappeared after being acknowledged. the second is that i twice received letters saying that theservice had been completed when it had not. I rated quality of workmanship as a four as i have found two problems with the electrical wiring. One was a dryer plugin wired incorrectly, the second was a ground wire left loosely connected that caused loss of power to the living room.

title: Happy homebuyer
review_text: We are happy to do bussiness with Sbea

title: Overall a good experience with Shea.
review_text: Shea has been very responsive to issues. The quality of the home is very good.

title: Good experience- few things to be great.
review_text: The positives. Shea builds a good home, and their staff is friendly and largely helpful. Issues encountered along the way in construction and close inspection were addressed timely. My issues have mostly been around post close items. The landscaper (TLB) didn’t follow up per 30 day. It took repeated outreaches to get them scheduled to resolve issues, and they’re still not fully addressed, and communication is very poor. Overall, I’d recommend others to Shea, but Shea should utilize their leverage with TLB to ensure the full experience is positive.

title: Dave and Frankie have been real assets to this process. I had a problem with the garage door opening on it’s own that posed security concerns. The vendor had to come twice before they replaced it totally.
review_text: The process was fairly well orchestrated throughout the months it took to complete. There were problems encountered outside the builder’s control that could have been better communicated but the overall experience was a good one with very positive results.

title: My experience.
review_text: My superintendent, Ryan, that worked with me during the project was triple A. He was all over everything and should be commended. 3 disappointments. 1) Quality of actual paint should be upgraded. Pulls off with blue tape, feels cheap. 2)This is our 2nd Shea House in 2 years. We built in the Canyons in Colorado. Better trades in Colorado than AZ. Cleaner workmanship. Oh well. Our 2nd bath, bath tubs, weren't covered during construction with cardboard and got scratched in Colorado. We warned Trilogy VR super of same possibility and got deaf ears. Scratches again, difficult to fix.Unnecessarily annoying. This was our 10th new build and have experienced this before. Seems like a simple fix to me. 3) We used your pool guy CalPools. Pool, hot tub, fire pit, outdoor grill. Nobody recommended to upgrade propane tank size. Our first pool. I learned our 120 gallon tank is filled to only 95 gallons. Pool heater burns 6 gallons per hour according to AZ Gas . The math doesn’t work well. We ran out of gas after turning on heater for pool for what seemed like 6-8 hours. Big surprise. Somebody from Shea Sales, or initial Shea construction meeting, or Shea landscape meeting, should have pointed this out to us. Now I have to tear up the landscaped front yard and deal with all sorts of companies to get this approved and remodeled. Now I am at the mercy of their pricing and owning an extra 120 gallon propane tank. Frankly, it is borderline unexcusable considering how many pools Shea has been involved with. Overall very pleased with house and Lala is doing her best to take care of warranty issues. The Shea landscaper did above average job, but has been very cooperative and accommodating since we moved in. Love the appreciation. Do the right thing and share the cost with the Pickleball Club and build some desperately needed extra courts. Embrace the opportunity to get some very good press about building more courts. Don't risk the potential for bad press if you don't help the club with more courts. It could get ugly for you. Turn this opportunity to a positive so you can crow about how Shea has recognized how big this sport has become for their Trilogy residents. Don't let the opposite happen. The sport has become huge for this demographic. We already have club state champions!

title: Shea Grove in Camarillo
review_text: The ladies in the office were extremely pleasant and very helpful. They gave us and continue to give us lots of useful information.

title: Overall a very positive experience. Once warranty work is completed we will be 100% satisfied.
review_text: SalesPerson David was awesome. Did above and beyond what was in his control. Represented Shea very well. Love the home, specifically the layout.

title: I purchased a spec home which included most, but not all, of what I wanted in a new home.
review_text: The purchase was a seamless process and all Shea representatives were helpful and responsive to any questions I had. Now that I am in my home the onsite team has been equally responsive and helpful in taking care of any tweaks that have come up.

title: Good experience
review_text: Buying process was a little difficult due to communication. The Teams app is not a good communication device. Too slow and lag time for response was slow. And responses were short and lacked detail. More detail of the interior progress would have been greatly appreciated. Just front lics of the exterior were not adequate.

title: So many wonderful things to celebrate at Trilogy
review_text: My home buying/building experience at Verde River was long, sometimes arduous, and because it took so long interest rates went up several points in the interim. This was very upsetting to me. It was not what I’d originally signed up for. Happily, I do love my new home. It’s lovely, excellent quality, and the Shea employees from A to Z couldn’t be nicer or more helpful. The area is beautiful, the club is wonderful, and my house is now feeling like my home.

title: Happy Homebuyer
review_text: Shae was great with keeping my husband and I updated during the home building process. We purchase during Covid and there were a lot of delays due to Covid delays. The construction manager was an angel and very responsive every time we sent a question or concern. Very happy with our builder choice!!! We love our new home!

title: Owen, Owen, Owen, Owen, Owen.
review_text: Owen was awesome to work with from the sales side. We built during a very difficult time for everyone, so I do understand the delays we went through.

title: Homeowner
review_text: Had a great experience with Shea overall at the direct level. However, had I hired some of the subcontractors myself outside of costs being part of the mortgage — including those who did the blinds (sloppy work), solar/Sdge (still trying to setup), or misnamed “white glove” service (just a bizarre lack of quality service) — I would have moved on long ago and found better service providers. Two months in, I still can’t get a few things completed that should be off the list of to-dos. These annoyances, and I hate to say it, ultimately fall back on Shea Homes — who has otherwise been fantastic.Overall, the important things are more than great. The annoyance level grievances are just an honest assessment of things a new homeowner, in a perfect world, should not have to waste so much time dealing with and Shea Homes should be more discerning/on-top-of their subcontractors on behalf of their new homeowners.

title: Good Experience
review_text: Jacqui and Mae were great, easy to work with, and informative every step of the way. This is our second time buying a Shea home. We hope there aren't too many physical issues with the home like we had last time, and that if any, they'll be taken care of within the warranty timeframe.

title: Good overall construction and service
review_text: Overall, a good buying experience with Shea Homes. I was told of a framed walk-thru, but never happened. Timelines were non-existed or vague. Construction workers and other workers were helpful and very knowledgeable. Location and pricing were excellent. Buying process were a bit chaotic with VIP's being involved in the buying process.

title: More options
review_text: The entire process was ok ….If you buy a lot to be build on it make sure that everything is transparently disclose if outlets and other utilities boxes are posted on your front yard …so no surprises at the end ! No to pleased with interior decorator support ! However After Closing the assistance and attention to details received from the house delivery representative Richard is been Outstanding!!!!

title: Great with build, slow with repairs
review_text: Shea does a really great job with their new builds. We were surprised how close they stuck to the original schedule despite all of the shortages and supply chain issues. After moving in there were a few issues that have taken a long time to resolve. Our water heater was supposed to have a recirculation pump, for example. 2 months in and the recirculation pump still isn't functioning after multiple visits from plumbers and the customer care team. Overall we're happy despite the minor issues.

title: Encanterra
review_text: If you are coming to Encanterra I suggest getting Aimee as your sales rep. She was the BEST part of the Shea buying experience. She was incredible to work with. I think all builds are a test in patience. This one was even more so, with supply chain and labor shortages it took a lot longer to build than would be expected. There were probably more issues to deal with than normal as well. Aimee was on top of all of them. Even when we came up with a snag with financing. I wouldn't suggest using Shea financing. But, Aimee stepped up to make things happen here too. She was in it until theend. I wouldn't be here without her. And, I am happy as can be to be here. Ask for Aimee if you get the chance! And welcome to Encanterra!

review_text: We found the house to be of very good quality for a nationwide builder. The only negatives were the lottery process for obtaining a lot and Shea Mortgage.
