title: Shea Homes - Well, it took a very long time to get the house but, it was worth
review_text: Well, it took a very long time to get the house but, it was worth the wait. First and foremost, the location is premium! The builder kept many of the older trees and blended water tolerant plants to create an open, green environment The architecture, aligned to Feng Shui principles, reinforces the feeling of harmony and tranquility reflected in the community. Finally, I have to truly say that Philippe's genius matched us with the most delightful neighbors. Wayne and I feel grateful and fortunate to have found our last home. Thank you, Shea,

title: Finding Our Forever Home
review_text: We started our journey with Shea in May of 2021. First we selected our lot and home model, signed the purchase contract and then spent a full day at the Design Studio to select our options to customize our home. Then the long wait for our home to be built began. Due to the effects of Covid19 on the availability of building materials as well as workers, what was normally a 5 month construction period lasted a bit over a year. Yes, it was frustrating to keep waiting for our closing date but in the end, we were so pleased with how our house turned out. We chose the Declare model and the finalproduct was just as we imagined it. Sure, we noticed a few things that were not perfect but Dawn and the Warranty team are making sure that all issues are being resolved. Thanks Shea for building our Forever Home in this wonderful community of Ocala Preserve.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - From signing contract to closing the home took 13 months to complete.
review_text: From signing contract to closing the home took 13 months to complete. Issue found during construction and walk through was not fixed prior to move in. Dry wall issues are being delayed repairs for one year. Irrigation system is tied into other houses and over watering of lawn has occurred for two months.

title: Shea Homes - It would better to each house in the area to have a unique house
review_text: It would better to each house in the area to have a unique house number even the homes are on different streets. There are several homes with the same house numbers. This causes mis-deliveries of packages

title: Shea Homes - Overall I felt Shea did a good job
review_text: Overall I felt Shea did a good job

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - My wife and I found that our customer representative did not
review_text: My wife and I found that our customer representative did not provide information to us unless we asked a specific question. Even then, there were many questions she simply relied with "I don't know". She did not offer to find the answer most of the time. Once we took possession of our home, we were very pleased with the workmanship and quality. We have had some minor repairs that needed to be addressed and the builder/vendors seem to be ready and willing to make us happy. While the purchase experience was not a pleasant as we expected, the end result is more than expected.

title: Great communication.
review_text: We bought here at Trilogy just from what we saw online and what our realtor told us as we lived across the country. Our realtor met with the sales staff and was impressed and that went a long way with us. Our first trip was a great success as we signed our contract. The process started and we went back to NJ. The construction manger Joe kept us informed all along the way with emails and phone calls. I met him for a pre drywalling inspection and all went well and was very informative. Building at this time was difficult due to labor and supplies being short but they worked through itall. The house was delivered approximately a little over 1 year from signing the contract. All is well with a few hiccups that is to be expected but they are fixing any small issues we have.

title: Shea Homes - Had good working relationship with sales team, construction crew
review_text: Had good working relationship with sales team, construction crew and post-completion complaaints team. Would have liked to see Shea offer more options at reasonable price, like interior cabinet, garage flooring, etc.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Feel like we have a good home - not custom but comfortable.
review_text: Feel like we have a good home - not custom but comfortable. Some minor changes would have been nice (semi gloss vs flat, etc.) but Shea doesn't allow it.

title: A perfect fit
review_text: After looking to downsize in several locations, we found way more house for the money at Trilogy at Tehaleh. Mount Rainier shines over this community that has fantastic amenities—a pool, an excellent restaurant, and a large network of well-maintained walking trails. Shea continues to works hard to seek our input on how things are going and if we have any issues. We also chose to work with Shea Mortgage which made the buying process seamless. This is a very friendly neighborhood. For us, it is a perfect fit.

title: New Home
review_text: Settling in new home and getting prompt help whenever needed

title: Wish I could give 5 stars…
review_text: First, the good. We love our new home. We love the community as well. From Jason (Sales) to Laura (Design), to Neil (construction) and then Connie (warranty and walk through) we could not be more pleased and thankful for their care, response time, excellent skills and assistance! They all get 5 stars!! Now, the not so good. The landscaping came up short, way short. From having a hard time with initial quote (getting answers on how the fence pricing would work, etc.) and then having it be last step in the design process and be hit with huge price increase from initial estimated quote! Thefence continued to be troubling as we neared close as no one communicated to us that our to be neighbors required pool fencing on both sides of us. We paid for our entire fence (which was optional for us) & having been given no opportunity to work with our neighbors, we almost lost square footage to our property. We were told, after we inquired why fence on side of our property was 6 inches closer to our home than the neighbor’s, that since we did not sign off on anything, they automatically placed fence 4 inches (albeit it was 6) towards our home. We were able to get it fixed…but it required a lot of emailing and phone calls, us being the driver. We do appreciate Omar (landscaper-labor) for his quick work and kind attitude. To add insult to injury on the fence, we were contacted 2 months after we closed and moved in, by TLB Office (Tara), informing us that they reviewed our paperwork and found in original quote and signed off price at design (where our fence price had gone from original estimate of $3k or less to $4400) had somehow not included the fence line across back of yard!! They wanted another $2800 from us! However, all our paperwork stated the fence was a full yard fence and we presented the copies we signed off on and they determined it to be error on those we worked with in beginning of process, (Carson/Hunter) , they “waived” the charge and hoped we enjoyed our fence over the 4th of July! Another item that we are still dealing with, and are concerned over, is our “whole home” water filtration system. Bottom line is this, they installed a different type than we understood we were to have. We were not aware this occurred until closing. We had to inquire several times for information on the system and still are not clear on how to maintain it. On an up note? Our water quality is awesome! And we require no salt with this system, and have no loud flushing sounds in wee hours of the morning, plus, we do not need to buy filters for fridge because water is filtered as it enters home. We just felt the change to different system was one we should have been informed of and certainly been given better info on maintenance, I mean, this is our WATER we are dealing with. I’ll end on the upnote I began with, we love our home, and the community. Had it not been for the issues addressed here, they would have been given 5 stars across board. We have been through building process 3 times before this, and none of those journey’s had Covid and supply chain issues…kudos to Shea on that point, as they did everything they could to stay on time with close est. we appreciate them.

title: Love my house but lack of information during the build process was frustrating
review_text: The build process was frustrating as the Builder was so afraid of saying something that could be interpreted as a commitment they they instead opted to just not communicate at all. They would not even give me a rough estimate of the timeline which made finding a temporary rental extremely challenging. I understand the COVID delays and that they are very real. But communication is better than no communication. Having said all that, I do love my house and have no regrets choosing Shea.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Overall a great experience since I was living in California during
review_text: Overall a great experience since I was living in California during the build. I have noticed some workmanship that I’m not 100 percent happy with though, but things I point out generally get corrected. Overall very happy and love my new home and neighbors.

title: Shea Homes - Overall the experience was good.
review_text: Overall the experience was good. Definitely frustrating that they raised prices as much as they have. Priced us out of upgrades we would have wanted. The sales and building process was fairly straightforward, but definitely took longer than they thought it would.

title: Shea Homes - The process was long due to Covid and the problems with materials

Shea representatives were always able to talk to even if the news from them wasn't what you wanted to hear. They did the best they could with the trades available to complete the home and make the required fixes. We love the design, feel, and function of the new home tremendously.

review_text: We received adequate updates during the build of our new home. We appreciated the quick responses to our questions & concerns.

title: Sales team are amazing! Quality of Contractors could improve
review_text: The overall buying experience was great. What I do think could have been improved is the oversight of the contractors. For our particular home there were some serious plumbing issues that had to be resolved right after closing. I understand that they work with a lot of contractors, but these types of things need to be caught and addressed before closing ideally. The sales team were amazing and always diligent in responding back to our questions/concerns. We never felt pressured to buy or forced to buy and they were always transparent of the entire process. Including discussing easements,what the HOA covers, important timelines, closing handouts, etc.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Sales (April) were very helpful; would like to have had more
review_text: Sales (April) were very helpful; would like to have had more flexibility in design phase. We had had experience in designing a custom home so this was a disappointing experience

title: Timeliness
review_text: The picking out of the options was difficult and not very flexible. Home structure is sound and Shea delivered our project pretty much on time even with the shortages the building industry is having at this time.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - It is obvious Shea has been around, as a developer, for many
review_text: It is obvious Shea has been around, as a developer, for many years. Not only is the community plan laid out nicely, but the floor plans of each individual model make sense and function very well.

title: Designing our home with Debbie. Great experience.
review_text: Overall a great experience, great quality and great service.

title: Armando our construction manager on keeping everything under his control accomplished
review_text: This Shea process is very well orchestrated but a few years behind in human needs and construction techniques and interior design and lighting opportunities. Most mechanical aspects are good but instant hot water is too far away from the delivery points and runs wasted water. Water lines in attic causing hot water rather than cold in hot weather. In interior design very little flexibility in choices and options along with vendors and time with designer. No options for lighting and wall plug locations. Landscape palette is not appropriate in many areas of varieties not regionally real in thesetting. Sales not clear on interior color options and fron/side street frontage and rear yard setbacks for pools and equipment and landscape. Policy on two trees per rear yard is not enforced. Astroturfing or other fake grass should be outlawed as to heat gain and burning feet. Trash enclosure should be mandatory and areas between homes have tree requirements assisting in reflecting heat and producing shade and oxygen. Policy on maintenance of property line block walls and steel picket fencing need to be maintained by the Association.

review_text: For us it was a no brained to go with a new build and we are so happy that it ended up being Shea.

review_text: Home is well insulated due to use of 2x6 studs. Seems solid.

title: Our sales person Sonja was very informative about Trilogy at Lake Norman and assisting us in make our decision to build after various meetings. Second person was Sonny our field manager. He was available to answer questions as the process began and we co
review_text: Overall, we are happy living here! We are enjoying the house as well as meeting so many new friends. This is the 4th home we have built and we understand the process. We asked questions in advance and accepted that this home wasn’t 100% a custom home since we needed to stay within the Shea home plan. Again, we have confidence with Sonja & Sonny and they made themselves available to talk to us when issues arise. We had issues with the design team where incorrect kitchen cabinets, doors, ceiling molding and bathroom counter were installed per our contract. We lost confidence with thedesign team’s lack of responsibility and this includes their manager on site who never followed up after meetings with us. Sonny did a yeoman’s job working out these issues and we have total confidence in him. We could count on him to get us answers.

title: Sales Team
review_text: When going over the options the sales team encouraged you to shop around for the best options for your needs and lifestyle. They didn’t try to encourage you to just buy what they offered.

title: Responsiveness to issues
review_text: Responsiveness to issues, excellent staff on issues

title: Like the community design
review_text: Sales office and Shea home mortgage personnel are very responsive for questions. The design and layout of the house is very modern. Love the house fill with lights.

title: The process was very orderly and the home was delivered on schedule. We were kept in the loop on progress and there were timely responses to issue which we raised.
review_text: The concern I had was with the disclosure or lack thereof regarding the surround sound system for the house. We had it installed by the builders subcontractor Adobe, but were never advised that they did not hook the speakers up to the owners receivers and equipment as part of the process. This should have been disclosed by Adobe and/or Shea as part of the build process.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - I liked being able to pick a certain package however I didn't
review_text: I liked being able to pick a certain package however I didn't like that I could not make a change. For instance I ordered blinds but did not want them on the small window & could not have them not put those up. The reason I took off the stars was because when I moved in my outside water did not work because of a kink in the line & they had to cut a hole in my wall. Then the breaker switch on my tv kept going off making me think I had a tv problem. No the electric lines in the switch plate were touching. In both cases Shea did react quickly to resolve the issues but both were verystressful for me.

title: Great customer service team post move in
review_text: First 30 day punch list was long but expected. Our customer service rep assigned to our house was on top of all the issues. The trades were great and responsive to the issues as well. Only wish the dust and truck traffic would be improved by requiring trucks to use the access road.

title: A pleasantly running machine
review_text: The overall experience was very good. As to quality: the windows offer much worse sound insulation compared to the replacement Windows we put into our other residence; the interior walls are paper thin; they saved a bit of money by not putting any natural light in the master bath and poorly placed the shower door to it is hard to get out. Still, while not perfect it is a lovely home and Shea is a professional machine which builds lots of homes on time.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - I think the project manager made sure we were happy with our
review_text: I think the project manager made sure we were happy with our home and on the whole, it was very good quality. However, some of the items we picked out were not what was installed. We picked out different doors than what were installed, that was a disappointment.

title: A solid home.
review_text: Overall the home is solid. Sales office was professional and responsive to questions during the purchase and prior to closing. However, the inability to make simple electrical or plumbing change to the interior "cookie cutter" electrical and plumbing design was frustrating! Very surprised at some of the pre-determined choices. It's the "details". Why puts a shingle roofs on an a $700k home in hail country? (We Understand costs...) Why put decor electrical switches throughout the home, but "old style" (round) plugs? They don't match. Upgraded to a tankless water heater, but "recirculation"plumbing upgrade was not offered as an upgrade to accompany to tankless heater. Very poor "stewardship" of completed trades' work by follow-on tradesmen. (Examples available). Lots of patching and fixing prior to move-in. For example, the upgraded stair railing looks Great, now; but the patches required to fix all the dents and scratches can be felt as one runs their hand along the railing. Excellent, excellent warranty follow-up by Shea's representative. "Above and beyond!" Again, overall a "solid" home which we hope to enjoy for many years to come. Just some annoying small details which would not take much work to correct.

title: Shea Homes - The home buying process was good.
review_text: The home buying process was good. They kept constant communication with me throughout the build. The home has many smart features that other builders don’t use so it’s nice to see that. The workmanship of the home is good, there were some areas that could have been done better. The team is working with me now to get those things fixed. Overall decent experience.

title: Overall good experience.
review_text: The builder did a good job. Just a little slow to follow up with little things to be corrected.

title: Korbin was amazing through the whole process
review_text: I really enjoyed working with Korbin, he was knowledgeable, very responsive and always on top of things. The construction was quick, but with that as usual there were some issues. Even things that were pointed out months in advance didn't get fixed before completion, and still haven't been fixed 2 months after move in even after promised within 10 days. Overall 4 out of 5!

title: Ability to work with sales and construction team
review_text: Shea allows a home buyer to work with the teams to answer questions and address issues.

title: Karina was fantastic....she provided exemptlary support and service.
review_text: Overall we were very satisfied, the experience was better than expected.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - House met our expectations and then some.
review_text: House met our expectations and then some. Very happy with all the personal involved in the transaction.

title: Great customer service!
review_text: Our Sales Person and Field/Site Manager were willing to do whatever it took to make us happy. Above and beyond what might be expected.

title: the passionate, knowledgeable people in all the different phases and the quality of the build
review_text: They care about the build and entire process

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Home was very well built and products supplied were of good quality.
review_text: Home was very well built and products supplied were of good quality. We we delayed by over 2 weeks which caused us problems with rental housing and extra storage costs for our furniture

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - We are pleased with our home and we feel that Shea deserves the
review_text: We are pleased with our home and we feel that Shea deserves the good reputation that it has. We had a couple of issues when we first moved but they have been resolved. The customer relations people have been excellent. We were a little disappointed with the slow response of the HOA on some issues but that is improving. (We are very supportive of a strong HOA by the way but they should answer phone calls and e-mails promptly.)

review_text: Quality home; Representative ensures any concerns are answered on a timely basis as well as any fixes are done immediately after each inspection.

title: They did good quality work and completed the house when they said they would
review_text: This was our first experience dealing with a large builder and we were pleasantly surprised. They were very responsive to our questions and concerns and they got the home done on time - even in the middle of a pandemic.

title: Salesman (Frank) at Trilogy was attentive to our needs. He was tenacious in selling us a home, presenting the community well.
review_text: Be active in your examination of the house and property, the company with follow up with problems that you find and repair them as needed.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Good quality and good responsiveness
review_text: Good quality and good responsiveness

review_text: Home completed early. Finishes are attractive, great floorplans

title: Really responsive and quality options.
review_text: Had good experience with sales staff. Construction is really good quality. Stuck to timelines and kept me informed. Everyone was available for questions and answered them timely.

title: Team is courteous and responsive
review_text: I will buy again w shea

title: We had a problem with work verification as the info needed was not asked for until a week before closing. While it worked out in the end it did cause much worries.
review_text: The loan process was laborious. The building process was top notch and a pleasure. Sales, design studio, and our construction manager, all great.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Inside has minor issues.
review_text: Inside has minor issues. Landscaping & trash enclosure have issues that haven’t been completed.

title: It's like the easy button!
review_text: Reasonable value in a friendly and highly amenitized community. Service was good both before AND after the sale.

title: Timeline
review_text: The builder was on time even with the pandemic. The time was followed. The downside is when we asked about the backyard to the Sales team how large it would be, the answer they said, it would be similar to the model homes. That wasn't the case. It was smaller. As a buyer, we would expect the same exact thing from the model home. Also, solar system comes with the house, but it's taking forever to get the activation done.

title: I am impressed with Shea's organization, employees and care for serving their customers.
review_text: My sales girl was my saving grace. She helped me with the many questions I had. Moving from another state, being homeless for nine months while my home was being built and traveling with three cats and a dog, across the USA, to stay with friends and relatives, during Covid wasn't easy. D. C. was so wonderful to answer all my questions often, and always within 24 hours.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - We love our new home.
review_text: We love our new home.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Olivia who helped us through all the time from showing us the
review_text: Olivia who helped us through all the time from showing us the various styles offered to moving in was a wonderful help.

title: Shea Homes - Buying a house from Shea Homes has been nothing but an amazing
review_text: Buying a house from Shea Homes has been nothing but an amazing experience. We are extremely pleased with the service that the whole team has provided.

title: Quality
review_text: The first word that comes to mind when I think about the homes built by Shea is Quality. As soon as you walk in, you feel like you have walked into a home where quality really matters, from the finishings to the sturdiness of the building. The salesperson gave us a great tour of the entire community and was very knowledgeable about the area. She showed us homes that fit into our budget which, in turn, helped us find our new home. We also looked at the homes being offered by the other builders in the community but they were either out of our price range or did not feel like the same qualitythat the Shea Homes have. Shea also have a great customer service program after the sale. Our representative has gone out of his way to find and replace one of the tiny lightbulbs in the vent over our cooktop! All in all, our experience with Shea has been very positive. We love our new Shea home! (By the way, I never give the highest rating because that leaves nothing for you to strive for!)

title: Shea Homes - Good build quality, but not great.
review_text: Good build quality, but not great. Sales process was simple and streamlined. Overall a good experience except for HOA CC&Rs - overkill for 70 home tract.

title: What a home!
review_text: Kudos to shea for coming up with a new, innovative, out of the box floorplan. Our single level flourish model is beautiful and we love it! Only wish that the design center had more samples of their finishes. Some of the samples did not show the variations in the material (which is normal), but not to the extent we encountered. Although it was a little misleading, we made it work, and after 90 days we're still in love!

title: Experience was pretty easy.
review_text: Overall the experience was easy and fairly quick.

title: The value for money that I got from Shea was Amazing
review_text: As a first time I was apprehensive of how it will be, but the Shea team helped me step by step throughout the home buying process. Most importantly since I was one of the first 3 buyers in this community we got a really good value for money.

title: Builder - Great! 3rd Parties - Needs work
review_text: Working with Shea has been a very positive experience. I have nothing but great things to say about them. My one negative has been the third party landscaper chosen which is Gothic Landscape. If I had known how unresponsive and unreliable they are, I would have waited to do my full landscaping with another vendor after closing.

title: quality control relative to workmanship needs attention
review_text: Poor quality control. Builder unfortunately cannot refute this because this is in the eyes of the beholder. Compared to other builders I cannot say, but coming from an industry where QC is extremely important Shea does not measure up. That is to say, my neighbor may very well think the workmanship is wonderful. I do not. Issues that I have had to call to attention of builder reps were easily fixed but why did they have to be pointed and then easily fixed? One SHEA person assigned to quality control could do a quality walk through minimizing buyer complaints and the buyers failure tonote discrepancies on their walk through. Afterall, typically the buyer is not the expert.

title: Shea stands behind their product. Responsive to problems. Our Shea rep, Jonathan, has been fantastic
review_text: Shea is a quality builder. Subcontractors are "iffy". Had some major plumbing issues that were resolved. Contractors leaving garbage strewn outside construction sites that happen to be in the middle of our neighborhood. HOA dues to cover all amenities but majority of amenities are closed. ADJUSTMENT TO DUES SHOULD HAVE BEEN OFFERED

title: Shea Homes - Shea started off with a good online tool to monitor progress
review_text: Shea started off with a good online tool to monitor progress of building process, but found it was not keeping pace with build out.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Lovely community, houses, and landscaping are as expected in
review_text: Lovely community, houses, and landscaping are as expected in a retirement area.

title: Our sales rep, Aimee, was key to our overall satisfaction with our Shea experience—always available and responsive.
review_text: This is the second Shea home we’ve built. We’ve always been pleased with the quality of the finished product, as well as Shea’s service both before and after the sale.

title: Style, size and lots to do
review_text: I love the style and size of my new Shea home. It has everything I need and for the right price with quality finishes. The community is well planned to include many things to do. It is everything I was looking for to have an active life when I retire.

title: Exceptional sales support from Sonja and build support from Connor
review_text: Enjoy the community very much

title: Trusted Builder & Team
review_text: Great buying experience and response to issues with the structure after moving in. Very disappointed with the landscaping behind our new home. The plantings along the walking path behind our home are dead/dying and the area just full of weeds. No one seems to want to fix it. Wish we had resolved this before moving in.

title: On time Schedule & follow up support during the build & after
review_text: Good quality, several options to make the house your home

title: I love Shea homes
review_text: I love the design and value of Shea homes, over all I would say they are a better than average builder. Depends on which customer service representative that being assigned, responsiveness is from bad to excellent. I had good and bad experiences with them.

title: We are really happy here
review_text: We can’t say enough about Karina and how much she helped us. That being said, I wish we had any time with the landscape folks. We didn’t. Sure, at the beginning they were there but it would have been nice to have more discussions with them along the way. We bought a 600$ maple tree for the backyard that comes with no guarantees. Lowe’s and Home Depot guarantees their plants for a year. Not happy about that. We are treating that tree better then our first born son. Shea homes has been very good to us and we are thankful for that. We are somewhat concerned about having another pool or clubseeing all the new building going on. I don’t think the existing pool and club can accommodate all the new homes.

title: Happy with my Shea Home
review_text: Shea produced a solid home and have been responsive to issues.

title: Unable to Access Garage….not remedied after 2+ months
review_text: We are unable to access the garage and it requires cutting the corner in the parking area to make our garage, however, if anyone is parked there it is difficult to make the turn into the garage with 1 car and impossible with our 2nd vehicle. Currently the parking spots are being used by non-tenants which fortunately has allowed us to discover this problem… when exiting, we can’t clear the cars from our garage and make the turn if they are sticking out just a hair. I honestly think making it an Emergency parking RED curb area is the only way to go! I noticed that the original Shea maps didnot include a parking area behind us (unit 1504) nor did it include extending the walkway from our front door to the back garage/parking area. I assume the parking was added at some point (maybe more guest parking / per unit ratio?) This is perhaps why Shea needed the to extend our front door entry walkway to the back parking so folks could reach the Units in front? We were unable to to discover this issue during the purchase because Shea stored their equipment and porta potties in the parking area so we never could attempt an ingress or egress of the physical garage. Thanks for listening and let’s get a vote/call made to reserve that parking for emergency vehicles only and allow us to have access to our garage before more tenets move in! Cheers, Jason and Lisa

title: Onsite staff great but working with Low Quality materials
review_text: We were told that "NO" is a favorite word of Shea's. That is not a problem as you know exactly what you can and cannot have. However, that NO was taken to extremes when even a minor tweak for a home almost $1M was not able to be granted. Upgrades are severely overpriced and structural options are limited. The best thing TLN has going is the clubhouse community - it's why IMHO anyone would spend an exorbitant amount of money to live here.

title: Seamless but flawed purchase
review_text: It’s hard for me to accept that when you buy a home the brand new oven trips the breaker. Shea knew before the purchase and said nothing. Now I’m stuck waiting on a part that is three more weeks out to hopefully fix the issue. It’s already been 10’ weeks. I’ve now learned it’s a know issue with many homes in the area. Pretty disappointing for a brand new home:

title: Shea Flats Fox Point Farms
review_text: Second Shea home purchased. Both Shea homes I purchased are built with quality. Any issues are promptly addressed.

title: Overall, satisfied with our move to Trilogy at Lake Frederick

Thank you!

There are certainly some quality issues and with the shea product and I feel they need to hirebetter subs to get this work done. Overall, the experience has been good.

review_text: Number of small details are missed. Over all a solid house but they are no longer as premium as they used to be. They have cut cost considerably and there finishes are not as they used to be.

review_text: There are obvious problems with doors, windows, water pipes, refrigerators, and walls, and the emergency response speed is very slow.

title: Our experience with Ryan, construction manager, was very positive.
review_text: Our positive part of this experience has been with the sales team, especially Anjuli and the building manager, Ryan. The negative portion of the experience is simply the quality of home and craftsmanship which is subpar. This is from framing and exterior wall studs between 16" to 24". Closer to 24" than 16", but never uniform. The backplash tile was redone, cabinets not level, mud on can lights, painting trim redone, exterior painting redone, very cheap linoleum that tore quite easily in the laundry room, an air bubble in the LVP, the island counter was not square and the dining room wallwas not square. Shea has fixed nearly all of the defects. We have had to spend lots of additional money to fix shortcomings and just make the home look like it has a higher quality to it.

title: We love Wickenburg Ranch!
review_text: Shea is a great home builder! Andrew, our warranty specialist, is great to work with!

title: Kathy
review_text: Responsive to concerns Not solved the larger concern Working on it

title: Overall pretty good
review_text: Over all my experience was pretty good. The home looks very nice, and most everything is in place. Some expected issues with a new construction are fine, but there are some more than I would have expected for the price paid, and some of the issues I pointed out were ignored, denied, or called normal when they should have been taken care of. Otherwise, quick to respond and set up trade calls, but it often takes a long time to get the trades to show up.

title: Sales person was excellent. Long wait time. Almost two years from the time I decided to build until I closed on my home
review_text: Staff was very friendly, but sometimes did not come through with requests. East to contact but not always responsive. If I needed an immediate response I had to go through my sales person even though I was told by project manage I could contact him.

title: Overall a great buying experience with little hiccups
review_text: All through the buying process, everything was a smooth transition. There are few issues after moving to the home related to USPS address issue and HOA. Both of them are pathetic and builder is not able to help us.

title: Ryan, LaLa, Kara
review_text: First, I would like to focus on the positive aspects of our experience. There are three people who have contributed to making this experience a success. They are Ryan, Kara and Lala. Each has contributed in their own way to help promote a collaborative effort to realize the completion of our home. Our first contact with Shea was Kara. From the start, she was professional, personable and had a great sense of humor. She was tolerant of our many questions, requests and complaints. Her graceful approach helped smooth things out that led to the creation of our home and hopefully a continuedrelationship. Ryan was with us the most. His generosity and gentle demeanor walked us through the different stages of the building process. He was challenged on many occasions by some of the vendors and then had to work with us to explain why things were not going as planned. There were challenging times, but he stuck with us, much to his credit. Lastly, LaLa has provided us with the follow up of warranty work, questions on how things work or don’t and kindness and humor when dealing with vendors who don’t show up for a service call. LaLa, like Ryan, goes above and beyond to provide us with the information and help we have needed to get this house off to a good start. On more than one occasion, she has made time for us outside of her schedule to help us. She is always positive and a delight to be with. There were a few issues that I would like to bring to your attention. During our buying and building experience, design changes were made but not communicated to us until after the fact. This required time and effort to develop a new design plan and to resolve ourselves that what we thought we could have was not possible. The other change that I would request is that you give your team the authority to make decisions based on their expertise and experience. For example, we have a scratch in one of our windows. An appointment was made for the vendor to address this. We waited an entire day for this gentleman to come out and say “Yes, it’s scratched and should be replaced “. This is a huge waste of our time to sit at home the entire day only to have the vendor confirm what Lala had already determined as a defect. It would save time and money and frustration for your homeowners not to have to go through an inefficient process such as this. Without the help, kindness and support of Kara, Ryan and Lala I would not have bought or continued with building this home.

title: Dismissed
review_text: The quality of Shea homes is amazing. Feel like I had to always reach out for communication. Follow up on services requests are slow and because my concerns were minor, I felt like I was often dismissed. When we purchased our home I requested a specific lot but was told they couldn’t go out of order and I had to choose from the lots they stated. After we signed our contract, market changed and low and behold they are not going in order and the lot I requested was built immediately.

title: Experience
review_text: People at Shea are great but have had several small issues after purchasing home. Response to resolve have been great.

title: Stellar Experience ***** Great Company
review_text: Shea Home is a state of the art home - if you can build updates with good design style, sky is the limit! You can arrange a great home build set up with Shea Team! Great Professionals! Thanks for our new Home!

title: Trilogy home
review_text: The home buying experience was overall good, but felt rushed before closing due to our agent being moved to another project a few weeks before closing. We were not able to work on a few changes and we were told it was not allowed. Our agent was excellent to work with but pulled off for another project before our close. In addition, the sub contractors that need to fix problems after the 1 month walk through are not very reliable, especially with window replacements, and other documented areas of the house.

title: Disappointed with several finishes
review_text: Stucco not cured properly on sunny side of house. Lots of cracking in the finish. Integral dark color finish installed. Where patches have been made, the color varies and photographs issues. Color missing in some locations. Stone veneer is sloped. Drywall nail heads are visible with some heads popping through. Tape joints are exposed. Cornerbead flanges are visible. 1/2" wide tiles installed from a 12x24 tile. Instead of recutting a larger tile to fit the space, a sliver was cut to make up the incorrectly cut tile. Sales team was great. Designer was extremelyhelpful. Superintendent finished the house on time. Expected more from a Shea home.

title: Mac on the sales team was great. Conor the warranty rep.
review_text: Sales team was great. Warranty representative and response is terrible.

title: The house was built with professionalism.
review_text: Our experience between purchasing the house and figure out the rest was intense meaning Club House Members - HOA (3 different identities) it will be nice to know at the closing. title: New Home at Shea
review_text: Field manager was outstanding! Plumber utilized was average at best! It is deceiving to see a model home nicely decorated and not having same or similar options when building a new house( very disappointing no alternatives were available). Very pretty house and development!

title: Needs improvement
review_text: The trades should adopt the saying “Do it right the first time”

title: Home Sweet Home
review_text: Communication with the Shea Rep, field manager and warranty manager was very good. The Adobe systems were not transparent during the options/design decisions. For example, they didn't say that there were 3 places installed for the alarm keypad. Instead they waited until I was doing the set up after close to tell me that there was an extra charge per extra keypad. I found out through a friend it was wired for extra keypads. Dimmers on some low voltage lights under the counter but not under the bathroom toe kick. Not until I asked after closing did I get that. It is hard enough to moveinto a new house without having constant workmen coming and going. I opted for a light on the back of my house but that didn't include the fixture. Really? Couldhave said that. I had to buy it online and pay an electrician to install it. More prompts from the design center saying "do you want this?" Can't think of everything in those 2.5 days of design options. Two months after closing I'm still dealing with contractors. The sloppiness of the painters getting specks of paint on the tile wverywhere could have been avoided if they used a drop cloth. Now I have to mark all the spots and have them remove them. It should be a requirement "enforced" to wear booties to avoid concrete footprints on the tile. No one paid attention to the signs on the doors. Cleaning in preparation for my move in could have been better especially the glue on the tub, shower door and the windows. I was never asked where I wanted my dining room fixture so I had to pay an electrician to move and center it along with a can that was in the wrong place. I was never asked if I wanted a 20 amp in my garage. Again had to hire an electrician to put that in and pay for drywall repair on that and the light fixture positioning. Glue on the granite should have been cleaned off when installed. Being a detail person makes it hard to miss or ignore all of this. Overall I am very happy with my experience with Shea. Nothings perfect but the added stress would have been nice to avoid after closing. I really love all my options and my home is beautiful.

title: The builder was responsive to my calls.
review_text: Overall the experience was a very good one. Due to the pandemic, it has been hard to get repairs done in a timely fashion but my Trilogy team has been responsive to other smaller issues. I do appreciate the coordination between Trilogy and the HOA.

title: Good home but after sales
review_text: Great floor plans. Great pre and post sales. Building felt rushed. Could’ve used more quality contractors with less mistakes.

title: Good
review_text: We have few issues that have been solved professionally snd timely. Crystal wax very responsive

title: Beautiful Home
review_text: Despite a 7 month delay of moving in due to “municipalities,” the home is very beautiful and just what we expected. Management is friendly and responsive for the most part. There is a lot going on while construction is happening. We enjoyed meeting workers and networking to install this or that in our home. In case we have any problems in the future, we know who we can call.

title: Shannon, the sales rep and John, construction supervisor are super. We have found many items that needed repairs (like. electric wires not connected correctly) and some fixtures look like contractor went cheap. Overall happy with home but expected a li
review_text: See above for comments in this section.

title: Home buying experience
review_text: Overall a good experience. Warranty issues and communication could improve.

title: Well Informed of the delays during the building process
review_text: Kirstie kept us informed during the building process of the delays we were incurring. We have been in the house two months and expected some snags in the new house. The response to correcting these snags has been mediocre in the sense of timeliness, the initiative on the part of the builder, and the accuracy of the corrective action. For example, a 220 Volt outlet was ordered in one specific part of the house. An additional outlet was actually wired to 220 V unbeknownst to us. We ruined several appliances in the troubleshooting process not to mention a potentially hazardous situation. Thestaff has been helpful but I can only assume they are overloaded with work to be done/corrected. Thermostat was also somehow miswired causing it to NOT shut off the Air Conditioner. It was apparent that finishing touches were rushed to make the 30 Sept closing deadline (delayed from original 30 April closing) to include no water softener installed yet, poorly finished landscaping of the yard, missing door hardware, and some smaller items. Still awaiting several snags to be fixed, corrected, replaced, or rectified after what has seemed a reasonable amount of time.

title: New Home
review_text: The home took a little more time to be built than expected. They did not have framers for 3 months. Shea mortgage did not contact us 90 days out as promised. I had to call them. Brandon was very professional and helpful and continues to be when ever I have questions. Overall, satisfied with product after being in 2 months.

title: Pretty good except….
review_text: Quality is good, walk-through before the move in did not address key electrical issues, such as misplacement of outlets in certain areas of the house and refusing to correct after move-in. Wrong wiring in the bedrooms (lights flickering when ceiling fan is on). Upper kitchen cabinets are very shallow. Overall we are satisfied with these exceptions.

title: Ryan and Brandon were very helpful and responsive.
review_text: Generally good production build experience. Would have liked more flexibility with options and finishing, such as built-in cabinetry and shelving in the great room and study, or requiring the Shea pre-defined basement finish floor plan to get 10' foundation walls.

title: Our experience with Shea Homes
review_text: Nice house. Good quality but could be better. Builder very rigid with their dates and times for required meetings. Correcting issues after the sale has been a slow process.

title: Our Dream Home - with Challenges
review_text: Finding The Grove and going through the process of application and hopeful anticipation on being able to purchase a home was both exciting and stressful. The Shea representatives could not have been more helpful as we set out on our journey. We were encouraged to visit as the build of our home started and we did that......coming out every week to see how the house was progressing. We watched it go from an empty plot of dirt - to a foundation and more. We met the crew foremen, who through the entire process were as professional and nice as anyone you could ever meet. When we hadquestions, they took the time to answer them. As the build progressed and we began to meet others already moved in, we started to hear of the issues we would need to look out for first. Ants for one. Understandably, the entire complex created upheaval in the ant community. We knew we would have to attack them. We heard, as the process to build picked up speed, that with the pandemic waning and supply chain issues relaxing, that the time frame on our home being completed would be sped up. This was exciting news and we embraced it in our planning. We met the Shea Customer Service rep as a final walk thru still bubbling with excitement. We moved in. We attacked the ants and after a long, several week battle we put them at bay. We started to hear stories from others just moved in about issues with their build. Some were very concerning and even a danger to health and safety. We started to find things that needed fixing in our house as well. At first we wrote it off as being much like a new car, small things need to be tweaked or fixed. Being under warranty made it easier to accept the problems. But as we lived here from a few days to a few weeks the list of issues grew. Many texts and emails to Customer Service resolved some of the issues. Some were handled quickly, some ignored (so it seemed). Some still not resolved. When we hear of other owner's issues - we feel lucky because ours in some cases cannot compare to the degree of others. The issue many of us as homeowners have come to believe is that the builder fast tracked too quickly. Crews were (and are) being pushed to get the homes completed and closed. The quality control checks to see if everything works......if everything has been done correctly, FAILED. Many of our "punch-list" issues would have and could have been found and corrected before we moved in.....if the builder had taken more time and utilized quality check resources to do so. As such, the after purchase Customer Service Department and the HOA are slammed with questions - problems and complaints. Economically this is foolish. The amount of money the builder spent to put us here is compounded by the frequent re-visits and repairs that simply should have been done right the first time. Do we regret our purchase and our move? Absolutely not. Would we recommend Shea Homes? Probably. Others including family members of ours have lived in other Shea communities without the initial move-in repair issues many of us have encountered. Do we expect all our issues to be resolved? Absolutely. Does it taint even a bit on the move here? Yes, a little bit. But we are being patient and assuming (and hopeful) that our list of issues will be resolved and no further issues will arise. It will be...it can be...a beautiful community full of very nice people. Everyone seems to have the same issues and it is a topic of discussion whenever people meet. But everyone is hopeful the learning curve will subside and we can live for years to come in a wonderful community.

title: Shea Homes - I have been living in the Cay since March.
review_text: I have been living in the Cay since March. During this period of time, I have scheduled for three major repairs: the laundry room leaking, kitchen hood no ventilation, the AC unit leaking. All three problems were caused by workmanship not material itself. The worst experience is noise level from construction. Shea Home could have done better if considering strategically new phase construction impact to customers who are living in the closed phases. Workers started work at 7 am with loud machinery noise and unbearable dust. Very disturbing!

title: Our home is still missing items
review_text: Our home is still missing items. Other homes around us that are being completed have the items we are missing, yet we are constantly being told that we have to wait due to shortages. Do not accept your home if not 100% complete or you will be treated like a second rate human when it comes to getting the home completed as promised. We were so excited to buy our new home and especially it being a Shea Home, but have been treated like second rate humans after receiving our home. Each time we inquire about items that are supposed to be completed or replaced, we are told there are still backorders and we have to wait....except all the homes being built around us are getting the items. One would think that a company would take care of the people who trusted in them to keep their word. Sadly disappointed in our experience with Shea homes up to this point.

title: Neighborhood and home
review_text: Only wish yard was more level. Too Many unlevel spots in yard.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Office staff is nice and friendly.
review_text: Office staff is nice and friendly.

title: Details matter
review_text: Most of our house is great. The quality of most of what we have is awesome. But the details do matter and it does feel like we have some issues that Shea just isn't able to resolve -- Starting with the fact that our carpet is ruined after less than 2 months because it's impossible to clean, it pills constantly. And when it pills, it runs and loses its pattern for inches that are very noticeable. Our appliances don't match - for some inexplicable reason. And despite every attempt to get our "smart house" to connect our outlets to the app provided, we still have issues with these lightswitches. Do I love the house on the whole? Yes. But when you buy a million dollar house - you expect the details to be at their best.

review_text: The buying process with Owen was exceptional. The design center process was rushed and way too many options in models were no longer available, making decisions very frustrating. Since we were local, we were able to find issues to be corrected readily. Primarily because we kept issues on our field manager’s plate did we end up with a home with most item’s resolved. Working with Joe to fix first month problems worked great.

title: New home experience
review_text: Let me start by saying that my wife and I love the area, neighbors, and the model home we selected. We also appreciate many of the Shea associates who helped us along the new home journey. With that being said, unfortunately in my opinion the quality that was touted prior to building has left us very disappointed especially for the amount of money you are spending to purchase your new home. We understand that with a new home there will be some issues along the way, but we have had our fair share of challenges which has taken away the excitement of enjoying our new home. To Shea’s credit,the staff and workers have been very responsive to our needs in trying to make things right. I sincerely believe that with so many new homes under construction each with their own time frames, quality is definitely suffering. So, would I recommend Shea homes, time will tell. To early to make that recommendation.

review_text: Builder made my lending from builder quite easy and painless.

title: Our field managers Abbey and Jason were wonderful. Always answered our calls and did their best to satisfy us.
review_text: We got off to a rough start with Shea. We selected the Latigo model at original document signing, only to find out 4 weeks afterward that Shea did not include cabinets in the Casita nor would Shea install cabinetry in the Casita. No one ever said a word about this, not our sales rep or any of the staff in the model home. And the models had no signage indicating such. We felt that was very misleading. When I tried to reason with Melissa T. about this issue, she gave me a car purchasing analogy that I found a bit insulting considering the amount of money we were spending. When Shea started thebuild, the foundation was put in the wrong place and our friends noticed the placement and contacted us. Fortunately, I had the correct plot plan and was able to get it to Abbey and Shea before the concrete was poured. This delayed our build 6 weeks and there was no apology, no compensation, nothing from Shea except an "Oops". Strike 2. That was souring. Working with the designers was unusual since we had to make all the aesthetic selections on line due to the quarantine, but we were able to finish. Angela was excellent. Shea built our home with all the correct selections. We will have to see how it fares living in it. I have already noticed some sloppy craftsmanship around the window installs. Large gaps between the stucco were filled in with caulking. Definitely not fine home building there. I will address this on our 6 month walk through (or whatever the month walk through coming up). Thank you for this opportunity. Greg and Yvonne Lot 794

title: Shea Homes - Shea's sales, project management, and warranty team were great.
review_text: Shea's sales, project management, and warranty team were great. Trade work wasn't what I am use to for a Shea product.

review_text: OK with most issues concerning the Model house we bought. Having trouble with TESLA over solar panel exchange from SHEA to our own account.

title: Ads and build don’t always match
review_text: The home is lovely. Some tweaks, of course. But while they were building, we were buying furnishings and getting ready to fill the house appropriately. Since there was no model of the Liberty design here, we relied on the floor plan and virtual tours of Liberty’s online. The dining light was 8 ft. From the wall. But here it was hung 5 ft. From the wall, contrary to the videos and tours. They said it was according to the plans. Apparently Shea advertises one thing and delivers something different. In this case, a dining chandelier that is in the wrong place. They refuse to admit theimpracticality of the placement, claiming the blueprints were followed. We feel strongly that because every virtual tour of the Liberty shows it 8 feet from the wall should mean they should have to move our Liberty chandelier to 8 feet from the wall. They admit it is not in a usable spot, but say the blueprints were followed. Then CHANGE THE BLUEPRINTS...and move our chandelier to where it should be!

title: Not fully customizable
review_text: The hardware for all cabinets and drawers were not an option. Cabinet and drawer pulls are necessary but not provided. Was not given option to add electrical outlets (buyer needed) while building. Costed more to cut through drywall to add later. These are simple things that Shea could have provided that would make a lot a difference to a home buyer.

title: Two and a half months to get a garage door.
review_text: Originally Shea didn't want us to close in mid-December until a much delayed garage door arrived and was installed. They finally agreed to install an alternative garage door to allow us to proceed with our closing. That was much appreciated. Our garage door finally arrived over a month later and was installed but had two damaged panels. Replacement panels arrived damaged. We are now approaching a three month wait without an acceptable door. We've asked our damaged door to be painted the proper color to allow our home to appear completed from a distance, but Shea has refused.

review_text: Shea homes have a reputation for building a well constructed home and that’s why I chose them over other developers in the area. Great club house with many classes to choose from.

title: Opportunity to show a great sale clouded by poor workmanship
review_text: While the sale of the house and responsiveness to issues is actually very excellent. I feel the quality of work was very poor and attention to detail was extremely lacking. Having to work through all the issues and make sure they get resolved seems like it could have been avoided had the work been checked thoroughly before signing off on it. Customer care rep. Dominick has been very helpful though to make sure work will be repaired to customer satisfaction and always follows through with requests.

title: Shea builds a good overall home, but the options for interior are too limited
review_text: Shea is a production builder and inflexible to offer any options outside its standard ones. In some cases, like placing electrical outlets, they are very flexible, but in other cases, like flooring, appliances or kitchen/bathroom cabinetry, the choices are too constraining, staying within a single quality line from the manufacturer used (Wellborn in my case) and not offering true premium products. If you are OK with doing post-closing remodeling, then you can benefit from some of the things Shea does right, like overall great home layout, tankless water heater, and energy efficient house,which is very important.

title: Great company but growing too fast to be Excellent
review_text: Great people and company. My impression is everyone is too stretched. Responsiveness and detail support could be better. At least in the community I am in, Trilogy Rio Verde

title: Purchase
review_text: Shea Homes have a great reputation as a builder, and this is one of the main reasons I purchased this home.

title: Great experience but finishes could have been better
review_text: The buying experience was great, all Shea members were lovely to work with. We had a ton of issues with the finishes of the home. Everything is just a bit off - crooked, messy, missing, etc.

title: Design and materials are not good
review_text: Design is not good for the kitchen, the cabinets are too low. It makes the work zone is useless

title: Quality of work and the lack of customer service
review_text: We have found the Valor neighborhood in Idaho to be quite enjoyable overall. However, we have encountered some challenges with the quality of the finish work and the pre-inspection process. Upon moving in, we discovered several appliance issues that had not been addressed prior to our occupancy, despite Shea Homes' assurance of a complete home review. For the past three months, the oven has been non-functional, and the exterior paint on the house has been found to be faulty, requiring repainting, both have not been rectified. In addition to these issues, we have also observed subparworkmanship in other areas of the home. The concrete in the back porch was of poor quality, necessitating Shea Homes to apply epoxy as a remedy. Similarly, the finish on the garage floor is also below standard, and despite our requests, Shea Homes has not taken steps to rectify this matter. Furthermore, the wood frames on all the closets are coming apart, indicating further deficiencies in the construction. Outside the house, there have been safety concerns as well. The front walkway presented a trip hazard, resulting in multiple individuals tripping or falling. While a solution has been implemented, it is not up to par with the standards of the neighborhood and does not match the aesthetics of other homes in the area. Regrettably, our attempts to address these issues with Shea Homes have not yielded a satisfactory response. Although the warranty person assigned to our home is making efforts to rectify the situation, the time it is taking to resolve these matters is unacceptable. In conclusion, we are deeply disappointed with the lack of prompt and effective action from Shea Homes in addressing these concerns. We trust that Shea Homes will take our feedback seriously and work diligently to address these issues in a timely manner.

title: Susan
review_text: It was as expected for a new build

title: Builder / customer service
review_text: Hard to communicate with builder ….only text messages his phone mailbox always full. I understand delays but communication important! He was better toward end of construction _ mind you a year and half! I had one month appointment and still two issues not resolved? I’ve called twice - no call back? It’s been a month - I’ll have these two issues on the 5 month appointment- a few other things are needing attention as well! I get new homes always have issues - usually simple but would be nice for at least a call back.

title: Service after the sale. Quality issues.
review_text: Issues after delivery take too long to get resolved. We have 2 broken windows that were supposedly ordered before the house was finished. Been in house now 2 months, still have broken windows. Other issues same problem. Not responsive at all.

title: Warranty struggles
review_text: Buying was the east part, getting things fixed has been challenging. Both due to availability and half baked solutions, appointments are rescheduled without any communication, contractors refusing to fix things and blaming the issue on another team (plumber refusing to fix water pressure and passes blame to appliance installer when the issue is the water pressure from the wall, at another appointment scheduled for two window issues and contractor refused to fix one issue saying one window would be fixed by another team from the same company only to come back with the same team at anothertime to fix it). Some appointments are scheduled months out, haven’t been able to use the oven since purchasing the home 2 months ago for example. Had an issue with a door frame being crooked so they adjusted the door but the frame is still crooked to the naked eye. Definitely would’ve reconsidered a new build or the builder if I had known how difficult the warranty process would be.

title: The product didn't live up to the hype
review_text: Poor workmanship. Many things were rushed, and it shows.

title: QA/QC?
review_text: Where was the quality control? We have had nothing but MEP issues since we moved in.

title: Poor quality of work
review_text: Quality of work is very poor. The flooring is uneven in several places with hallow space underneath LVP planks , Huge gaps at cabinet alignments, leaky faucets and so on.

title: Poor craftsmanship
review_text: Customer service has not paid attention to detailed request. Disorganized. Perhaps volume of repairs & poor craftsman gas made job more difficult to keep up with it’s understandable nothing would be perfect but I’ve had to repeat my request multiple times to clarify previous concerns. Most alarming leaky sink.

title: Shea Homes - Everyone is responsive and will answer you in a short time.
review_text: Everyone is responsive and will answer you in a short time. But after 5 months we still are waiting for repairs to our fireplace and stucco. The bathroom shower is dripping, plants that were supposed to be replaced 4 months ago still haven’t been replaced. Bathroom hardware fell off the wall and the front yard has sunk along the gas line from the rain. It was explained to me when this happened at a Taylor Morrison neighborhood that it was because of improper packing of the soil when the gas lines were installed. We waited for our range and hood due to shipping issues, which weunderstood. They came in October and the hood was damaged. review_text: The overall homebuying experience was long and a little convoluted, which may have been in part due to the development being new and things still being worked out. An example is that I was told that I would have a backyard sprinkler station installed for me to use while looking at the model homes, but then told after I started the buying process that it was not part of the offering. I was also not too impressed with the Shea Mortgage folks and the amount of paperwork I had to continually update and resubmit, seemed very duplicative and time consuming. For a track home, the quality ispretty good, The most disappointing was the cracks in my garage cement, which I get will happen as the house ages, but it hasnt been that long. I pointed it out during the delivery and was told that the cracks were within tolerance and did not have to be fixed. The rest of the issues overall have been minor, with some electrical switches and fans not working properly. I do get the impression that they do want to fix things and make sure all is working properly, which helps with the trustworthiness and responsiveness scores. The overall value for the price paid is pretty good in comparison to other homes for sale in the area.

We never had a clear pricinglist of the upgrades. e.g. arrived at AZ Tile to pick out our kitchen counter tops all to realize from AZ Tile there was an approved Shea list. Who knew!? Everyone seemed to have an assistant - so you'd get an email out of the blue stating 'sign this'. Why? Who are you? Full quarterly HOA fees were never fully explained - I will say in writing because I have zero record of it. Again - LOVE the end result. Just be ready to be very attentive along the way . . . and ask over and over again "what should I expect next?" "What should I do next?".

title: New build with things to desire
review_text: Our sales rep was awesome. Attentive, kind, helpful the whole way through. The home itself is nice, however not as thought out as one would hope. A 5 bed 3 bath home should not have a 2 car garage. It means bigger family size but they made the 4 bedroom homes with 3 car???? Most people now a days want a 3 car garage minimum. Every house should have had 3 car garage options. Big draw back. The materials used are very basic. The wall paint is cheap, damages at the slightest touch, not living friendly and chips at the slightest touch despite paying for upgrade in paint choice and being quitecareful. Upgraded features like doors are so extremely cheap despite the mark up price. They are very hollow and offer zero privacy when closed. Upgrade in price and size should come with better quality and insulation and soundproofing. These have none. The noise heard when walking upstairs is almost unbearable downstairs. There is so much vibration and loud thudding even with someone barely walking. Poorly crafted. The construction process is way too fast for quality. They are focused on pushing out new builds yet no one carefully ENOUGH fine tuned the finishing details in the home. Attention would have shown their care and precision. While we understand its a man made product and subject to discrepancies, these could have been made correct had someone given a careful review. Its like they take their vendors and builders for gold. There should still be a quality control check and that was lacking in several areas within our new home. The neighborhood itself is crowded and with tight lots. A shame for such a beautiful area. Need more space between homes which obviously cant happen now. Uncomfortably close on a noise level and for room windows that face one another. Poorly thought out in that regard. And lastly, a massive HOA shared wall with our property was painted on the interior portion of our home with no prior notification! It felt like a tacky, unnecessary, and disappointing move on Shea’s part just before our closing. There was no need to alter the wall, it was decorative and high end looking than what they painted it to. This left a very bad taste and really dampened our happiness about the property. There was no fair reasoning given as to why they painted it. Our lot choice and property buy was based on this wall to begin with and it was a blow to have them paint it unbeknownst to us. They had been keeping us informed of everything but never this. It spoiled the whole sale and thrill of moving in just days before. They completely turned the wall ugly. It had specific speckling and decorative features to the wall that are still present on the exterior of it but on the inside section that lines our backyard they painted it an ugly clay brown which does not match the other retaining walls and had no purpose in being done. Highly upsetting occurence for us. And still troubles us to this day. We built this home on a whim from out of state. We wish we could have known more about what was to come with regard to the finished product. We are content for now but there was certainly room for improvement which is unfortunate because its such a new community.

title: Shea Homes Nevada St
review_text: I want to share my experience with Shea Homes, highlighting both the positive aspects and the areas where communication and transparency could be significantly improved. From the beginning, I encountered challenges in selecting a floor plan. One of the most frustrating aspects was the lack of explanation as to why a balcony was not included in the design for the location I had chosen. I specifically wanted a balcony, but it wasn’t offered—and this important detail was never clearly communicated during the selection process. Another major concern arose when I discovered a $6,000 chargefor a three-panel sliding glass door I did not request. It was automatically included in the home’s price, despite the absence of a balcony. Paying for an upgrade that serves no functional purpose felt unnecessary and unjustified, and again, there was no prior discussion or approval from my end. During the first walk-through, I identified numerous construction issues. Fortunately, the builder addressed them promptly, and I want to commend Vince, the superintendent, for his responsiveness and professionalism. However, the final walk-through was poorly communicated. I was not clearly informed about the timing or process, which left me confused and unprepared. After moving in, I was disappointed to discover the water in this area is extremely hard. I later learned—on my own—that Shea Homes offers a water softener installation during the building phase. Unfortunately, I was never informed of this option. Had I known, I would have absolutely included it, as hard water has a long-term impact on plumbing, appliances, and daily comfort. This lack of proactive communication resulted in a missed opportunity that continues to affect my home experience. Additionally, the post-move-in requirements from the Homeowners Association (HOA) added an unexpected layer of stress. I found it odd and somewhat unreasonable that homeowners are required to complete their yard landscaping within six months, yet the HOA’s own design approval process takes more than four weeks. On top of the delay, we were required to pay an additional fee for architectural design approval. The communication around this process was not smooth or supportive—it came across as demanding, without offering clear guidance or flexibility. I believe this process could have been handled more collaboratively to reduce unnecessary pressure on new homeowners. On a more positive note, I want to highlight the outstanding support I received from Mr. Will Duke, the warranty representative. He went above and beyond to resolve several post-move-in issues, including improperly installed bathroom drainage, water heater concerns, and electronic problems. His responsiveness, professionalism, and genuine concern for my satisfaction made a meaningful difference. I truly commend him for his dedication and customer care. Overall, while there were positive elements to my experience, including some helpful individuals along the way, I feel that several important details were either omitted or poorly communicated throughout the process. The lack of transparency around the balcony design, sliding door charge, water softener installation, and HOA landscaping requirements significantly impacted my overall satisfaction. I hope Shea Homes and its partners will take this feedback seriously and improve the level of transparency, communication, and homeowner support moving forward.

title: Good quality home, but could use a few minor improvements that would make it better
review_text: Builder is a bit inflexible, "this is how we build it, no changes allowed" Understandable, I suppose, but not especially customer focused. The home is advertised to be energy efficient but really only meets minimum building code standards for insulation. HVAC unit and hot water heater are not energy efficient. Builder cheapened out on several items that I would have preferred to be able to upgrade. Lighting fixtures in closet and laundry are ugly tube flourescent that are fine for the garage; Fancy iDevice switches are nice but if they are installed to control dimmable bulbs, they should haveused the version that supports them. Garage door opener controller should have been provided with the motion sensor version to turn on the lights automatically when entering. No landscape fabric in the garden area, so weed popping up. Lots of small details were simply overlooked by providing the cheapest options. Cracks in the garage concrete floor they would not repair even before closing. Light switches for the flex room are not in convenient locations, neither is the switch when leaving the master bedroom hallway into the great room, hard to find them in a dark room. Maybe not a builder problem, but I wish they would have worked closer with the HOA. There was no internet when closing, in fact it was not even arranged to be hooked. In general, quality is good, Some trades were better than others.

title: What started with an awesome experience is turning out to be somewhat subpar
review_text: Maybe I had higher hopes, but the quality of the workmanship and materials wasn't quite what I had expected. Stains on the carpet, misaligned/unaligned wall openings, and a broken appliance that was supposed to be fixed over a month ago. I still trust that all my issues will be resolved since the customer service rep is very responsive. But the wait is way more than I had expected from a builder like Shea.

title: Some build and window issues
review_text: There is a lack of attention to details, such as drywall, trim and paint application. The build process is rushed and can show in the finished product. Also the windows that are used are of a poor quality.

title: The Grove at Pomelo
review_text: All in all, Shea was great. The only negative aspect is the White Gloves Service was very poor in that they did not take enough time to explain all the smart home apps and how, to use them. Very Poor.

title: Not the greatest
review_text: Design center and landscaping were truly both lacking!

title: Nabeel home buying experience
review_text: I would recommend Shea Homes to others based on the construction and the construction manager Randy. I would warn them about Shea Homes sales team, people like Rachelle and her manager as they gave me one of the worst experiences of my life. Also I would warn them of some of the sub contractors like Home Pro who where anything but pro.

title: Workmanship is questionable
review_text: Water leak was found after close. Bathroom light was unable to turnoff and some other minor issues. Quality control seems to be substandard before closing.

title: Disappointed with our fixer upper
review_text: We were super excited to be building a brand new home. After choosing many options and getting the grand total of almost $700,000, we thought that things would be perfect. They were far from being perfect.
