We have crooked walls, crooked tiles, uneven flooring, and many other things that we were told that there is not enough workers and trained people to do quality work. Now that we are moved in, we just heard that they are slowing down the process and focusing more on quality. That would be great if they could refund us about $100,000.

title: “God is in the details” ~ Eero Saarinen
review_text: Home plans are fantastic. Amenity center is top notch. Quality workmanship in homes is sorely lacking in finishing details. Restaurant atmosphere is lovely but the menu needs work. It should reflect not only the local area but also speak to the diversity of the community. The dirty paper menus were a bit of a turnoff. Shows a lack of pride in presentation.

title: The wait was worth it, but.
review_text: We had a 13 month wait for our new home. And now we wait to have some minor repairs that we are waiting to have fixed. After the one month walk Thur we are waiting for over a month or two to have things fixed that need to be done. But the wait is worth it, we love the community. It would just be nice if Shea would fix things in a timely manner.

title: Our first new home - be wary!!!
review_text: We came across the Dunes several times and it was a decision between The Dunes and Sea Haven. Between The Dunes and Sea Haven, Sea Haven offered more details in their constructions and the price was comparatively the same. We finally decided on The Dunes due to several things: 1) Closeness to the ocean, 2) Lower HOA cost, 3) What you see is what you get (on the model homes as we were told) so we did not have to worry to much on the final cost of the house, 4) We were given the impression that we could customize our house as it was being built (not true!!!). Unfortunately the only thing thatwas true was the closeness to the ocean. The HOA turned out to be more expensive, no customization was allowed (looks like we missed the part of AS-IS). We could not even get an extra outlet installed during framing (completely doable). We were even willing to pay for it, but no. Overall, I would greatly reconsider if I had to redo it again.

title: Some what ok and soso
review_text: It took too long to receive the unit, if shea home had told us estimated waiting period we would’ve made better decision

title: The build process was long, complicated and delayed
review_text: Too many unrealistic reasons to explain an 18 month build cycle. However, management responded well when faced with a rate lock expiration deadline to complete the build on a specific date.

title: My Shea home
review_text: The build took quite awhile, shortages, labor etc. Overall my project manager kept me up to date with pictures and progress reports weekly. From design to move was about 15 months. I am very satisfied with the build. I think Shea uses quality products and good workmanship.

title: House quality was good, but building completion was delayed.
review_text: The house quality was good. Also, the builder's representative whose name was Randy was very responsible. He was great! But we were not happy for the delay to build the house. It delayed almost half year for completion to lead to our mortgage loan rate to be higher, which means that we need to pay more money for the mortgage loan.

title: Need better follow up on issues
review_text: Make sure customers don’t order the washer and dryer set. It’s a joke doesn’t work wrong product

title: Shea Home Buying Experience Townsend
review_text: The home itself is good. The 1-3-5-11 check-in plan is a good idea and has been helpful. Tami who helps with the move-in process and the periodic check-up is fantastic as is Eddie (maybe Freddie, sorry) who seems to be one of the lead contractors. They are both amazingly responsive and seem to truly care. Kveta from the design studio was very helpful and kind as well. We never felt rushed. She listened to our wants and needs and helped us to come up with a design that looked great and functionally has been fantastic as well. She allowed us to maximize our upgrade budget and we are veryhappy. She communicated with us throughout the process. We even had a question after moving in that she helped to answer for us. What was not good was the community representative, Kathy, and unfortunately, she is the one that we had the most contact with. I would not recommend her and wish there had been another representative we could have switched to (if this had been an opportunity, I would have switched after the first or second interaction). She walked us through the community on our initial visit. As someone who is trying to sell me on something and trying to represent a large brand and a community, I would’ve expected her to be somewhat upbeat and pleasant (not over the top, just a little bit, even a smile). She was not. There was no enthusiasm or excitement. If the market wasn’t as crazy as it was at the time, she would not have had a sale through us. I hope that Shea does not rest on the same laurels, that they can do and act however they want because the housing market is competitive. Lack of excitement is not enough of a reason for me to write a negative review about a person or company and as you will read, this was the least negative thing about the experience. She was very unresponsive regardless of the request, but if she sent something to us, expected a return e-mail within the day. For example, I had to e-mail her multiple times the month prior to moving in. I wanted to get the information for the utilities I would need to setup, I wanted to know when I should start doing all of that as well. I never got a response even after multiple e-mails. She eventually e-mailed my wife separately and mentioned that we would get that information closer to the move-in date and not to set anything up for the time being. She could have sent that simple e-mail to me after the first time. Instead, I now have the impression that she either doesn’t care, doesn’t know, or doesn’t care enough to find out the answer for me. When the roles were reversed and she would e-mail us with requests we always attempted to respond ASAP and send her whatever she requested. I truly believe that in nearly every case we responded to her with what she needed within the same business day. If we didn’t have what she needed, we explained how and when we would get it for her. The one time we did not respond to her within 24 hours regarding a question that was actually directed at our financial institution, she sent a follow-up e-mail with our loan officer attached saying that the failure to respond had meant that we were in default or about to be placed immediately into default. This was less than 48 hours after the first e-mail. While I understand there was information she needed to get from us, and mind you this was one or two months prior to move-in not our initial loan info/pre-approval or anything like that, threatening us with default after not immediately responding to one e-mail is absolutely inappropriate and unprofessional. We had made all of our payments/deposits on the first day it was available to do so. A little bit of good faith would’ve been appreciated before jumping the gun and going to such drastic measures. One of my largest concerns was when there was actually a potentially emergent situation at the home prior to move-in. We had a small leak in our house literally the week before moving in. The leak was noted on a Wednesday or Thursday, we were not notified until Saturday (mind you our move-in date was the following Friday). Why we were not notified immediately is still beyond me and I still never received a satisfactory answer. When we were contacted by Kathy she was exceptionally brief on the phone. She gave little to no details and told us to meet her at the house the following day. We were left with the impression that our entire home was flooded and we'd have a delayed move in date. We were told we should contact our apartment complex ASAP to try and revoke our written move out date and to try to extend our lease. Fortunately, when we did arrive to the home, the leak was relatively small and had nearly been resolved at that point. There was a good plan in place from the construction crew in terms of identifying the leak, checking adjacent piping, drying drywall, and checking additional moisture levels. The crew had been actively working on the problem since its onset. Had Kathy, spoken with any of them, I feel that she could have communicated that information over the phone and at least taken some of our worry away. Instead, she left us confused, scared, and unsure of what to do. After talking with fellow neighbors, we understand that at least two other homes in our motorcourt also suffered leaks prior to move-in. The way Kathy interacted with them was, from what I am told, was exactly the same and some of them had significantly worse leaks. Honestly, if there were only very limited interactions with Kathy, I would not have written this review. However, as she is the community representative and main point of contact throughout the whole process I felt this information to be pertinent. I like my home and community thus far, it’s just too bad that Kathy put such a damper on the whole process. As of now, I would buy from Shea again. But, if Kathy were the only community representative where I was looking, that would cause me to pass solely because of her involvement.

title: Shea Homes - Friendly and Engaging Staff, but Overall Workmanship Needs Improving
review_text: Recently I took delivery of a new home build by Shea. The design fit my needs and the home is nicely laid out. After some annoying delays with the city power, I tool delivery in the fall of 2022. On the whole the home turned out well, but there were areas of quality control that got missed or were ignored. I encountered some poor stain work and finishing on the cabinets, damage to appliances, interior and exterior paint and finishing issues and some electrical issues. The repair service is coming along and some items, I feel, will be pushed to the side. The sales and construction staffhave been friendly and fantastic, but the care in the workmanship is less than stellar.

title: Purchase process was good.
review_text: Three items: 1. Our experience to date with SheaConnect has not been pleasant. The WiFi shut down, taking the applications, plus our printer and the WiFi functions on my cell phone with it. 2. We can not add a security front door to our home because there is a beam on the ceiling that prevents a door from opening outward. I consider this a design flaw. 3. There is no electrical outlet on or in the area of the front porch. The steps are shaded and we need electric pads to melt snow and ice on the steps. Another flaw not obvious until winter arrives.

title: Shea home buying experience
review_text: I am going to begin with the positives. I am very pleased with the results of our home. I was very pleased with the responsiveness of the construction manager, Ryan. Ryan was also dedicated; however I feel the crew’s were spread too thin with over 30 new builds going at the same time. This resulted in multiple details being overlooked. Our punch list at the time of our walkthrough prior to closing was extensive with more than a dozen items. The most serious items were three separate previously undetected gas leaks. The connection to the swimming pool heater was not connected properly. Therewas leaking gas from the cooktop burners even when they were turned off. There was also later found a gas leak from the fireplace where a flexible pipe was mistakenly used instead of hardline pipe. The flexible pipe eroded where it was bent. This one was discovered 2 days after we moved in and could have been deadly. I give kudos to Ryan for staying with us at the house for six hours while the problem was repaired and the gas company could return to turn the gas back on and retest for further leaks. It has been over 2 months since we had our closing and we still have 4 unrepaired items from the punch list. These include a cracked window, an area of drywall in garage, garbage disposal dysfunction and an area of grouting. This seems excessive in its delay. I am very disappointed with the landscaping bushes. Other older areas of Shea homes in our development have much more extensive and colorful bushes. Lastly, it is my opinion that Shea’s policy of not providing visual documentation of the building process is outrageous. This is the fourth home I have built with different builders and this is the first time I’ve not been allowed to visualize the progress. I adamantly oppose this policy.

title: A mixed bag
review_text: The sales team was outstanding, and structurally, we love the house. The floor plan works well for us, and the house itself is well built. But the quality and condition of the appliances is another story. The dishwasher and hot water heater were defective when installed and were not repaired promptly. The kitchen faucet turned on by itself, wasting considerable water. These things shouldn't be happening in an expensive, brand-new house.

title: Good quality built home, but lacked in communication and timelines in completing the home.
review_text: Overall we are pleased with the price for the quality. We did not like the lack of communication and taking sometimes 2 weeks to get a response to a question. We also only got updates every 2 weeks by email, no calls, no customer service in that regard. Most emails were along the lines of "hey we got your framing done, next is air conditioning" with no timeline, no response to my question on when the hvac crew would be there. They never confirmed anything related to the schedule until it was already completed. I spoke with their Director of Construction Shawn and he was short, wouldn't offerany information on when they were scheduling tasks to be completed and essentially laughed when I asked for a concession because we were told at contract 7-9 months when it took 12 months and 9 days from contract to closing. At the end, we are paying $700 more a month because they could not complete the home in 9 months or less due to interest rates. I have spent 20 years in the residential construction and home building business and this was not the norm even with the supply chain and labor struggles in the market. The super that finished our home didn't complete 75% of the list we made 8 days before closing. On the final sign off the day before closing we noted most of those items and the painters had shown up as we were leaving. Not the way to do business and we spent most of the 12 months frustrated with Shea Homes and there just wasn't much we could do about it. Here we are 2 months post closing and we still are waiting another week to get the items that were supposed to be done prior to closing completed.

title: SanTan Build 2020-2022
review_text: I've waited until now to complete this review, as we've been away since September, after officially closing on July 4th 2022. We began the process in 2020-2021 and experienced many delays, thru the pandemic, lack of supplies, labor, etc... For the most part the process has been adequate, having never built a home before, I don't have a reference. I with the Design Day had included the exterior of our Resort Model, as there were a few structural things that weren't discussed and weren't done when we took possession last June. We're still waiting on those to be complete. We are ready to comeback in January, I hope they will be done by then. It's also already time for our 5-6 month Inspection. I feel these things could have easily been accomplished before now. Looking forward to working out a few of these details.

title: Wish we could give five stars
review_text: We looked for a couple years before deciding on an Encanterra home- unfortunately our timing gave us a lottery as are only option for a home. We won our pick and were so excited except it took 7 mo longer to build than expected. Our salesman was the best and so patient with the time, questions and our needs along the way. Unfortunately, we have had many issues with our new build that need attention. Our pergola is the wrong size, our oven broke, our garage door opens randomly and the list goes on. I think there was a rush to finish a lot of the new buildings, so quality and dependabilitysuffered. Our building coordinator has had her hands full with all of our issues- and has been great and efficient attending to our needs- but had the house been built properly, she wouldn’t have to back track on so many issues. Thankfully, we have a year warranty- so that gives some piece of mind to what’s been very frustrating and unexpected

title: Fay
review_text: The builder used cheep stuff

title: Mixed experience
review_text: Very mixed experience while building this house with Shea Homes. Sales people were good, but we had a pretty bad experience with the design studio. We had a great experience with the construction manager but have struggled post close with warranty items. The master planner of this community has also done a horrible job with community area, parks, and pools.

title: Expected more from the Shea name
review_text: We expected a lot more from Shea Homes, especially in quality of workmanship. The COVID excuse needs to stop, yes supply chain issues still exist but quality of workmanship should still be priority one. Everything was going great right up until 2 months prior to closing when oversight of final build went out the window, When we did a walk through and blue taped everything, hardly anything had been fixed when we closed and moved in, it literally looked like they just went in an removed all the taped items and called it good. The house was filthy with paint and paint can marks on the LVPflooring, dirt on floors and walls etc. We were shocked at how dirty the place felt. In fairness, Shea has been outstanding with our concerns, complaints and trying to make things right, but it has been a tremendous amount of work on our part to schedule all of these trades to come fix everything.

title: Disappointed home owner
review_text: The sales team is very disorganized and not very responsive. Home prep work before delivery was done at the eleventh hour. Production workmanship is subpar. Warrenty workmandhip is very good but inconvenient because of schedule and waiting for work to be completed; it should have been done correctly before home delivery. Rob Shay is outstanding as a warrenty specialist.

title: Contractor liason was very helpful.
review_text: I am happy with my home, but knowing it is over-priced for comparable other commuities, I cannot endorse Trilogy. The pre-sheetrock walk-through revealed very poor beams were used. Beams my husband would have burnt before using them to support a house. Light switches don't make a lot of sense where they are set. Two require walking across the room to turn them off. One room has 2 switches for the same light 3 feet apart. Quality of carpet choices was disappointing. the one we chose unravels at the entry way, and shows even a piece of thread! Garage needs more outlets to charge powertools. Driveway is too narrow for 2 cars. Guest cars scrape the gutter when backing out because it is so steep. Even one less structure per block would allow more space between houses, thus more privacy. That would at least make me feel less like I'm in an expensive condo. Your Warranty Team has corrected a number of items missed at the final walk-through.

title: Nhome
review_text: First time buying new home build , it was new experience , but you could have your sales people more clear in their instructions or information on what to do. But at the end I am happy with m house, thank you

title: The Grove, Camarillo, California
review_text: There were a few "bumps" in the road before the home closure. Overall, however, it was a good experience. The salespeople were friendly and accommodating. That said, we have had a number of issues that needed to be addressed and, as of now, nearly six months later, some of those issues are still outstanding. Not everything was finished before we took ownership of the property in August of 2022. We understand we are not the only new homeowners in this development, but the response times are not always acceptable. In addition, there are times we have not been notified when the individualsare coming to our home to address the issues, especially issues that are on the outside of the home. We would prefer to be at home when they come to fix issues that have been noted by us so that we can discuss the issues with the crafts-person when he or she is there . As of today, there are two primary issues that remain outstanding and need to addressed: (1) A dented refrigerator door that needs to be replaced, and (2) work that needs to be done/finished on decorative tiles in a front view window.

title: Agent and engineers and other staff were nice.
review_text: Quality of kitchen cabinets not good, drawers wiggles . K Kraft or J kraft came to check and said that’s how it is. Doors not installed well. Floorings in the second floor noisy when someone is walking. I think for a new house not supposed to be like that. was address when we reported it but still the same noisy floor. It’s weird and not good for a new house.

title: Shea Home
review_text: Shea is a good homebuilder and their staff are hard workers. I wish they will consider to keep the sink in the big laundry room (not even an options for buyers).

title: Home Buying, Post-Pandemic
review_text: We found after we built an ocean-front house in Oregon in 2005, that building in a 55+ community in 2022 was a lot different. First, we were asked to make a purchase of a home that we had never seen, and that delayed us from being in on the start. Once we solved that, we did buy because we liked what the community offered: people our age all living in the same place. However, because we were moving to Las Vegas, we had to schedule a lot of things and with not being able to ascertain a move-in date, it made it that much harder for us to schedule our move-in. Once we got in, we found that notbeing able to select interior things, floors, carpets, appliances that we liked, e.g. we had cherry floors and cabinetry in our old home and we were not able to get them in our new home. Once we got in, we found that some of the construction work was not to the standard we were led to believe, and it has taken a lot of time out of our unpacking and setting up a new home to deal with those issues. We have been in the house now for 1 1/2 months, still not all unpacked, but we are seeing that we can make this work. The fact that this is a 55+ community, it means we have friends that are our same ages, and that is a plus. The last temporary home we had in Colorado, the community was a mix of everything you could possibly not want for neighbors and that made Trilogy a shining star for us. Even with the 110-degree temperatures this summer, we acclimated to the area without a lot of stress. I think one of the most helpful things was the support of Trilogy staff. They answered all of our questions - sometimes not with the answer we had hoped for - but they answered us and gave us explanations. For us, that was essential. I think the worst thing to happen to us is that we discovered that Sunstone is not located on 3+ acres of ocean-front and we have realized how much we miss that setting. However, we now have more new friends and more new places to explore. I think the best thing that has happened is that we have more doors open to us to do the things that we enjoy...and I would suspect as we get to know the area better, we will find more. Our move was a good move and Trilogy fits most of the things in our life that we need.

title: Shea needs work to be exceptional
review_text: Shea is already above the rest of the builders in Colorado. However, they are not fulling living up to their perceived reputation. Communication, communication, communication is big in the world between customers and the business. It is just as important internally in a business. Shea has a lot of work to do with communication. Lastly, I would say Shea could do a regular audit of their employees’ job performances. Of the handful of employees I interacted with, there were a couple that had competency issues with job duties. Aside from the previous faults, I do like my house and quality within.

title: Shea Homes - Overall good experience until the final walkthrough.
review_text: Overall good experience until the final walkthrough. None of the punch list items had been taken care of so we refused to close escrow until they were. Delayed closing by 5 days. Never received refrigerator which we paid for so had to get refund for that. There was a disconnect between sales, construction, & customer service. No one seemed to be on the same page. We were a previous Shea customer in Trilogy community and the process with that transacton was much more seamless & better coordinated. It is production construction so they are pushing out homes as fast as possible so I guess thatexplains some of the disconnect.

title: Shea Homes - The process was a bit confusing and some areas were rushed but
review_text: The process was a bit confusing and some areas were rushed but overall we are happy to be in our new home. The warranty period has been good, having fixed the issues pretty quickly. Having the neighborhood gatherings sponsored by Shea was a nice touch to bring us all together and foster a community, not just people who live next to each other.

title: Joe
review_text: Not very happy with getting warranty work completed in a timely manner. Issues with cabinets, driveway pavers, stucco coloring and other smaller but just as important issues. It took several conversations to resolve some of these listed problems but still have most UNCOMPLETED! We moved in late June...It seems like passing the buck is a staple response.

title: Sloppy construction and workmanship - delivered home with *many* items in need of repair.
review_text: How do I feel about the entire process, from initial purchase to final move-in? Honestly I do not feel good about it :/ In general and overarchingly I feel blessed to be in a position to buy a new home so I don’t want to rain on that parade. *But* I’ve had dust-ups with builders/management at Pre-Drywall, Landscaping and Warranty; pushback from builders/management about not wanting to fix things or not admitting that things needed to be fixed - only to have builders/management a few days later relent, fixing most, but not all items. Altho many, but again not all, items were fixed, theprocess each time, left me stressed, exhausted and disillusioned. How would you describe the quality and value of your new home? Mixed. Some materials/construction appear top-notch, and some things appear to be low-end cost saving materials/construction. My front door, for which I paid for a premium elevation is a mess. A door guy from Focus came out to look at it and explained that first the door was (accidentally) painted blue and then without sanding or prepping the door was then painted yellow. He explained that paint does not stick to paint. So at delivery the door was cracked, chipping and the blue paint showing thru and there are drip marks at door well/frame - all this due to *not* sanding or prepping the door before repainting. Focus door guy could not fix because he is not from painter trades, but he fully understood the problem. It’s aMaZiNg that I paid extra for all that. This is just one example of the sloppy workmanship I have encountered in my new home. How was your experience with the builder’s team? The builder’s team; as I mentioned above, aLoT of pushback from builders and management, then the reversal, the 180 - so aLoT of unnecessary stress for me, the homebuyer. Did they do what they promised? Did they respond to you in a timely fashion? Pls see above. What would you most like potential home buyers to know about this builder? I would like potential home buyers to know that this builder, Shea Trilogy, builds an amazingly positive and wonderful product for the Active Adult Living lifestyle. But, I have to say that, at this juncture, Shea is not living up to it’s reputation as a supreme builder. I understand issues of demand, supply chain and labor shortages - but none of that accounts for the pushback behavior I’ve experienced and, for the most part, no verbal apology for such behavior.

title: Good plans and options, but not great at open communication.
review_text: The design portion of the home building process with Shea was great. However, the construction portion lacked in terms of communication and willingness to correct mistakes.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - No problem with build.
review_text: No problem with build. Although prices are at a premium, we were disappointed in how small and crowded the lots in Sunstone felt after the build. We have a corner lot that is surrounded by an extremely ugly brown cinder block wall which is all that can be seen in the backyard. Apparently ,nothing can be placed against it to block this unfortunate view and we have tried with our backyard landscaping which still remains unapproved after numerous follow ups since we bought the house in May.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Problems, problems and more problems.
review_text: Problems, problems and more problems.

title: Shea Homes - Good experience during the whole process.
review_text: Good experience during the whole process. But I would make sure to get third party inspector for thorough inspection.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - The house is beautiful
review_text: The house is beautiful

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - The sales and design process were excellent.
review_text: The sales and design process were excellent. Shellie and Tedi were outstanding, kind, helpful and responsive. Once escrow closed things turned very quickly. We have been in our home for 60 days and punchlist items from prior to close have not yet been addressed. Several appointments with the Shea Team have been missed without a call or email. It took me over two weeks to find a person who would take accountability. Froy was not only NOT helpful but was also rude and disrespectful to my wife. He acted as a roadblock not a facilitator. Once we were connected with Jocelyn our issuesbegan to be addressed but we are behind and very frustrated. I would be happy to discuss this at length

title: Shea Homes - It was nice to be regularly updated on the build of the home
review_text: It was nice to be regularly updated on the build of the home via email and to be offered to view the home in multiple stages. However, at the same time if there are any questions or issues that arise there would be times that getting feedback would take some time. You can tell there is not enough ancillary support compared to the amount of homebuyers, so that individualized customer care can be properly given.

title: Shea Homes - No flexibility.
review_text: No flexibility. Many mistakes in the process

title: The unfortunate delays in construction
review_text: We very much enjoy living in Wickenburg Ranch. It took considerably more time to build the house than originally planned, which created a ton of challenges. We also had turnover of our building manager several times. There is still a missing component to the new home build. We also utilized the preferred landscaper of Shea homes and have some unresolved issues as well.

title: Shea Homes - House built very nicelyEquipment must be tested before final
review_text: House built very nicely Equipment must be tested before final delivery

title: Overall a Great Experience with one Hiccup
review_text: Overall my home buying experience was great. I love the home and community. Jacqui and Mei in the sales office were amazing, helpful and very responsive. They continued to update me as the home was being built on progress and would send photos of the house. If I asked them a question, they would always get me the answer in a timely matter. All of the on-site construction staff and management were great as well. I had a few meetings/walkthroughs with them and they explained everything to me in detail. I decided to hire my own private home inspector and I was so happy to hear that myinspection went well and only 1 minor item needed to be fixed. I did have one large hiccup that caused a huge issue and headache. We met with our designer Chrissy and the initial meeting went well. Went went over all our design selections and then were sent our contract to sign. A few months passed and we went to the house to view the progress. We immediately noticed that the grout in one downstairs bathroom shower and was incorrect. We had requested the same grout and tile pattern as our model home bathroom. This grout that was installed was the wrong color. After going back and forth with two different managers, not getting email responses for 3 weeks after I followed up and management refusing an in person meeting to discuss this issue, they offered to stain the grout the correct color. This whole design experience really turned me off with Shea Homes and my overall experience. I was just shocked to be refused an in person management meeting to discuss. In the end the grout was stained white and hopefully that will last well.

title: Great during sales,; not very good after move in responses.
review_text: The sales force was very good and responsive. After we moved in and through the 4 week walk through the response has been very poor. One person has the responsibility and he is overwhelmed. The communication from Shea Homes office where you submit an issues request is non-existent.

title: Generally good feedback on progress
review_text: Have only been in the house for 3 months and still have some quality issues to address. Most are addressed timely, but need to be patient with the labor challenges since Covid.

title: I thoroughly enjoyed working with David, the builder superintendent. He was very responsive to my questions and needs, even after I moved into my home.
review_text: All in all, it was a good experience, despite the pandemic and supply chain issues creating delays in the move-in date. One word of advice to new homeowners, be very critical and thorough during your final walk-through. It seems the workmanship was a bit 'sloppy' perhaps due to the extended delays and deadlines not being met on time. There were things that could have been done more carefully, had the workers taken the time to do them correctly the first time. As an example, the caulking wasn't completely done around my tub and faucet, which meant a plumber had to be brought in after Imoved into the house. The water wasn't turned on to the fridge and when I pointed it out during the walk-through, the sales person wrote it down but didn't follow through on the complaint. Thus, it took 2 weeks before I could get someone in to fix the issue (and it happened to be the builder superintendent I ended up calling, because Shea Customer Service never responded to my calls and emails).

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Rome, our customer service rep, has been very helpful in getting
review_text: Rome, our customer service rep, has been very helpful in getting proper trades out to fix numerous quality issues. Overall, disappointed in the attention to detail in construction.

title: Workmanship of trades and Quality of Materials is very lacking
review_text: Given the price point of the home and Shea’s reputation I would have expected much better workmanship from those various trades that did work on our home. The fit and finish on the home was really lacking. We have seen issue throughout our house with the attention to details including paint, tile work, cabinetry and windows to name a few. We know that trades do this work but there needs to be better oversight from Shea. We regards to materials it is definitely clear that the best materials weren’t used. From the faucets to the exhaust vents these weren’t the best quality or the mostappropriate for a home of this price. The kitchen exhaust vent didn’t have the correct damper and causes loud noise when it’s windy. The Moen fixtures feel very cheap and low quality. The toilets are below the quality we expected, they don’t even have soft close seats. This is minor but iemblematic of the lack of attention to detail that should be fitting of a house at this price point. When compared to our previous new construction with a different builder the materials are definitely not up to par. Another example of the lack of attention to details would be the HVAC system. For a house of this size there was no zoning. It immediately caused issues with cold and hot spots in the house. Even though the issue was corrected, I think there should have been planning to address this during design and construction. Overall given how these homes were positioned in the market and the price point the trades really disappointed with the quality of work. The materials and attention to details were extremely lacking. As a homeowner this puts the responsibility on our shoulders to try to work after move in the figure it out. Even though this is expected in any new home, the amount of issues that needed attention seems extraordinarily high.

title: Shea Homes-Trilogy - Workmen have not shown up when they were scheduled to.
review_text: Workmen have not shown up when they were scheduled to. Closed 12/31/21. I am still waiting for mirrors and shower doors on my master shower. It has been 9 weeks. Smart home visit was just completed yesterday. There appears to be a disconnect between contractors.

title: The tile work was the best craftsmanship in the entire home. Floors,walls, tub surround and shower look great.
review_text: Overall, we love our home and the neighborhood in Wickenburg Ranch. There were a number of issues regarding paint quality (recommend hiring your own painter) , drywall quality, door placement, leaky shower door, and exposed pipes on the patio. After 2 months of difficult discussions we were finally able to get Shea to resolve most of the problems.

title: Quality of the home is good but waiting time was very bad. I waited 1 year plus 1 year for construction
review_text: Quality of the home is good but minimum they need to give house for 1889 sqft for the price. On top, house rates shld be stable. Every 2 weeks 30 to 40k increase I did not like it. Communication is good Workers are good Work site manager is good

title: Sales team was great but the quality of the house was mediocre
review_text: Top notch service from the sales team (Anne Marie & Jim)! I would rate them 6 star if I could. But the quality of the house built, especially the workmanship was at an extremely low level. I'm a bit disappointed by Shea Homes on that part.

title: Frustrated
review_text: Once coverings started, so did poor workmanship. Holes in the sheathing, misaligned and damaged dry wall, poorly aligned trim. A lot of hacking, followed by hiding with plaster and RTV. Materials were above average, hence the rating of 3.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Nice home.
review_text: Nice home. Don’t respond to problems

title: We loved working with Jeannie and Lesley
review_text: It was a pleasant experience, however we have an awful brick wall all around our property that is a terrible eyesore and nothing has been done about this, we bought a premium lot with no one next to us and now we have no view whatsoever.

title: Once the property closed the care and follow up on problems was not good
review_text: Shea is a good builder. We've built two homes with them, However the Canyons home has sloppy workmanship. Many items were installed wrong, banged up, loose, missing, incomplete, not working etc

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Be aware that if you buy a “Designer” home, it’s nothing more
review_text: Be aware that if you buy a “Designer” home, it’s nothing more than a spec home with no extras. I’ve already spent $25k upgrading things…not happy about that.

title: Inflexibility
review_text: The builder has a an attitude of it is our way or go somewhere else. The communication is spotty and not always consistent among their employees. The house itself is okay but nothing special. I have worked with other builders and would rate Shea as mediocre at best.

title: Disappointed
review_text: House is fantastic. Used Shea to hire fence install; terrible height placement abs more than five requests to talk with me about it, zero response. You will love the house just watch for these types of potential issues.

title: Shea Homes - Sales people were very kind to concerns
review_text: Sales people were very kind to concerns

title: Need to be sure all appliances are working from day 1.
review_text: Please be sure to check all appliances, my oven hasn’t worked from day 1 to now day 60 something, difficult to get assistance thru GE and thru the builder, doing it on my own thru constant calling of GE. Also upgraded painting, cabinet work, etc. has flaws, mostly cosmetic, however, been told it was difficult to get the proper help. The builder has sent people to try to fix the flaws, however, I though I was finished with workers coming into my new home. Speaking to other new residents and they seem to have similar issues.

title: Second time not as good as the first
review_text: This is our second home at Encanterra. By far the first experience was much, much better than this time. Our construction manager - Mike Cama - was consistently good, but his management team above him were not supportive, often challenging and at times rude. The quality of the home is much worse than before. Craftsmanship is moderate and many items were simply “missed” or done “half-way”. A prime example is the A/C unit had almost no (zero) refrigerant in it yet passsed Shea’s “stringent quality check”. Wiring issues, more A/C issues and damaged tiles are just some examples of thepoorer quality of our new home compared to the one built in 2018. Our customer service rep is trying hard and we appreciate his effort, but he has a bigger task than what should be necessary.

title: We are very disappointed in what we got in the home because we were told we would have Air conditioning, we never got, also we were told we would have choices in upgrades we never got. I would not recommend Shea after they mislead us.
review_text: Be sure you know what you are getting

title: Delays in move in date.
review_text: Very well insulated. They fix any errors. Building too many homes at once, workmanship suffering.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - We love the community.
review_text: We love the community. Throughout the building process the communication could have been improved. As an example, we were given a closing date and then two days before the close were told it was not going to happen. Despite the fact that the Shea representative knew we were in a rental and had given notice. One issue that may contribute to communication issues is the staff turnover seems very high. Additionally while some of the subcontractors did stellar work others are not as diligent. At the end we are happy with our home despite some repair issues that are under way

title: Owner Explanation of Star Rating Given
review_text: This Trilogy division was built for 55 age and over owners who desire a simple down sized home with minimal problems. There seemed to be a disconnect between the on-site contractor (Construction Supv) promises and the follow up person handling identified owner tasks still needing to be fixed. It seems that, neighbors and I, feel the need to get down and nasty for anything positive to happen. IE; The top surface of covered porch was 12" (step) above the ground surface which caused me to fall during construction walk-thru. The contractor (Supervisor) measured the step and told us that aconcrete step all across the front would be provided before we took posession. During the final walk-thru there was no promised step as dirt had been pushed up against the front of the raised porch reducing the drop in elevation from 1 foot to 7.5 to 8" step down and we're told that was within code. Does it seem reasonable that two 77 year old owners living in a 55 and over development would have to negotiate an 8" step daily to get to their back yard not to mention the builders commitment for a step to be added? Also, it should be noted that owners were not allowed on-site after hours or not and on weekends. We could not follow up on what was happening verses was not. Here is another example of problems being ignored. Putty work around outside vents, front door bell, and other sealing applications needed reworking. Instead of running caulk smoothly across a seam, it was applied in small bulges all along areas needing to be sealed. It looked like it was applied by someone very inexperianced or who did not care enough to do the job correctly. The answer we were given was "That is the way it was done on all the houses"(?). Is that an acceptable answer? I could go on and on but I think We have made our point. Last, when we were at our site before anything was finalized, the sales person told us that he could not decide whether our lot was a view lot or not. There were no houses as yet built around our lot and we did have a beautiful view of Mt Rainier in all its' majesty. A few days later we were notified that it was indeed a view lot so the price of the lot went from $5000 to $10,000. When all the houses around us were later added and competed we could not see Rainier even from the street. When I inquired I was given the message from sales, "He is crazy if he thinks we will give any money back." So the issue of whether the home was in fact a view lot, or not, was mute because by law I had already signed and was stuck with the misleading criteria for the cost of the lot included in the agreement. A poor example of what Shea considers fair and above board dealings with their customers. The only other issue was that the top soil was removed from the lots during construction and not put back. The remaining soil is gravel and rock. You cannot penatrate the remaining soil with a shovel. You need a pickax and luck to find an area between the rocks for your pickax or shovel to penatrate more than an inch.

title: Bad sales department
review_text: Do not trust the sales department

title: It seems to me they may be pushing too fast to get homes completed as we have had numerous issues that basically show poor attention to detail
review_text: While the overall quality of the home seems good, we have had numerous issues that to me demonstrates a lack of attention to detail before turning the house over to us as the new owners. For instance, dishwasher and refrigerator were not working properly (installation issues), front porch lights not working (even after first repair not working again), dryer vent duct on roof obstructed, toilet paper holder fell off, electrical conduit for TV (smurf tube) obstructed can't run cable through it, door from garage to house would not lock. While all these things are fixable, seems that if someonewould have taken to check these "little things" before turning over the house to us would have been an overall better experience for us.

title: Davie and Goliath
review_text: I feel like you were very anxious to pay off my realtor and get her out of the picture leaving me do negotiate with a large corporation without any real representation. Seemed like your attitude was pretty much - "well, we have six units left - take it or leave it." In other words not much accomodation. It's a little early to decide what kind of value we got. Some aspects of our new home I truly love. Others, well, seems like you cheaped out or the at least the jury is still out.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - I was frustrated with my experience because I relied on the models
review_text: I was frustrated with my experience because I relied on the models to represent (at least to the extent possible) what the features I selected would look like. In a couple of instances, the expectation and delivered product (both aesthetically and workmanship) were miles apart.

title: Shea Homes - Shea homes has been a positive experience for us.
review_text: Shea homes has been a positive experience for us. Every person on Shea team has been great to work with.

title: At times difficult to work with
review_text: The buying process was mostly good but at times we found it difficult to collaborate on issues or concerns and are still dealing with a couple of items. We're also very disappointed with the dual metro district situation and that added lifetime cost of ownership.

title: To busy catering to potential buyers
review_text: Once the ink was wet on the purchase agreement, Shea's entire attitude changed towards me. It's taken several months, but I believe we are back on track. Patrick has treated me fairly. Just too bad it took so long to get to this point. love the community, and the stunning vistas. My advice, less focus on the next home sale and more focus on the customer you just sold to.

title: Very awkward financing.
review_text: Seems to be higher quality than other builders.

title: The location and amenities in Trilogy
review_text: Pretty nice home and customer service overall is so far okay. Beware of what is standard and included in the price vs. what is considered an upgrade extra. They can add up to a premium price very quickly. Attention to detail in the fit and finish during construction could use a bit of improvement with closer quality control.

title: Miscommunications and quality control disappointment
review_text: There were several miscommunications with the Shea sales. It ultimately was resolved but it was a hassle That I had to fight and argue each time, Something that no one should go through during the purchase of a new own. So stressful. The quality control is not up to the level that I would expect. There were several things that needed to be replaced and fixed upon moving in. It’s like they are just slapping these homes together so fast and the quality is lacking.

title: The Experience
review_text: Quick review - The selection of options experience was terrible (didn't know the products, not helpful with selections). Follow through on making sure options on the home get installed was not good - forgot to put in insulation in key areas as an example - really need to keep an eye on the build process. Good news - after purchase support / follow through very good so it all eventually gets done. Still, home and options are very overpriced for the money...

title: They won’t budge on anything
review_text: I ultimately bought the place for the location and view...but Shea didn’t make it easy. It was a struggle every step of the way. I don’t know if that’s because I had already been working with a lender, and didn’t want to go with Shea Mortgage, (they wouldn’t even quote me ballpark rates), or because that’s just how they work. Getting responses, tracking down information, getting forms completed, it was always chasing after multiple people to get what we needed. Now that I am living in the space, I see a lot of workmanship issues, things that were just lazy/sloppy finishes, where I expectmore for the price I paid. Still working with them to take care of some of those issues. That side of customer service seems to be going ok.

title: Shea Homes - I like how the frame is built.
review_text: I like how the frame is built.

title: Design of homes and neighborhood make it very appealing.
review_text: Love the Ranch and layout, love the design of the homes, love the friendliness of the community. Disappointed in the quality of our home. One month punch list has 38 items to be fixed/corrected and growing daily. Just yesterday we both went into the bedroom and closed the door; couldn’t get out. Used the patio door and electric door to get back in.

title: Meeting deadlines
review_text: Be sure to be comfortable with the limited flexibility offered in the design stage of the process, and the complexity of the "smart" option.

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - Struggled somewhat with the sales team but am overall satisfied
review_text: Struggled somewhat with the sales team but am overall satisfied now that I've moved in

title: Sloppy
review_text: A lot of sloppy work. Paint spots on floors. Crooked stove vent. Countertops installation half-assed. Multiple cabinet scratches. Cheap toilets.

title: Great sales and post sales experience. Design process is terrible
review_text: I loved working with the sales team before we purchased the property. And post sales was great as well. The final product is nice. But the design process was bad. Response was slow and when we got a hold of the designer, it felt like she did not care to really help us. And got to a point where she was not responding to our requests. Kind of like seeing a surgeon who is ready to punch the clock. And I mean we are spending a ton of money to buy the house, at least act like you care.

title: Good Communication
review_text: Good communication and follow up by sales staff, construction supervisors, and warranty rep. Very professional. Homes are very close together, Design Center choices somewhat limited. Quality of workmanship and attention to detail by subcontractors is lacking, especially by painters., GE appliances have many issues, and GE has horrible customer service for warranty repairs.

title: Shea Homes - many things that were not complete at move in, had to deal with
review_text: many things that were not complete at move in, had to deal with workers coming to fix things for the past 4 months

title: Shea Homes - Trilogy - I had a great field manager who took care of issues promptly.
review_text: I had a great field manager who took care of issues promptly.

title: Disappointing Post-Purchase Experience Despite Strong Community Appeal
review_text: I've been in my Shea Homes property for two months, and unfortunately, the experience has fallen well short of expectations. Staff turnover has been significant and disruptive: the original sales representative is gone, the initial lending agent is no longer with the company, and the HOA contact has also changed. This constant churn has made continuity and accountability feel nonexistent. The warranty software/system was switched mid-process, and the transition was far from smooth—leading to delays, confusion, and repeated follow-ups on my end just to get basic issuesaddressed. Communication between phases (sales → construction → warranty → HOA/community management) has been consistently poor. Information often gets lost, responses are slow, and it feels like each department operates in silos rather than as a cohesive team supporting the homeowner. While the build quality isn't outright disastrous, several aspects of the home's finish and materials have not met the level I expected from a premium builder like Shea. Issues that should have been caught during construction or early warranty periods persist or have been inadequately resolved. Shea markets itself as delivering exceptional communities, and I will acknowledge that the neighborhood amenities, layout, and overall vibe are indeed strong selling points—arguably unmatched in the area. That said, when comparing value, other builders' homes on the market often appear to offer more square footage, better finishes, or stronger "bang for the buck" at similar price points. At this stage, I feel like I overpaid for a house where the community and support systems seem to work against the homeowner rather than for them. The promise of a seamless, high-quality experience hasn't materialized, and it's left me regretting the decision despite loving the location. I hope future improvements in staffing stability, communication, and warranty handling can turn things around for newer buyers.

title: RECEIVED NO RESPONSE TO MY PREVIOUS FEEDBACK
review_text: No response of any kind was provided after our last feedback. Please don't waste people's time.

title: Good Build Quality
review_text: With the exception of our outstanding Sales Rep, Amy, this home purchase experience was very stressful and frustrating. The production team lacked follow through and adequate communication. The Sales leadership is difficult to work with. Despite all of this, our warranty team is responsive, and we are pleased with the overall build quality of our home. We enjoy living in such an active community.

title: Michael
review_text: Sales office was fantastic. My review would have been much better however, the poor quality control of flooring company that Shea contracts with (Primera) and the unwillingness to correct the issues properly lead me to give the poor review. I am very disappointed that Shea isn’t taking ownership of the issue and making their subcontractors fix correctly and in a timely manner. My next step is to report to AZ ROC if this isn’t remedied soon.

title: My walk through with the builder was good. However, I did not do a final walk through because I was ill at that time.
review_text: I would have appreciated a more comprehensive explanation of exactly what items were going to be put into the house, For example, the tub in the second bathroom is not quality and some of the workmanship in the house is sloppy.

title: Poor build, great community.
review_text: The Trilogy Sunstone community is exceptional. Melba my salesperson, Denise in Design, Katie in sales, and Theresa in Warranty are all above and beyond. The reason for the poor review is that on the construction end of it, I am totally disappointed. Poor workmanship, horrible attitutude by the trades, sloppy workmanship with little or no care for a solid product. Lots of issues with the build. I would still recommend buying and living here, just expect a lot of frustration on the build end.

title: Smooth Home Purchase, but True Quality Revealed After Months of Living
review_text: Until you move in, you wouldn't realize that the home that you are getting is not the same that you expected or promised. There are descripencies. 1. Prices have been increased for all models and that's because of merket on inflation. However you won't get the same things that your neighbors might have got for same elevation and plans. 2. Garage walls are literally foam, while some models have stronger and smoother garage walls. If your kid kicks ball inside garage, they will break. You won't have access to garages in model homes and this will not be included anywhere in contract and indesign studio to chose what you want. 3. Laundry room is literally like apartment. In model homes, you will see cupboards, but you won't get that in home. This will not be discussed during design studio and you will not have a clue until you move in. 4. While handing over the keys, they won't listen to you and won't let you talk. They only bother to get rid of home. 5. Yes, they do offer 1 year warranty that is to cover cracks etc. May be builders are bounded by law to do this which I am unaware of. All they do is repaint things and hit nails. Ofcourse, some small electrical work. 6. Solar is another thing where you will not have more options. 3.5KW panels doesn't cover much and this is just because of california law they go with minimum capacity I think. I wish we should get more options here. Overall, it's not unpleasant experience and it's not pleasant either. You will get a home, but not good feel due to these small compromises.

title: Mail
review_text: That there is no mail delivery and you will have to drive to the post office to pick up your mail. No answers when we'll have delivery.

title: no names to enter here
review_text: it took 2 months to get basic things like the front doorbell working. sales said could park in driveway. This does not work and HOA fines us.

title: Message didn’t match actions
review_text: After going through the entire experience, we’ve realized very quickly that if it wasn’t for the sales staff, we would have walked away. It started with the design studio session, we were told to be prepared which meant going through Shea design studio software (via computer) which had a 3D model of our home and allowed us to select our materials (flooring/ countertops/ cabinets etc.). My wife and I spent 2 weekends going through software and designing what we felt was our perfect house, only to show up to our design studio (in person) session to find out our Shea representative didn’t evenreview prior to our appointment. Thankfully my wife brought her computer which we had to get out and open up the Shea design studio software to show the Shea representative our selections. After spending an hour or so going through the model we spent hours building, we were told that 90% of our selections are no longer available - Are you kidding me? The design studio session was a one time only, 4hr block, and we just wasted an hour or so of our 4hr block to learn all the pre-work we’ve done was completely useless. Since we had to start from scratch, I advised the Shea representative to get comfy because we will be there until closing time only to be told that we only had less then 3hrs to make our choices. I immediately requested to speak to a supervisor, after a few minutes of going back and fourth, the Shea representative called her supervisor and got approvals to spend and extra hour with us but also allow us to have a second 4hr session. This was great news, however the dog fight to get to that point was complete nonsense. To add to a day that was supposed to be fun, during our design studio session, the Shea representative thought it was ok to show us examples on her computer (flooring, countertops, cabinets) vs product examples I could see and touch in person. We were told by the Shea representative that she hurt her back and was moving slowly, which I sympathize, but I told her to show me where the product is and I will carry it all but without seeing it in person we’re not making any decisions. During the final walk through, my wife and I were talked down to and belittled from the construction superintendent and we were told countless times - ‘trust us, warranties team has a huge bucket of money and will take care of everything’. The issue with this, during our final walk through, which was specifically scheduled +2 weeks before final signing/ closing, it was explained to my wife and I that we are there to point out any inefficiencies and that we wanted to identify now so it can be fixed for the final signing/ closing. Well, during this walk, we were repeatedly told to not worry about inefficiencies we’ve identified and constantly told we’re being too picky and this isn’t a ‘custom home’. Fast forward 2 weeks to final signing/ closing and wouldn’t you know it, not 1 issue we identified was addressed. Not only were the issues not addressed, as I walked in I was immediately given an iPad to sign stating all issues have been resolved. I asked to walk the house prior to signing and was greeted with attitude by the field superintendent, which immediately changed my attitude. After reluctantly walking the house with me, I pointed out inefficiencies we had reviewed 2 weeks prior and asked why these weren’t taken care of, only to be given attitude and told ‘this is no longer a conversation, your to needy/ picky and we’re not fixing and this is warranties issue now’. WHY would you not want to address all and any issues prior to the home owner moving in??? After I move in, I don’t want you back as much as you don’t want to have to return to fix an issue that was clearly identified in a timely manner. During the entire build process, I can’t begin to count the amount of times we were told about the warranties team and their responsibility and that Shea wants a happy home buyer. This was great until we actually have to go through the process now, again I go back to final walk through and taken 4hrs out of my day to walk our house to help review the build and support identifying inefficiencies only to learn none were taken care of prior to closing. Now we have to go back and walk our entire house again with warranties and do it all over again? But now warranties team tells us about all these ‘rules’ that must be followed in order to identify if Shea is responsible of fixing or if it’s on us. ARE YOU SERIOUS? Top it off, as we point out the exact same inefficiencies with the warranties team, we are now questioned if we created the issue or if it was actually during construction. As if things couldn’t get worse, the title company that is Shea preferred, had a security breach the Friday before the week of our closing. Outside of our bank reaching out, the only person who stepped up to let us know what was happening was a Shea sales representative and continued to support us by keeping us informed. Meanwhile, we still had our final/ closing scheduled which we proceeded with. Outside of the other issues that transpired during the closing walk, I was also treated like I was the reason there was a security breach at sheas preferred title company and told by the field superintendent that he wasn’t obligated to give me my keys. Seriously? Again, I had nothing to do with the security breach and my wife and I were the only ones reaching out to all parties to get updates and asking for next steps and out our entire lives on hold to make sure we were available only to be treated like that. We spent a lot of money on this home, we’ve done a lot of research on builders and truly believed Shea was the right fit for us but we question if we made the right decision spending the extra money for the customer service that doesn’t seem to line up and was preached to us constantly. Again, the sales team was top notch and seemed to be the only Shea team members who actually had our backs as the home buyers.

title: When I purchased the property it was flat. I have a picture of the flat property. I move in to see there is a hill between my house and the house next door. I am responsible for paying for the retaining wall to prevent water and dirt drainage issues.
review_text: The feeling is like buying an unfinished home. A linen closet, but no shelves or doors, a kitchen sink is only included if you select from the chepest counter top, pay additional for light wiring in the ceiling but no light bulb is included, etc. The backyard is not your choice but the HOA approved choice. Like it or not.

title: Overall, the quality and workmanship is terrible
review_text: Way too many problems with the home. Poor workmanship overall.

title: Follow-up Not Their Strength
review_text: Had quite a few issues. In the cases of simple little problems where the builder can shove the repair off to their subcontractors (doesn’t cost the builder anything) they get right on it however, for the more difficult items the builder either doesn’t respond (probably hoping you’ll go away) or flat out refuses. I shudder to think of what will happen if any real warranty issues come up.

title: Good buying experience. Terrible construction experience.
review_text: Our sales experience was very good. Our sales person Ted was very helpful in helping us make decisions but wasn’t pushy. The Trilogy house designs are very well laid out. Construction, however, was a disaster. 22 months to build, constant oversight of quality needed due to lack of construction supervision. Poor quality of most trades, not corrected by construction supervisor. Very slow or nonexistent response to problems noted during construction. Now the warranty people are trying to fix a bunch of items left over due to the inattention of the construction supervisor. In summary, greatdesign and sales which were then ruined by an incompetent construction process.

title: building and HAVAC issues
review_text: not working lighting with found not to code electrical wiring, attic light left on, backwards install door locks, 2-inch crowned tile in living room, ongoing not fixed broken irrigation box with box lid not secured, Heating and Air drainage issues, 2 inch crowned bottom frame of kitchen island.

title: Plenty of issues on a brand new home.
review_text: Ensure to take your time during the walk-thru process prior closing to your house. We had a major option that was missing after closing, We could’ve not close if only I caught it during the walk-thru. Other than that we keep finding faulty items all over the house. The warranty reps been taking care some of them so far. To compare it to the other new construction home I bought 18 years ago, this has so many issues.

title: NA
review_text: There is no storage is this house in the laundry room or room to place linen and bedding. The bathroom needs more drawers under the bathroom counter.

title: Staffing
review_text: During the build process, Shea Homes changed my field manager multiple times for company reasons, not because the employee left the company. This practice delayed completion of the home. The final manager assigned had little to no project-management experience and did a poor job at quality assurance. I have a long list of items that need to be repaired or replaced. Thankfully, the home is under a one-year warranty.

title: Quality of workmanship not as expected
review_text: We have been very disappointed on the quality of workmanship on a home from Shea that has been so highly regarded. We have been in our home for almost 5 months and they are still trying to take care of our 1 month warranty work. When a homeowner points out poor quality of work, it should be taken care of, not argued about.

title: Not what I had hoped for
review_text: Sales team was great up until I dropped of the deposit check, and then communication was sparse. They neglected to tell me what was in the model homes but not in my unit, and no option for any changes or additions before drywall went up. They are slow to fix problems and some of the problems have returned. Other problems were 'within tolerance' and not fixed. The location is excellent, but feels overpriced for what you get (no yard here and very high HOA dues.) It's fine, but probably not the best choice for me - was hoping for more than just 'fine.'

title: Shea home
review_text: The experience up to the closing was great, but once you sign your name on the dotted line, good luck getting anything done. The warranty department is like pulling your own teeth, they have a lot of excuses for any problems you bring up, after you move in you’ll start finding issues with the poor workmanship.

title: HVAC venting and system i
review_text: HVAC seems to be poorly installed. Multiple issues in first couple months in the home. Walls cut open everywhere, mold from condensation.

title: Promising start, inconsistent finish to our home purchase process.
review_text: Sales team was very friendly and knowledgeable. They made the purchasing process fairly easy. Our issues started toward the end of the construction process: inconsistent quality control, pipes that were hanging 12 inches lower than they should have been in the basement, and a water heater that needed to be moved 3 different times. It was difficult to get concerns addressed throughout construction and during the final walkthroughs. We kept being told “well, it was built to our standards.” If that’s truly the case, Shea Homes needs to re-examine its standards. The goal was clearly to build ourhome as quickly as possible without much attention to detail. We were hoping for a much more positive experience after the great service we received from the sales team.

title: Meh
review_text: It started out great and then was just average overall. I would most likely not purchase through Shea if I were to do it all over again.

title: Home Buying Experience
review_text: My after action review will be forthcoming as there are many facets in grading this experience. Major issue is that it took way too long to complete construction from out Aug 2021 purchase date (we moved in May 2023) and that Shea did not accept that we were not available the last 2 weeks of Apr 2023 per my Jan 6, 2023 telcon with project mgr #2…always a moving completion date. Finally, when inspected after completion, it was not cleaned per Shea model…it was a rush job to get to close escrow date. Disappointing.

title: Build was ok follow up from Shea Homes not so good
review_text: Need to be more responsive and follow up doing/completing what is supposed to be done

title: I just doubt Id do this again
review_text: The experience we had was what I'd call, "just ok". In todays world and economy, striving for "Raving Fans" is critical (google the book RF). It was clear, it's about the money and the customer is simply granted lip service. I don't need to revisit things like construction delays, we all know about those. However, getting the home closed so the money can be put in the bank is way too aggressive and simply a poor way to do business. There were "promises" made and not kept. Thats why people use the term, I was lied to. Sorry, that's just fact. Your VP (Brian) did not like it at all when I usedthat term. I should have stuck to my guns in that conversation and emphasize my point better. I was lied to on one occasion. It was a big lie and it cost ME money as a result. As well, it's just bad business to complete something like a home which is a big investment for the home buyer, just to have to correct a large list of things after the fact. Clearly 2 different profit and loss centers. As for the good? We worked with a few wonderful people who we believe did their best. Ok, no last names but Deanna, Scott and Richard were wonderful. Hopefully Shea recognizes them as helpful in what were some significant, problematic situation. As for me, I'm going to be all in on this project to the tune of around $1.05M. Thats a lot of money to me. I wish I did feel better about it than I actually do.

title: Overall Good Experience
review_text: First time buying a new construction home. Home was delivered on time but there were several open issues that were rushed in order to deliver on time. From stucco repair issues, incorrect water softener, incomplete tile finishes, and even refrigerator issues. This house was close to $1M, I expected the service and quality to be better.

title: Quality work shows in the details. Unfortunately, windows are crooked, the kitchen cabinets were postmarked and distressed and nails are popping out of the wall. The heating unit is not large enough to handle both the upstairs and downstairs are not suff.
review_text: The home buyers are buying a lifestyle- not a quality built home. When we bring issues to the warranty manager the appropriate tradesperson is dispatched to check it quickly. Most issues are assessed and fixed PDQ. The heating and electrical concerns are still a concern. Having your own inspector or engineer evaluate your living space is appropriate. If the homes would built better ( level, plumb and Better than tolerance ) so much aggravation would be a non issue. Owning here has been discouraging. I do like the clubs and facilities.

title: New homes
review_text: Things really happened slowly, without much help.

title: Definitely not what I expected....
review_text: Major communication issues from beginning to end, hard to get timely resolution on issues, missing appliances is an ongoing issue. It will be a beautiful house...once all the issues are finally resolved. Plan on managing fixes after you move in like a second job - they always expect you to be home and available and the list of issues to address post move is VERY long.

title: Beautiful House
review_text: We love our house but getting anything fixed after the fact is impossible.

title: No response toward fix request
review_text: Barely have response. My warranty ticket has been opened for months and it is still open. No update nor schedule. When I email Shea home warranty, it just replied the ticket is still opened. I can not rate any star for it. The reason for the 1 star is because the system do not allow me to select 0 star.

title: Owner
review_text: Many large problems with construction of workmanship both large and small issues. Priority to resolve is treated as secondary once closed. Concerned these issues will ever get resolved

title: Pretty disappointed
review_text: I'm in the process of slowly moving into my new Trilogy home. There were lots of issues about delays in obtaining materials. The completion seemed rushed. That's pretty obvious from the small, niggling issues that require repair/fix contractors to come back on a weekly basis. Who knows when it will end? I'm not willing to say I would recommend Shea to anyone I liked.

title: Issues pending for long time
review_text: Rob promised me to replace the dented range hood on 11/21. It is still not done as of today. There are also other smaller issues never addressed since closing.

title: We're Disappointed on many levels
review_text: Seems like the construction was rushed to facilitate closing . Surprising lack of attention to detail with the finishing work ,door hanging etc . Preliminary punch list is significant after just one months occupancy . Construction crews working in the area make transit challenging and in some instances dangerous . We were told not to visit the area as it was a dangerous construction site ... yet closings were required and now residents are living in the same construction site . This demonstrates an apparent lack of concern about the level of enjoyment and safety of the residents postclosing . I've said it before and I'll ask again - send a rep and we can chat further .

title: OK
review_text: House was not ready when we moved in. Quality was not good as our previous Shea home. This situation is understandable because of the economic conditions during building. But we bought a life style rather than just a house. We love it

title: Thanks for nothing.
review_text: Shea did a great job of overpromising and under delivering. I’ve asked for 10 to 15 items to be fixed. A couple have been and haven’t heard from anybody in three weeks. Pretty pathetic.

title: Experience till now with Shea
review_text: Buying time and experience was good and no complain. But after movement there are no help / services on timely provided. Infact, we have an Emergency / life threatening situation but still trying to get it resolve. From Home service, no one is taking the raised complaint seriously and no/slow response.

title: There is a problem with the quality of the house and the repair service is slow
review_text: There are many minor problems with the quality of the house, and the after-sales maintenance service is very slow,

title: Poor wukirty
review_text: Workmanship is poor with finish work and some structural

title: PMP
review_text: Great neighborhood. Uncomfortable living in construction area. Flat tires due to construction nails. Lower quality products. Careless errors . Endless mud.

title: Document & Double Check EVERYTHING!
review_text: I encourage buyers to document conversations about color schemes and upgrades especially if the home is flipped from the model. Don’t trust that the electrician at the designer will remember that your home is flipped from the model. Also, get clarification about membership dues and the three HOA fees and assessments up front! Knowing what I know now, would I do it again? Probably not.

title: Sub-par Cabinets and Sloppy Warranty Service
review_text: They have good defaults like tiles and base carpets. But they really screwed up the cabinets. They installed with lots of scratches and damages.
