Most of the subcontractors are good, but some are not so good and Shea's supervision isn't diligent enough to maintain good quality with the poorer subs. The best advice is to keep close tabs during the building process because mistakes will be made. I strongly advise to get issues cleaned up before signing,because their responsiveness after closing is not very good.

title: My experience was not good
review_text: Shea home you really don’t want me to have anything reviewed about buy shea home experience right now, my new house just got done with one month warranty fixed up . right now I’m right literally is word for waiting for months April comes so I can continue doing more fixing things to do my house , foundation problem or house setting problems I don’t know things are cracked ? please don’t use the word congregation to me , if you understand how I feel , experience is not very good .

title: Poor customer service
review_text: Always telling me why they wouldn’t help me. They don’t listen. They just have standard responses. Very disappointed with Shea

title: Nothing good to say about Shea Homes. Still dealing with a fraudulent Solar lease we were forced into by Shea and multiple structural issues that are not being addressed.
review_text: Have nothing good to share. Lending department was nothing but harassment for months, several structural concerns that are being ignored, HOA that demands everything but returns nothing. Still stuck in legal mitigation over a solar lease we were forced into by Shea and told that it is our problem. Product and experience is not worth the money.

title: Needs good management
review_text: I waited to respond as it’s been a rocky start. I like my unit even with issues from start. I’m still waiting for them to fix Feel focus here is on the visitors and not those of us who live here. Needs increase in infrastructure stop signs at restaurant and I’m not sure what do you can’t have outsiders roaming around all night long after cafe etc locked and gated. It’s very noisy rarely quiet, too many events which at times make hard to leave or get in. Feel we as owners pay for others to have the experience with no thought how impacts those living here. Mgt has been mostly absent.They don’t respond to issues.

title: Poor Quality
review_text: 1 day after moving into our home we have had flooring issues. After nearly 3 months of dragging their feet Shea is finally repairing the issue and having to move us out of our home for 2 weeks. While they are apologizing they are offering $200 a night for room and board for a family of 5. Absolutely ridiculous. They say they are sorry but don't truly step up.This seems to be a common issue for Shea currently.

title: expect to oevrpay for what you get
review_text: low quality at premium pricing

title: Overall not satisfied with Shea homes
review_text: Shea homes builders are not friendly in nature. completely they are thinking as business gain, no customer favor. some are not standard but they are always telling it meet shea standard. Even customer paid for options, not reaching up to standards. No honesty at all. No proper response from shea homes builder and no choice to talk from customer perspective and no response from shea homes builder. Quality wise its not good as per what customer paid. Need to improve a lot in all sectors.

title: Regret buying a Shea Home
review_text: Our Shea home in the Canyons has not been good. First, the salesperson Abbe who sold us the house told us that if we acted quickly we'd get a $50k credit. She didn't explain that $30k of that would only apply if we used Shea Mortgages. Now she's gone to fleece some other people at a different homebuilder. The craftmanship of the house is less than ideal. Our lights keep flickering, appliances don't work correctly, and many of the materials and craftsmanship that they used were less than standard in what I would assume is an effort to maximize their profit. The service folks we've dealtwith have been good, even though it seems like their hands are tied by corporate in many of the issues that we've found. Overall disappointed.

title: Overall bad
review_text: The quality of work and attention to detail is seriously lacking. For a nearly 1,000,000 dollar home you’d expect a lot more in terms of finishing. The baseboards bow inward in some areas, the staircase where the dry wall meets the flooring is atrocious, and nearly all the downstairs light dimmers are making a buzzing noise. There are multiple areas where the drywall seams are noticeable and even lumpy. The tile work in the bathrooms is horrible in some areas and the master bath shower glass is scratched. Also there is unique touch at the top of the stairs beneath the newel post where the 3/4inch trim, baseboard, and newel post corners all point in different directions. The tile work in the master bath shower is also horrendous.

title: We closed the house with lot of unfinished work
review_text: Shea did not meet our expectations at all due to so many open issues. They close our house in April of this year and mostly cabinets are having issues. Supervisor from cabinet company was surprised that how come they gave you keys with so much unfinished work. Baseboards in the house are installed so badly. I am not sure how shea builder can deliver such bad quality work for an expensive house? After closing the house priority of unfinished is not treated properly. so much frustration.

title: Shea Home purchase
review_text: Overall we did not have a great experience with our first home purchase. Three different builders worked on our home. The first two were let go. I will give credit to Jorge for inherited a myriad of problems, did his absolute best to clean up the mess. You must get a third party inspector. It's a must. The builders gave us a very hard time over that but we still had him come through, despite costing us $750. Our inspector delivered a report well over twenty pages. We were wooed by the model house. Yet the quality of the build on ours was anything from it. Sloppy craftmanship throughout, facets and receptacles not level, light fixtures out of socket, and unsealed window sill molding letting spiders and various insects in. A leaky sprinkler systems, and an AC that doesn't cool the house. Needless to say the paint job was poor too. The garage remotes never worked. The workers didn't even bother removing the wraps from the garage rails, and there is spray pain everywhere. It's just sloppy hurried work. Be careful, verify, verify, and then verify some more. Do not fall for the razzle dazzle of the model or the sales people.

title: Bad experience
review_text: Keys were handed over to me before a thorough inspection of the home was done. During the final walkthrough, appliances were not run. Move in day, There was no water whatsoever in the washing machine, so I was unable to wash work clothes. The ice maker also had no ice/water hooked up. When I called to get help, they said I was out of luck because it was a weekend! I couldn’t get a single person to come out to address the problem, each one emphasizing that it was a Saturday. I got someone on the line to help me hook up the water lines by myself. It was NOT easy to say the least. I assumed thatthe keys being handed over, meant the home was ready to move in to. Not the case! Had someone taken 5 minutes out if their “weekend” to properly hook up appliances, I would not have had to take additional time off of work. So unprofessional on the part of Shea Homes! I expected way more from them.

title: Bad staff and their customer service
review_text: Sales lady was not helpful and was rude during closing, created so much confusion between the loan lender and us. The warranty guy sucks, doesn't even respond to emails, have items pending to be fixed from customer-walk through about 3 months ago. The Shea loan guy was just as bad, had to complain to his manager to get him to respond to my emails to get a Shea loan. Overall, almost backed out of the deal because of how horrible the staff and their dealing is. It was very stressful and frustrating at every step. The floorplan is great which is the only reason we bought it.

title: Did not get what we ordered
review_text: We have been a months after our close, never should have closed, to address many defects noted in the pre-close walk through. Ordered and paid for specific appliances (paid for options) that we did not get, they substituted inferior appliances and so far have refused to replace them. Trades show up and have no idea what they are repairing.

title: Very poor quality and workmanship
review_text: I am extremely disappointed with the quality and workmanship of my newly-built home. As a new homeowner who has invested over $500K into this property, I expected a level of quality that is simply not present. From the moment I moved into my newly built home 3 months ago, I have encountered numerous issues that should have been addressed during the construction process. The walls are not straight, the paint job is sloppy, wall texture is uneven, tiles are crooked, cabinet paint is peeling, windows seals are broken, roof was leaking, pipes were leaking behind the wall, and a number ofother issues. We’ve addressed these issues, and some have been corrected, but with it was met with lots of difficulties. Almost 3 days a week for over 2 months, we’ve had various contractors in our home to fix the issues in which they come over unprepared without the correct materials and equipment. They also come uninformed of what they are supposed to do and it’s difficult to communicate to them because there is a language barrier. Also many of them are sloppy and created more issues resulting in multiple visits to fix the issues they’ve caused. There are still a lot outstanding issues that needs to be addressed and the warranty team seems are not as responsive as they were when we initially had our 30-day follow up. This lack of communication is unacceptable, especially considering the amount of money that I have invested into this property. I am frustrated with the level of service that I have received thus far, and actually hate my house. I regret buying it and can’t wait to sell it.

title: Long wait times
review_text: Terrible lot. Since all I saw were dirt lots when I purchased, I didn't know that the air conditioning units would be placed right outside my bedroom window! Both my neighbors and mine are there. On the other side, my neighbor's unit is right in front of my living room. Houses are squeezed together. I'm still waiting for them to put the cabinet door on. It's been sitting here for 3 months. I had to have the electrician out 3 times to correct things. Bathroom was mis-wired and the Ring doorbell has been serviced twice. Doesn't seem like sturdy construction. I though Shea was a goodbuilder but I guess things have changed. Would not recommend a Shea home.

title: Not impressed.
review_text: We were delayed over and over. The house sat completely still at frame for 5 1/2 months waited for HVAC. It took ONE day to do the HVAC when they finally showed up. 21 month build time. Then closed the house with items not completed. Every neighbor we have talked to had items not completed when they closed. Not a quality home. This is our third new build and by far the worst one. We are very disappointed.

title: Not Impressed
review_text: I'll start by saying if I knew what I know now, I'd have gone with a different builder. The workmanship and quality of Shea Homes product (specifically) my home is complete garbage. For what the house cost, anyone in their right mine would expect a better product. From poor paint job, floor/tile completion, misaligned doors and the list goes on. Each day I'm in this house, I find more issues of poor craftsmanship and rush job. What makes it frustrating is the fact there were zero compromises from the builder in regards to making minor changes during the building process but here I am waitingweeks for them to fix items that should have been done from day one. The finance lady acted like she couldn't care less if we went with a different financial institution, which actually should have gave me a clue on what I was getting into. Bottom line is, other than the sales rep, the rest of the experience has been well below average. Tired of taking off work to get crap fixed. Which brings me back to my beginning sentence, if I knew what I know now, I would have sprinted away from Shea. But lesson learned! Just my experience.

title: Poor quality product
review_text: We purchased a new build Mosaic model at the beginning of the pandemic, sadly what we purchased has not come close to meeting our expectations. We rented in the community during the build process and we were able to catch many issues. Once we moved in, it’s been an on-going punch list of repairs due to poor craftsmanship. Not one cupboard or drawer works correctly, light fixtures are off center and now a broken primary condensation pipe has lead to opening up our living room wall to dry out the insulation and sheet rock. Our Shea customer service person has been great, but scheduled tradesdon’t show up to fix the repairs or if they do show up they’re not fully briefed or prepared to fix the problem. We closed the beginning of November and we are no where near getting everything fixed.

title: Workmanship is awful. Attempted to patch poor quality. Can’t get things fixed. Always saying this is not a custom house. Wow a million dollar tract home. Amazing
review_text: A million dollar tract home. Amazing 0

title: End product OK; experience not
review_text: I would not recommend Shea

title: Move in condition
review_text: During the final inspection we found several issues that were documented but do not have any action plan to address. We had discovered the manufacturer of the main power panel had reported issues with quality of breakers that could cause a fire. We were told that would be investigated but 90 days later no update. A garage door panel was dented after installation and prior to taking possession. We were told a replacement was back ordered but not surprisingly, they are still building houses with the same garage door being used. Why can't mine be replaced before a new house getsone? They must be able to get doors from somewhere.

title: Raj
review_text: Responsiveness is totally Zero Warranty guy does not want to fix problems After paying very very large amount for house , the finishing product ( the house ) is totally Disgrace , The warranty guy has either been fired Or not interested in fixing the warranty work It almost 2 months no body Responding from Shea Homes 831 920 8496

title: The quality of the house quite poor
review_text: I have paid premium price for my house, but did get poor quality house for it. - The sales team very stubborn do not give opportunity to tell the quality issues identified, they just say whether to cancel or buy it. How can we cancel it after putting such a huge amount. We have to accept what ever quality they built it. - The put the chipped roof beam, when I pointed that issue, construction officer says it is what it is. - On receiving day of the key found huge cracks on the side yard. They can’t address it with in few hours, as I got the keys, I have to wait for service request. - After20 days of living every nook and corners has huge crack/ displacement, during the 1 month appointment learned that they can fix these issues at 1 year appointment. - With in 15 days there was big wall enclosure displacement at the entry of the house. This shows the quality construction, I feel disappointed with this problem. Everyone in the neighborhood think it is the structural problem, where as your team think this model has known issues with wall enclosure displacement. - the garage handover with a dirty wall, I have notified before the keys handover. They didn’t care to fix it. Positive side: All minor problem fixed on 1month appointment findings on time. Overall the quality of the construction is very poor.

title: Extremely Disappointing Build Experience Marked by Poor Management and Retaliatory Conduct
review_text: Sales team was great; however, my experience building with this builder has been deeply frustrating and disappointing, particularly given the price point and expectations set at contract signing. From early in the process, I encountered significant project management failures, including repeated errors, missed details, and material deviations from the approved plans and selections. These were not subjective preferences—they were clear inconsistencies with documented drawings, specifications, and contractual agreements that I had to identify myself. When I raised legitimate concerns andrequested corrections, the response was troubling. Not only was I met with resistance and deflection, but I was also threatened with contract cancellation for simply holding the builder accountable. The Quality Assurance Manager’s interactions were combative and unprofessional, making an already difficult process unnecessarily adversarial rather than solution-oriented. Post-closing, the experience did not improve. HOA management and oversight following close have also been questionable—further extending the pattern of inadequate management beyond construction itself. Throughout the build, I was repeatedly forced to act as my own quality control to protect a major financial investment. Changes and deviations were often discovered only because I proactively reviewed progress—not because the builder identified or corrected them. For a new-construction home of this scale and cost, I expected: • Competent project oversight • Adherence to approved plans and selections • Professional, respectful quality assurance • Transparent communication • A collaborative approach to resolving issues Unfortunately, that was not my experience. To be clear, the final home itself is beautiful and has received positive feedback from independent inspectors from a structural standpoint. However, that outcome is largely due to my own extensive oversight, documentation, and persistence throughout the build. A favorable end result does not negate the months of unnecessary stress, mismanagement, and adversarial interactions that defined the experience—and it does not improve my overall assessment of the builder’s performance.

title: Cant build to minimum standards even for $1 Million dollars
review_text: Cant build a home to minimum standards no matter the price point apparently, constant lies and broken promises. Just point the finger on their sub par subcontractors (and dont take the blame for not paying to get quality people) The executives for these home builders deserve jail time honestly.

review_text: As a real estate agent myself, I have more experience dealing with builders than a normal person. This is by far the worst experience I had. During the set time of closing, I was very sick under Covid. They were not willing give me an extra day to complete the transaction and threaten me to add penalty and forfeited my deposit. After closing, there were absolutely no services at all. I could not reach anyone forquestions and services. Moreover, five months passed, none of the initial concerns was addressed and I still don’t have the key to the community pool and public area access. Worst ever experience!!! No one deserves this kind of treatment when making a big investment in life.

Want a nice countertop? Get a cheap cabinet. Since we hadalready signed the contract we opted for nothing and are gutting the house and getting what we want for half the cost. Cracked woods, alignment, Gaps in exterior fly sheets (not stapled and low quality), wavy walls inside, tiles alignment, cracks in concrete. 7 windows needed to be replaces after we pointed out the issue. Our blood boils thinking about what we have been made to go through this. We have spent so many hrs wit observation, documenting and follow up with project managerto get things fixed. title: Poor Build Quality, trying to maximize profit rather than live up to values as quality builder
review_text: I would recommend against buying a home with Shea. The sold a vision of build quality and then continually made excuses of how the subcontractors in the area were low quality but all they had available for the build in a large city. There were multiple unfinished aspects of the build but when we asked that the complete aspects of the build prior to close they threatened to void our contract and keep our deposit if we didn't keep the planned move in date. Every subcontractor that we've had to bring in to fix things has commented on how absurdly poor the workmanship was and how things were justleft unfinished (ex. cabinets weren't completely installed). They try to push all aspects of the unfinished build to after closing so they can be fixed "under warranty" without telling you that means they won't get to it for 6-12 months based on their warranty schedule. Overall, very poor experience with a builder that sells itself on "Shea Quality" only to cut corners at every turn. Use a cheaper builder because Shea uses the same subcontractors as other builders but charges a premium for the same build quality.

review_text: Once the house becomes yours, consider yourself lucky if you don't have a silverfish infestation living inside your walls, or a huge amount of ponding in your backyard, or cracks in your stucco or anything else that is significantly wrong, because Shea will not help you.

title: SHEA EXPERIENCE: BAIT & SWITCH
review_text: I put my deposit on a home site with a lot premium. I attended a zoom call of purchasers of homes in the area. The call had many hostile participants. My observations from the interaction of the host with the participants asking questions was a person who was at times disingenuous, evasive mixed in with a lot of happy talk and hyping the Shea family. Questions: cost of HOA dues, communication issues, quality issues, shelving issues, timing. After this experience my instinct was to walk away. I did not. The development is reported to be a gated development yet early before upgrades wereselected. I learned Shea was not going to pay for the rear fencing along our street since the street is a boundary of homes for the development. It seems there is a free for all when it comes to fencing. My instincts told me something didn’t seem right. I thought of walking away. I did not Next up was an upgrade meeting Interior Logic Group. No beef with this company, I enjoyed both contacts. I was incensed that the restrictions were severe relative to the options afforded my investment.as well as in my opinion quality vs cost of the upgrades and the convoluted option package provided. I am still incensed by it all. I debated upon returning home, of walking away. I did not. This was the dead of winter, so I took the opportunity to visit my snowcapped site foundation in place. I thought to myself the water features were questionable sunk low relative to my site elevation. I questioned the lot premium, and the road elevation which I was not aware of. These issues did not sync with the premium. It was, however, difficult to tell during the visit due to the weather. During my zoom call noted above, during the happy talk part the host stated we want the purchaser to come out and take a tour of their home during construction prior to drywall installation. My construction contact tried to dissuade me from taking the opportunity to visit. I came anyway and found a hole cut in the exterior wall and a fireplace framing in place. I did not purchase a fireplace. I asked for a guaranteed warranty relative to the mistake and hole cut in the wall and was ignored. After leaving I sent an email to my contact asking why did I paid a premium for a site the road elevation slope down toward my end of the road and water feature was sunk low relative to the elevation of my site? The answer back, my premium was for the view of the golf tee across the road to the neighboring development. My instincts said to walkway. I did not. I arrived at home the week before closing for a warranty walk through with a professional and charming person. The quality of the home was below what I had expected. I walked into a half bathroom and to the left of the toilet about four inches plus or minus from the wall next to the toilet sticking out in plain sight viewed for the doorway was the water connection. The proper place would be the opposite side of the toilet out of sight, near the wall. This issue characterized the inferior builder I found Shea to be. So many little things which were tacky or of inferior quality. So, second rate for a company in business for so long. My driveway has footprints, the concrete finish is very ruff, inferior, and there were large gaps from the fake façade to the concrete noted. My site is on such a slope foundation vent needed to have extension covers. The bath sinks are extremely small, causing water to be slopped everywhere when used. The bath fixture quality is inferior. There were no shelves in the bathroom vanity something asked about during the hostile zoom meeting. The host blamed it on Idaho contractors saying that is just how they did things, and it would be rectified going forward. The kitchen shelves were also cited during this discussion. This statement turned out to be disingenuous. During the warranty walkthrough I called out the lack of shelves in the bath vanities repeating the zoom discussion, but the warranty person tried to tell me it was the drawers which were the issue. There are kitchen shelves, no bath shelves in my home. I am still appalled at the tacky closet / laundry fixture/finishes. My upgrades became so expensive I had to forgo laundry cabinets of which I would be offered Formica type counter tops with no design options. I will never recoup the cost of the amount of upgrade cost charge to me relative to the quality. At the end of my selection, I had to back track and remove many options because of the increasing cost of the upgrades, downgrading the flooring and other issues. Still my final cost was off the charts. One of the first calls I made after my home closed was to obtain information relative to requirements for landscaping/fencing. I made the call to the HOA manager and was told my 120-day deadline was in the dead of winter January 20th, the expectation for me was to have my landscape review approved before the deadline and ready to install after winter thaw. I made my plans accordingly, meeting with a landscape architect and relaying the deadline. My first visit to my home was in early November due to untimely personal challenges and issues I faced at home. My site currently looks out to the water feature (and golf tee across the road to the neighboring development so they say), I have a house on each of my sides. The first thing I noticed was eight butterfly bushes planted in the rear of my parcel. These plants grow to approximately 5’ to 10’ feet tall and fan out. I thought to myself why of all shrubbery would this shrub be selected. As I drove the development, I saw no other butterfly bush shrubs. I contacted the head of the HOA, she was having none of my concern that I paid a premium for the location backing to water feature and yet it would be obstructed and could there not be more appropriate shrubbery to install similar to my neighbors, she was having none of it and simply said I wasn’t guaranteed a view and then abruptly sent me th

title: Will never buy a Shea Home again
review_text: My husband and I were excited to get the chance to finally purchase our first home, but since we decided to purchase from Shea the experience has gone downhill. First they force you to make choices about any upgrades within weeks with only one visit to a showroom in which the designer does not know the prices of any of the options. You then wait weeks more for pricing and have a few days to make the final choice, giving you no time to choose more options and properly price compare. I recommend not purchasing any of their upgrades and doing it yourself afterward. You'll have more choice,better prices and likely better quality installation by anyone else. The actual builders do a very poor job. We've heard from all our neighbors about issues with their homes. Not only cosmetic problems but showers needing to be demolished and redone, exhaust vents not being connected properly, windows leaking. Our internet router was installed in the ceiling and when they finally came to repair it they said the wires were never connected and since it would be too much hassle for them to fix it they moved it to another part of the ceiling and only covered the previous hole with a white rectangle plastic plate. Not one stretch of baseboard on one wall is actually painted cleanly and white...it looks like they installed the wood flooring after the baseboards and didn't do any touch up. There is paint and extra grout everywhere. It is evident they bang up and damage the wood stair case, door frames and cabinets and just paint over them. Walls are literally wavy and floors are so uneven that bookshelves and furniture that were fine in our old condo are now wobbling when placed on the floor. When they make a mistake they do not admit it, such as how they installed the wrong countertops in our kitchen. The construction manager and others rudely walked into our house when I was there with an inspector, did not introduce themselves and talked so loudly that I had to go outside to hear the inspector speak. They immediately said the countertop was polished (which is what we chose but not what they installed) instead of matte without even turning on the light or examining it properly as we had compared it to two samples of the two finishes to demonstrate how it was the wrong one. Thank god the designer came later and verified it was the matte finish. Every time they fix one minor thing like a closet door off the track, they would bang up the door and just slap on some paint to cover up the damage, only after we'd point out the new damage. There are countless issues that have caused me to have to stay at home during work hours for multiple appointments for people to come evaluate and fix the builder's mistakes. Everyone I have dealt with liken the actual cabinet company and other third parties that come for repairs has been much more of a pleasure to work with and obviously have some level of pride in their work and care for the products. Many have shook their heads and pointed out the carelessness by the builder when reviewing the damange. Another significant issue is our refrigerator has NEVER WORKED! In our walk through we noticed the freezer was leaking. Though the construction manager first admitted he saw the same water drops I was looking at he later kept saying via email that he did not see water in subsequent visits. I had to take pictures to prove it was reoccuring. They refused to fix it and just put in a service request with GE. This should have been fixed before we closed, but Shea took advantage that our interest rate would be expiring (because they delayed our closing over a month) and we could not refuse to close until after the refrigerator was repaired. We closed on January 18, 2023, but the refrigerator still does not work and Shea refuses to replace it. They keep making us fight with GE which has already come out for seven visits to fix multiple issues with the refrigerator. Not only does the freezer still leak despite the GE Tech "fixing" the seal, but the entire refrigerator freezes everything, you cannot use the water dispenser or put anything in the refrigerator section. We already replaced the motherboard and are currently waiting for two new refrigerator doors and other parts that are backordered because GE refuses to be accountable as does Shea. This is corporate greed and unequal bargaining power in action. Shea took advantage of us and refused to provide a working refrigerator by closing. Since they have been aware of the issue for OVER FOUR MONTHS, their only remedy has been to give us one mini refrigerator and make jokes about how they need to charge us for calling them about our refrigerator continously failing to work since we closed on the house despite the fact they knew it leaked before we closed. My husband and I both work very stressful and demanding jobs that require more than full time work. We have a toddler and are expecting another baby in late June. The stress and waste of our precious time that this has caused is ridiculous and should not be happening. Now I have to worry about how I will store breast milk for my baby and am unable to food prep or store enough fresh food for my family because Shea will not take responsibility. Despite the fact I do not have time, I now have to review our contract which we were only given 24 hours on a weekday to sign during the purchase process (and its 100s of pages long) to determine if they have breached the contract since they delivered a house with a known nonfunctioning appliance. We did not spend over 1.6 million for such a subpar quality home and I recommend no one else invest in a Shea home. When the timing is right and we can afford it, we will be moving. But unfortunately we are stuck here and trying to make the best of it. I will likely be on maternity leave handling a small claims court case as that may be the only thing that will get Shea's attention and have them replace our refrigerator.

title: Worst builder I have ever worked with
review_text: I have built four homes with three different builders. My first home was with a very low end builder and was a much better experience than this. Working with Shea Homes has been by far the worst building experience I have ever had when building a home. I have had a real estate investment company for over 15 years. I have renovated sixplexes , triplexes, townhouses, and condos. Therefore, this is not my first rodeo however, it has been the worst rodeo I have ever had. I am staying anonymous because I am concerned about potential retribution. I am still in the warranty stage and need a lot ofwork done.

title: Extremely disappointing New home Delivery
review_text: This is an extreme disappointment and grief I want to bring into your notice that we are facing each day since we have moved to our new house. There were lot of plumbing/ drainage issues each day at different corners of the house. The delivery of the house came with lot of problems that we did not expect at all with Shea homes. We trusted that home inspections are properly done by Shea at each point especially in plumbing and drainage matters. At this time, in midnight we have called After hour emergencies to get this drainage and plumbing issues resolved. We were at risk of our liveswhere there was water all over in the jack and Jill bathroom and one of the attached rooms that too underneath the tiles and carpet that we cannot have any control over. In the night, around 11 pm accidentally I went to use the bathroom where I noticed water leaking in jack and Jill bathroom underneath the toilet seat area and bathroom floor tile and it got spread all over underneath the carpet in one of the attached rooms. This was an extremely disastrous for us to have faced such an issue in the middle of the night. It took away my sleep and I woke up my husband to look into it. Our infant sleeps in these rooms, this was not at all okay for us to compromise with the quality and delivery we have got to what we have invested in. Earlier also, we had noticed that bathroom fixtures were not installed properly which we ignored as minor problems and fixed it ourselves but past few days getting each part of the house dismantled and getting it all fixed while residing with an infant is a huge stress for us. I do not know what else to say to show how unhappy we are. We did not expect this kind of work from Shea. We are awake at this time and getting it fixed is so annoying and disturbing for us. I mean do we have to live in fear that something else might come up in future again. Also, there is a structural issue which we have mentioned before closing. The main door is crooked and have gaps which allow the air to pass through. The warranty rep told me to bend your neck and you won’t see the any problem. There are many other issue we mentioned and nothing is getting fixed. Worst experience with Shea homes.

title: LC
review_text: We are extremely dissatisfied with our experience with Shea Homes new build. The construction is subpar. title: Sales personnel was amazing
review_text: House was not ready during our walk thru. People weee still working and cleaners were there too. Missing shower doors, bad workmanship, lots to be fixed

title: Would not recommend building with Shea
review_text: The whole build out process was terrible. The general contractors here are basically sales people and have a made up answer for everything you ask them. We’ve had numerous items that were on our addendum that we pointed out that weren’t there while we did our check ins during the build. Sent our sales rep and GC an email listing out all of those things. GC made up lies saying like our coach lights are wired, they bricked over it and they just need to cut it out. Come back a week later, electricians are at the house installing it and even told us that they were called back out because theyweren’t initially told to do that. In addition to other items, they ended up having to add it later and ended up doing a shotty job. Another example is that they missed that we were suppose to have a cut out in our master shower, added later, and spilled white grout in a section of our black stand shower floor, and the GCs excuse was that it’s going to change color because that’s where the water hits the ground.. It was clearly grout and he said professional cleaning will get it out.. lie. So be sure to check your addendum items that you paid for as an “upgrade” also our flooring has paint throughout that won’t come off and the wood flooring has noticeable gaps and said no flooring is perfect and they also have planks with wood putty filing and said it’s the texture… we have engineered wood floors, not real wood. When it’s clearly a damaged piece. GC even admitted that the floor was delayed because they were having issues leveling the floor and had to do it 3 times. They also had our dishwasher on top of the counters and ended up scratching it up, we could clearly see how they pushed the dishwasher off and left a clean straight line scratch that was black. It wasn’t coming off, he told us it was the pattern in the granite.. another lie, they ended up having to get someone out to buff it. Don’t get me started on how long it takes them to send someone out. Warranty hardly answers. I have to constantly follow up. We’ve had issues with electrical since we’ve moved in. They are all nice in person but when it comes to taking action.. silence. We’ve had issues with the doors and the weatherproofing causing bugs to come in, there’s a clear line of sight where light is coming in.. GC tried to say it’s a reflection from the glass on our door.. it’s not, when our warranty person came out she even said there’s clearly a hole. Our side fence is poorly cut our dogs can get out of it. GCs response was put chicken wire there because the slant of our lot they had to cut it like that.. also not true, even shea sales rep saw it and agreed that was bad. It got to the point where we were catching so many mistakes, the GC told us we can’t come check on our house anymore and that he would start locking the door if he had to. He wouldn’t respond to our emails, assuming he didn’t want it in writing, but when my agent would call him he would say he’s working on all fixing all the issues. The quality and overall experience we’ve had with shea has been terrible since we signed our contract. I would never build with them again or recommend anyone to build with them.

title: Will never deal with shea again
review_text: Do your job correctly! I cannot trust Shea at all!

title: Buyer Beware!!!
review_text: I don't even know where to begin. What was suppose to be my dream home has now turned into the most horrible home building experience, I've ever had. I'm a seasoned homeowner, this is not my first rodeo. I've built three other homes that I did not have this many issues with. I had never heard of Shea Homes, but they were the only ones left that were building in the area I wanted to build with the price point that I was comfortable with (mid/high). They pride themselves as a mid-level luxury builder, but they are far from that! The sales experience was great I thought until later when Ifound out that the items that were not done, originated at the sales office. The construction experience was ridiculous. At the construction meeting my build was handed over to another construction manager who came from a reputable builder, however the manager was not prepared as he did not even familiarize himself with the blueprints of the home! We spent most of the meeting figuring out where to place the in-ground outlet in the family room. We also discovered at that time the study was not recorded with doors, just an opening by the sales office. Spoke to the sales office, which offered to add framing for a door at no cost due to the error, but I needed to install the door at my expense later, which I was fine with. At the pre-drywall meeting, he didn't have the inspection report from my inspector to go over, so my time and my realtor's time was wasted. He said that most everything that was on the report was already on their list as well and would be fixed. We pointed out that the study did not look like it was framed for the door, he said that they were still working on it. Overall the construction manager was not communicative of the process and would get agitated when asked questions. As with many other builders the construction was delayed due to Covid and that was understandable. However, as my build was nearing completion I asked before putting my existing house up for sale if the estimated date of completion is still end of December and was assured yes. I was able to sell my house at full asking price in one day with multiple offers. We had it planned out perfectly with the close being end of December. Three weeks before the close, we were told by the builder that the house was not going to be completed until second week of January. We had to renegotiate our existing house sale to include a lease back for the additional time and lost 7K in the deal. Then the week of our close the construction manager asked if there was any way to push it another week, I said absolutely not! He said the aesthetics (paint, caulking, cleaning, etc.) were still not complete and the home would not be 100% move in ready, but would take care of everything in the weeks to come. I said that I was fine with that and closed on the home. At the walk through they were still working on the home, so I did not sign any documentation as it was only a ten minute walk through. Move day, they were still painting rooms as we moved in. They gave us a few weeks to settle in and that's when all the trouble began. Found out things such as gas dryer connection, carriage lights in front of home, etc., things that you would expect from a mid level luxury builder to have included in the home was not included but were an option. The options should have been brought up in the sales meeting and were not. As I we settled into the home we noticed that the seams in between the drywall in the whole family room were showing. After having a few independent contractors/engineers look at it, it was found that Shea contractors did not let the mud/filler in between the drywall boards dry before texturing and painting. At this point I did not want to have anything to do with the construction manager Jason. The matter was escalated to Josh, who quickly tried to diffuse the situation and was helpful in getting the drywall, dryer connection and driveway taken care of before he handed me off to the warranty department. Although Alexis in warranty has been helpful in trying to get things taken care of, she is now unavailable. Nobody is available. I have left messages for Alexis and Josh and have not heard back. It is now four months and my house still has the orange tape they marked on everything that needed fixing. I have family coming at the end of May and need all this fixed NOW! I have communicated this with Josh two weeks ago and have not heard back. Speaking with my neighbors, I'm learning they are having the same issues. We all have decided that if this lack of communication continues and issues are not taken care of soon, we will have no choice but to take action. So Shea, where is "The Shea Difference" that you are so proud of?? "The thoughtfulness that sets you apart"?? Your website says you offer "An experience as enjoyable as the place you'll call home", I think not! Those of you that are contemplating buying a home with Shea, think hard. Keep in mind all things you would normally see in a home may be considered options with Shea. They nickle and dime you on everything! I spent almost 70K just in options/upgrades and my home does not show it due to their craftsmanship. Four months later I'm sitting in a home with drywall issues, unsealed trims, unpainted trims, cracked countertop, cracked toilet, rusted bath tub, holes in concrete patio, cracks in concrete in garage, no gutters in back of home, no carriage lights, the list goes on. This is the real Shea Home experience!

review_text: From start to finish you're simply a # to Shea. They clearly do not care if you have a positive home-buying experience and will cut corners whenever possible. Their trades are subpar at best and their Project Managers have little control of the quality of their work. At closing our home was not finished and we were left with more excuses. Now two months post-closing and our over-priced $10,000 fireplace still doesn't work. The Rinnai tankless water heaterwith recirc pump does not deliver hot water and is riddled with glitches making our lives miserable. We have sagging floor boards, nails protruding from the pull-trowel drywall finish and constant electrical issues and these are just a few of the ongoing problems. In the end, I could never recommend Shea to any homebuyer. You'd think that due to their size they'd have safeguards in place to ensure they can deliver a home that is of modest quality but you quickly realize they are so big that they simply do not need to care if you like it or not.

At Closing my frontand back garden was not finished and part of the backyard was already dead (it is still dead after 3 months ...). After the first month's inspection I detailed all the haouse issues but after many promises, no one came or called me to fix them. I will not recommend Shea to family and friends.

title: BUYER BEWARE!!!
review_text: BUYER BEWARE is an understatement. The sales office claim of delivering a "zero-defect home", the result of a series of supposed meticulous inspections by teams of construction professionals is a total farce. I invited the senior Customer Service Rep and the Senior Construction Manager for ALL of Trilogy-Vistancia to my one-month follow-up. They were receptive and did attend. I presented a list of 36 issues (including cracked and chipped driveway, extensive cabinet damage, incredibly poor drywall and painting including globs of paint drips, globby/orange-peeled baseboards vs smoothfinish, 5 visibly scratched windows and more), and when we were complete the list had grown to 54 (to Shea's credit for identifying additional issues)...and is still growing. The Senior Construction Manager even stated "we never should have released this home". It's clear Shea is just moving too fast and sacrificing accuracy for speed. Shea agreed to remedy all but 2 issues, but any follow-up on my satisfaction is nearly non-existent and if not for my persistent follow-up on open items this list would never be exhausted. When I inquired about any concession for the extreme impact to my time (2+ months since move-in and still working-off the punch list), establishing my home including having to obtain a storage unit given the significant amount of tile, baseboard, and wall re-work, I was informed that Shea doesn't provide any concession for work accomplished after the buyer takes possession, thus BUYER BEWARE! 54+ issues the Shea "inspection teams" missed and, if I had not identified them and insisted on remedies I'm confident they would have walked-away from this debacle with zero-interest in "customer satisfaction" (my primary Customer Service Rep is more interested in rationalizing obvious defects vs. resolving them). All this re-work is built-in to the price of the home driving the price well beyond a fair asking price. The friends we have made in this community have, and will, overcome this prolonged drive to craftsmanship vs. "craps-manship", and I remain optimistic my wife and I will ultimately enjoy our move to Trilogy; however, the home-buying experience has truly eclipsed nightmarish proportions. BUYER BEWARE!

title: Attitude towards customer
review_text: If I was going to grade the Shea group from sales to construction to customer service, it would be a D- They have "It is what it is" attitude, quality control went out the door. For the price we pay for the house I would expect better than what we received.
